doc/src/sgml/release-18.sgml
1<!-- doc/src/sgml/release-18.sgml -->
2<!-- See header comment in release.sgml about typical markup -->
3
4 <sect1 id="release-18-4">
5  <title>Release 18.4</title>
6
7  <formalpara>
8  <title>Release date:</title>
9  <para>2026-05-14</para>
10  </formalpara>
11
12  <para>
13   This release contains a variety of fixes from 18.3.
14   For information about new features in major release 18, see
15   <xref linkend="release-18"/>.
16  </para>
17
18  <sect2 id="release-18-4-migration">
19   <title>Migration to Version 18.4</title>
20
21   <para>
22    A dump/restore is not required for those running 18.X.
23   </para>
24
25   <para>
26    However, if you are upgrading from a version earlier than 18.2,
27    see <xref linkend="release-18-2"/>.
28   </para>
29  </sect2>
30
31  <sect2 id="release-18-4-changes">
32   <title>Changes</title>
33
34   <itemizedlist>
35
36    <listitem>
37<!--
38Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
39Branch: master [b63f25bdd] 2026-05-11 05:13:46 -0700
40Branch: REL_18_STABLE [f7a191f53] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
41Branch: REL_17_STABLE [32a4ce55c] 2026-05-11 05:13:48 -0700
42Branch: REL_16_STABLE [66cf26b9e] 2026-05-11 05:13:49 -0700
43Branch: REL_15_STABLE [3fb66d302] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
44Branch: REL_14_STABLE [3b4e66739] 2026-05-11 05:13:51 -0700
45Branch: REL_17_STABLE [6dffaeb8e] 2026-05-11 05:13:48 -0700
46Branch: REL_16_STABLE [c2e6ef863] 2026-05-11 05:13:49 -0700
47Branch: REL_15_STABLE [16fda4df6] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
48Branch: REL_14_STABLE [14a4a7040] 2026-05-11 05:13:51 -0700
49-->
50     <para>
51      Prevent unbounded recursion while processing startup packets
52      (Michael Paquier)
53      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f7a191f53">&sect;</ulink>
54     </para>
55
56     <para>
57      A malicious client could crash the connected backend by alternating
58      rejected SSL and GSS encryption requests indefinitely.
59     </para>
60
61     <para>
62      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks Calif.io
63      (in collaboration with Claude and Anthropic Research) for reporting
64      this problem.
65      (CVE-2026-6479)
66     </para>
67    </listitem>
68
69    <listitem>

139     <para>
140      Fix assorted integer overflows in memory-allocation calculations
141      (Tom Lane, Nathan Bossart, Heikki Linnakangas)
142      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e1c30458a">&sect;</ulink>
143      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;01e568b8c">&sect;</ulink>
144      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f3cee4dc4">&sect;</ulink>
145      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;dd8af778d">&sect;</ulink>
146      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;55328e3a9">&sect;</ulink>
147      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;67dd6243d">&sect;</ulink>
148      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8d1489d50">&sect;</ulink>
149      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c7fb9f765">&sect;</ulink>
150      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3fbec9e50">&sect;</ulink>
151     </para>
152
153     <para>
154      Various places were incautious about the possibility of integer
155      overflow in calculations of how much memory to allocate.  Overflow
156      would lead to allocating a too-small buffer which the caller would
157      then write past the end of.  This would at least trigger server
158      crashes, and probably could be exploited for arbitrary code
159      execution.  In many but by no means all cases, the hazard exists
160      only in 32-bit builds.
161     </para>
162
163     <para>
164      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks Xint Code,
165      Bruce Dang, Sven Klemm, and Pavel Kohout for reporting these problems.
166      (CVE-2026-6473)
167     </para>
168    </listitem>
169
170    <listitem>
171<!--
172Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
173Branch: master [d389415ff] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
174Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c2e44c370] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
175Branch: REL_17_STABLE [d7de7fa84] 2026-05-11 05:13:49 -0700
176-->
177     <para>
178      Properly quote subscription names
179      in <application>pg_createsubscriber</application> (Nathan Bossart)
180      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c2e44c370">&sect;</ulink>
181     </para>
182
183     <para>
184      The given subscription name was inserted into SQL commands without
185      quoting, so that SQL injection could be achieved in the (perhaps
186      unlikely) case that the subscription name comes from an untrusted
187      source.
188     </para>
189
190     <para>
191      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
192      Yu Kunpeng for reporting this problem.
193      (CVE-2026-6476)
194     </para>
195    </listitem>
196
197    <listitem>
198<!--
199Author: Noah Misch <noah@leadboat.com>
200Branch: master [46b4f5c11] 2026-05-11 05:13:46 -0700
201Branch: REL_18_STABLE [cb35d7306] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
202Branch: REL_17_STABLE [f0f59b658] 2026-05-11 05:13:48 -0700
203Branch: REL_16_STABLE [248a433cd] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
204-->
205     <para>
206      Properly quote object names in logical replication origin checks
207      (Pavel Kohout)
208      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cb35d7306">&sect;</ulink>
209     </para>
210
211     <para>
212      <command>ALTER SUBSCRIPTION ... REFRESH PUBLICATION</command>
213      interpolated schema and relation names into SQL commands without
214      quoting them, allowing execution of arbitrary SQL on the publisher.
215     </para>
216
217     <para>
218      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
219      Pavel Kohout for reporting this problem.
220      (CVE-2026-6638)
221     </para>
222    </listitem>
223
224    <listitem>
225<!--
226Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
227Branch: master [d388e1d7f] 2026-05-11 05:13:46 -0700
228Branch: REL_18_STABLE [62ad26266] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
229Branch: REL_17_STABLE [3ed3dbbf4] 2026-05-11 05:13:48 -0700
230Branch: REL_16_STABLE [5919e0005] 2026-05-11 05:13:49 -0700
231Branch: REL_15_STABLE [7fe365693] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
232Branch: REL_14_STABLE [2d267ffc4] 2026-05-11 05:13:51 -0700
233-->
234     <para>
235      Reject over-length options in <function>ts_headline()</function>
236      (Michael Paquier)
237      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;62ad26266">&sect;</ulink>
238     </para>
239
240     <para>
241      The <literal>StartSel</literal>, <literal>StopSel</literal>
242      and <literal>FragmentDelimiter</literal> strings must not exceed
243      32Kb in length, but this was not checked for.  An over-length value
244      would typically crash the server.
245     </para>
246
247     <para>
248      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
249      Xint Code for reporting this problem.
250      (CVE-2026-6473)
251     </para>
252    </listitem>
253
254    <listitem>
255<!--
256Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
257Branch: master [6d6348f03] 2026-05-11 05:13:46 -0700
258Branch: REL_18_STABLE [661095c40] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
259-->
260     <para>
261      Detect faulty input when restoring attribute MCV statistics
262      (Michael Paquier)
263      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;661095c40">&sect;</ulink>
264     </para>
265
266     <para>
267      The statistics restore functions were insufficiently careful about
268      validating most-common-value statistics, and would accept values
269      that could crash the planner later on.
270     </para>
271
272     <para>
273      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
274      Jeroen Gui for reporting this problem.
275      (CVE-2026-6575)
276     </para>
277    </listitem>
278
279    <listitem>
280<!--
281Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
282Branch: master [76ab76f87] 2026-05-11 05:13:46 -0700
283Branch: REL_18_STABLE [ba27389c2] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
284Branch: REL_17_STABLE [4197c880c] 2026-05-11 05:13:49 -0700
285Branch: REL_16_STABLE [24e0e3254] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
286Branch: REL_15_STABLE [126a236ba] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
287Branch: REL_14_STABLE [a50ae8306] 2026-05-11 05:13:51 -0700
288Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
289Branch: master [ec8ded4b3] 2026-05-11 05:13:46 -0700
290Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c6e7a9ef3] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
291Branch: REL_17_STABLE [a386d14fe] 2026-05-11 05:13:49 -0700
292Branch: REL_16_STABLE [79b7847c7] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
293Branch: REL_15_STABLE [c3fff3950] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
294Branch: REL_14_STABLE [2c8226f52] 2026-05-11 05:13:51 -0700
295-->
296     <para>
297      Guard against malicious time zone names
298      in <function>timeofday()</function>
299      and <function>pg_strftime()</function> (Tom Lane)
300      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ba27389c2">&sect;</ulink>
301      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c6e7a9ef3">&sect;</ulink>
302     </para>
303
304     <para>
305      A crafted time zone setting could pass <literal>%</literal>
306      sequences to <function>snprintf()</function>, potentially causing
307      crashes or disclosure of server memory.  Another path to similar
308      results was to overflow the limited-size output buffer used
309      by <function>pg_strftime()</function>.
310     </para>
311
312     <para>
313      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
314      Xint Code for reporting this problem.
315      (CVE-2026-6474)
316     </para>
317    </listitem>
318
319    <listitem>
320<!--
321Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
322Branch: master [4793fc41f] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
323Branch: REL_18_STABLE [a44780f41] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
324Branch: REL_17_STABLE [c27ba08cd] 2026-05-11 05:13:49 -0700
325Branch: REL_16_STABLE [d92852d62] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
326Branch: REL_15_STABLE [08c397b02] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
327Branch: REL_14_STABLE [8bca85e9f] 2026-05-11 05:13:51 -0700
328-->
329     <para>
330      When creating a multirange type, ensure the user
331      has <literal>CREATE</literal> privilege on the schema specified for
332      the multirange type (Jelte Fennema-Nio)
333      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a44780f41">&sect;</ulink>
334     </para>
335
336     <para>
337      The multirange type can be put into a different schema than its
338      parent range type, but we neglected to apply the required privilege
339      check when doing so.
340     </para>
341
342     <para>
343      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
344      Jelte Fennema-Nio for reporting this problem.
345      (CVE-2026-6472)
346     </para>
347    </listitem>
348
349    <listitem>
350<!--
351Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
352Branch: master [5924e256c] 2026-05-11 05:13:46 -0700
353Branch: REL_18_STABLE [d93ef4131] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
354Branch: REL_17_STABLE [c4e7435b3] 2026-05-11 05:13:48 -0700
355Branch: REL_16_STABLE [00e27235e] 2026-05-11 05:13:49 -0700
356Branch: REL_15_STABLE [c95275f18] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
357Branch: REL_14_STABLE [4608619a1] 2026-05-11 05:13:51 -0700
358Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
359Branch: REL_17_STABLE [8e34acfda] 2026-05-11 05:13:48 -0700
360Branch: REL_16_STABLE [1604939b2] 2026-05-11 05:13:49 -0700
361Branch: REL_15_STABLE [9dcfcb92f] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
362Branch: REL_14_STABLE [b282280e9] 2026-05-11 05:13:51 -0700
363-->
364     <para>
365      Use timing-safe string comparisons in authentication code
366      (Michael Paquier)
367      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d93ef4131">&sect;</ulink>
368     </para>
369
370     <para>
371      Use <function>timingsafe_bcmp()</function> instead
372      of <function>memcpy()</function> or <function>strcmp()</function>
373      when checking passwords, hashes, etc.  It is not known whether the
374      data dependency of those functions is usefully exploitable in any of
375      these places, but in the interests of safety, replace them.
376     </para>
377
378     <para>
379      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
380      Joe Conway for reporting this problem.
381      (CVE-2026-6478)
382     </para>
383    </listitem>
384
385    <listitem>
386<!--
387Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
388Branch: master [bd4811493] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
389Branch: REL_18_STABLE [be0136440] 2026-05-11 05:13:48 -0700
390Branch: REL_17_STABLE [d88c7be15] 2026-05-11 05:13:49 -0700
391Branch: REL_16_STABLE [614474996] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
392Branch: REL_15_STABLE [e3a1f83ea] 2026-05-11 05:13:51 -0700
393Branch: REL_14_STABLE [8ac723b2b] 2026-05-11 05:13:51 -0700
394-->
395     <para>
396      Mark <function>PQfn()</function> as unsafe, and avoid using it
397      within <application>libpq</application> (Nathan Bossart)
398      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;be0136440">&sect;</ulink>
399     </para>
400
401     <para>
402      For a non-integral result type, <function>PQfn()</function> is not
403      passed the size of the output buffer, so it cannot check that the
404      data returned by the server will fit.  A malicious server could
405      therefore overwrite client memory.  This is unfixable without an
406      API change, so mark the function as deprecated.  Internally
407      to <application>libpq</application>, use a variant version that can
408      apply the missing check.
409     </para>
410
411     <para>
412      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
413      Yu Kunpeng and Martin Heistermann for reporting this problem.
414      (CVE-2026-6477)
415     </para>
416    </listitem>
417
418    <listitem>
419<!--
420Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
421Branch: master [a1063eece] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
422Branch: REL_18_STABLE [6a67c540a] 2026-05-11 05:13:48 -0700
423Branch: REL_17_STABLE [8f881e188] 2026-05-11 05:13:49 -0700
424Branch: REL_16_STABLE [6778af13e] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
425Branch: REL_15_STABLE [0c83fe8e4] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
426Branch: REL_14_STABLE [498829dca] 2026-05-11 05:13:51 -0700
427-->
428     <para>
429      Prevent path traversal in <application>pg_basebackup</application>
430      and <application>pg_rewind</application> (Michael Paquier)
431      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6a67c540a">&sect;</ulink>
432     </para>
433
434     <para>
435      These applications failed to validate output file paths read from
436      their input, so that a malicious source could overwrite any file
437      writable by these applications.  Constrain where data can be written
438      by rejecting paths that are absolute or contain parent-directory
439      references.
440     </para>
441
442     <para>
443      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks XlabAI Team
444      of Tencent Xuanwu Lab and Valery Gubanov for reporting this problem.
445      (CVE-2026-6475)
446     </para>
447    </listitem>
448
449    <listitem>
450<!--
451Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
452Branch: master [43451a7a2] 2026-05-11 05:13:46 -0700
453Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c5790ec4f] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
454Branch: REL_17_STABLE [c4d04cc48] 2026-05-11 05:13:48 -0700
455Branch: REL_16_STABLE [5c1069c35] 2026-05-11 05:13:49 -0700
456Branch: REL_15_STABLE [84a9f2641] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
457Branch: REL_14_STABLE [074702525] 2026-05-11 05:13:51 -0700
458Branch: master [906ea101d] 2026-05-11 12:12:03 -0400
459Branch: REL_18_STABLE [05e73b5c3] 2026-05-11 12:12:03 -0400
460Branch: REL_17_STABLE [2b429d887] 2026-05-11 12:12:03 -0400
461Branch: REL_16_STABLE [6f0bff33d] 2026-05-11 12:12:03 -0400
462Branch: REL_15_STABLE [fc1fd3d97] 2026-05-11 12:12:03 -0400
463Branch: REL_14_STABLE [479823a71] 2026-05-11 12:12:03 -0400
464-->
465     <para>
466      Guard against field overflow
467      within <filename>contrib/intarray</filename>'s <type>query_int</type>
468      type and <filename>contrib/ltree</filename>'s <type>ltxtquery</type>
469      type (Tom Lane)
470      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c5790ec4f">&sect;</ulink>
471      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;05e73b5c3">&sect;</ulink>
472     </para>
473
474     <para>
475      Parsing of these query structures did not check for overflow of
476      16-bit fields, so that construction of an invalid query tree was
477      possible.  This can crash the server when executing the query.
478     </para>
479
480     <para>
481      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
482      Xint Code for reporting this problem.
483      (CVE-2026-6473)
484     </para>
485    </listitem>
486
487    <listitem>
488<!--
489Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
490Branch: master [2f1b16e86] 2026-05-11 05:13:46 -0700
491Branch: REL_18_STABLE [7f019f341] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
492Branch: REL_17_STABLE [8c3426110] 2026-05-11 05:13:48 -0700
493Branch: REL_16_STABLE [6b6b26fde] 2026-05-11 05:13:49 -0700
494Branch: REL_15_STABLE [9c2fa5b6a] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
495Branch: REL_14_STABLE [b545c3787] 2026-05-11 05:13:51 -0700
496-->
497     <para>
498      Guard against overly long values
499      of <filename>contrib/ltree</filename>'s <type>lquery</type> type
500      (Michael Paquier)
501      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7f019f341">&sect;</ulink>
502     </para>
503
504     <para>
505      Values with more than 64K items caused internal overflows,
506      potentially resulting in stack smashes or wrong answers.
507     </para>
508
509     <para>
510      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
511      Vergissmeinnicht, A1ex, and Jihe Wang
512      for reporting this problem.
513      (CVE-2026-6473)
514     </para>
515    </listitem>
516
517    <listitem>
518<!--
519Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
520Branch: master [260e97733] 2026-05-11 05:13:47 -0700
521Branch: REL_18_STABLE [1ebda7da9] 2026-05-11 05:13:48 -0700
522Branch: REL_17_STABLE [2dc64ef28] 2026-05-11 05:13:49 -0700
523Branch: REL_16_STABLE [710995782] 2026-05-11 05:13:50 -0700
524Branch: REL_15_STABLE [8053235ab] 2026-05-11 05:13:51 -0700
525Branch: REL_14_STABLE [2b026df29] 2026-05-11 05:13:52 -0700
526-->
527     <para>
528      Prevent SQL injection and buffer overruns
529      in <filename>contrib/spi</filename> (Nathan Bossart)
530      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1ebda7da9">&sect;</ulink>
531     </para>
532
533     <para>
534      <function>check_foreign_key()</function> was insufficiently careful
535      about quoting key values, and also used fixed-length buffers for
536      constructing queries.  While this module is only meant as example
537      code, it still shouldn't contain such dangerous errors.
538     </para>
539
540     <para>
541      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
542      Nikolay Samokhvalov for reporting this problem.
543      (CVE-2026-6637)
544     </para>
545    </listitem>
546
547    <listitem>
548<!--
549Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
550Branch: master [f76686ce7] 2026-05-01 11:13:50 +0900
551Branch: REL_18_STABLE [e8fd5e579] 2026-05-01 11:16:36 +0900
552Branch: master [ba82de48e] 2026-05-08 10:57:50 +0900
553Branch: REL_18_STABLE [1132af22c] 2026-05-08 10:59:13 +0900
554Branch: master [a1b754558] 2026-05-08 12:45:51 +0900
555Branch: REL_18_STABLE [5c214b58b] 2026-05-08 12:47:26 +0900
556Branch: master [5a55ea507] 2026-05-05 10:22:53 +0900
557Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b62f514ac] 2026-05-05 10:26:17 +0900
558Branch: REL_17_STABLE [d0e73bb18] 2026-05-05 10:28:24 +0900
559Branch: REL_16_STABLE [748fe9e60] 2026-05-05 10:30:37 +0900
560Branch: REL_15_STABLE [872c9fae7] 2026-05-05 10:32:25 +0900
561Branch: REL_14_STABLE [8395446df] 2026-05-05 10:33:47 +0900
562Branch: master [574581b50] 2026-05-05 10:23:31 +0900
563Branch: REL_18_STABLE [bed3ffbf9] 2026-05-05 10:27:06 +0900
564Branch: REL_17_STABLE [13226050e] 2026-05-05 10:29:01 +0900
565Branch: REL_16_STABLE [5a24cef08] 2026-05-05 10:31:17 +0900
566Branch: REL_15_STABLE [bab4f7fa5] 2026-05-05 10:32:42 +0900
567Branch: REL_14_STABLE [172034f6e] 2026-05-05 10:34:07 +0900
568-->
569     <para>
570      Check for nondeterministic collations before assuming that an
571      equality condition on a collatable type implies uniqueness
572      (Richard Guo)
573      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e8fd5e579">&sect;</ulink>
574      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1132af22c">&sect;</ulink>
575      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;5c214b58b">&sect;</ulink>
576      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b62f514ac">&sect;</ulink>
577      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bed3ffbf9">&sect;</ulink>
578     </para>
579
580     <para>
581      Numerous planner optimizations assume that, for example, at most one
582      table row can satisfy <literal>WHERE x = 'abc'</literal> if there is
583      a unique index on <literal>x</literal>.  However this conclusion is
584      unsafe in general if the index and the <literal>WHERE</literal>
585      clause have different collations attached.  It is safe when both
586      collations are deterministic, because that property essentially
587      requires that equality of two strings means bitwise equality.  But
588      nondeterministic collations don't act that way, so that optimizing
589      on the assumption of unique matches can give wrong query answers if
590      either the <literal>WHERE</literal> clause or the index has a
591      nondeterministic collation.
592     </para>
593    </listitem>
594
595    <listitem>
596<!--
597Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
598Branch: master [cfcd57111] 2026-04-20 14:48:23 -0400
599Branch: REL_18_STABLE [16fb94605] 2026-04-20 14:48:23 -0400
600Branch: REL_17_STABLE [766d40286] 2026-04-20 14:48:23 -0400
601Branch: REL_16_STABLE [d509be4ac] 2026-04-20 14:48:23 -0400
602Branch: master [f0ac6d494] 2026-04-20 19:24:52 -0400
603Branch: REL_17_STABLE [53cb4ec1d] 2026-04-20 19:24:46 -0400
604Branch: REL_16_STABLE [798dabe83] 2026-04-20 19:24:46 -0400
605-->
606     <para>
607      Fix incomplete removal of relation references
608      in <literal>RestrictInfo</literal> structs during join removal
609      (Tom Lane)
610      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;16fb94605">&sect;</ulink>
611     </para>
612
613     <para>
614      This oversight has been shown to result in planner failures such as
615      unexpected <quote>FULL JOIN is only supported with merge-joinable or
616      hash-joinable join conditions</quote> errors.  It may also have
617      caused failure to consider valid plans in other cases.
618     </para>
619    </listitem>
620
621    <listitem>
622<!--
623Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
624Branch: master [c1408956e] 2026-04-09 16:41:31 +0900
625Branch: REL_18_STABLE [8e8b2bef7] 2026-04-09 16:43:28 +0900
626-->
627     <para>
628      Improve planner's matching of partition key columns to sub-query
629      outputs (Richard Guo)
630      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8e8b2bef7">&sect;</ulink>
631     </para>
632
633     <para>
634      Strip no-op PlaceHolderVars from operands before comparing them to
635      partition keys.  This change enables partition pruning to succeed in
636      some cases where it previously failed to recognize that a partition
637      need not be scanned.
638     </para>
639    </listitem>
640
641    <listitem>
642<!--
643Author: Alexander Korotkov <akorotkov@postgresql.org>
644Branch: master [07b7a964d] 2026-03-20 15:46:30 +0200
645Branch: REL_18_STABLE [e8b9d6497] 2026-03-20 15:47:11 +0200
646-->
647     <para>
648      Fix self-join removal to handle join clauses that are bare boolean
649      columns, e.g. <literal>ON t1.boolcol</literal> (Andrei Lepikhov,
650      Tender Wang, Alexander Korotkov)
651      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e8b9d6497">&sect;</ulink>
652     </para>
653
654     <para>
655      Previously such a case led to a <quote>no relation entry
656      for relid <replaceable>N</replaceable></quote> error.
657     </para>
658    </listitem>
659
660    <listitem>
661<!--
662Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
663Branch: master [5548a969b] 2026-04-22 11:50:17 +0100
664Branch: REL_18_STABLE [f3d03fbd5] 2026-04-22 11:50:18 +0100
665-->
666     <para>
667      Fix <literal>UPDATE/DELETE ... WHERE CURRENT OF</literal> to work on
668      tables with virtual generated columns (Satyanarayana Narlapuram,
669      Dean Rasheed)
670      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f3d03fbd5">&sect;</ulink>
671     </para>
672    </listitem>
673
674    <listitem>
675<!--
676Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
677Branch: master [783425175] 2026-04-22 09:03:44 +0100
678Branch: REL_18_STABLE [cf38dedf6] 2026-04-22 09:03:44 +0100
679-->
680     <para>
681      Fix expansion of virtual generated columns
682      in <literal>EXCLUDED</literal> column references in <literal>INSERT
683      ... ON CONFLICT</literal> (Satyanarayana Narlapuram, Dean Rasheed)
684      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cf38dedf6">&sect;</ulink>
685     </para>
686    </listitem>
687
688    <listitem>
689<!--
690Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
691Branch: master [c6a79be3f] 2026-04-21 14:28:26 +0900
692Branch: REL_18_STABLE [e528bfe97] 2026-04-21 14:29:33 +0900
693Branch: REL_17_STABLE [9d6208939] 2026-04-21 14:31:15 +0900
694Branch: REL_16_STABLE [07b257189] 2026-04-21 14:33:07 +0900
695Branch: REL_15_STABLE [7062bd577] 2026-04-21 14:33:57 +0900
696Branch: REL_14_STABLE [8e39951be] 2026-04-21 14:35:43 +0900
697-->
698     <para>
699      Fix incorrect handling of <literal>NEW</literal> generated columns
700      in rule actions and rule qualifications (Richard Guo, Dean Rasheed)
701      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e528bfe97">&sect;</ulink>
702     </para>
703
704     <para>
705      Previously, such column references would produce NULL
706      in <literal>INSERT</literal> cases, or be equivalent to
707      the <literal>OLD</literal> value in <literal>UPDATE</literal> cases.
708     </para>
709    </listitem>
710
711    <listitem>
712<!--
713Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
714Branch: master [570e2fcc0] 2026-03-24 06:28:33 -0400
715Branch: REL_18_STABLE [cceb9c18a] 2026-03-24 06:28:32 -0400
716-->
717     <para>
718      Fix spurious <quote>indexes on virtual generated columns are not
719      supported</quote> errors (Robert Haas)
720      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cceb9c18a">&sect;</ulink>
721     </para>
722
723     <para>
724      Creation of an expression index could sometimes incorrectly report
725      this error.
726     </para>
727    </listitem>
728
729    <listitem>
730<!--
731Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
732Branch: REL_18_STABLE [11c2c0cc8] 2026-04-06 14:22:17 -0400
733Branch: REL_17_STABLE [681a91d29] 2026-04-06 14:22:17 -0400
734Branch: REL_16_STABLE [3c7a6bbe6] 2026-04-06 14:22:17 -0400
735Branch: REL_15_STABLE [07e833e3c] 2026-04-06 14:22:17 -0400
736Branch: REL_14_STABLE [40fa04e7c] 2026-04-06 14:22:17 -0400
737-->
738     <para>
739      Fix spurious <quote>generated columns are not supported in COPY FROM
740      WHERE conditions</quote> errors (Tom Lane)
741      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;11c2c0cc8">&sect;</ulink>
742     </para>
743
744     <para>
745      Use of a system column in a <literal>COPY FROM WHERE</literal>
746      condition could sometimes incorrectly report this error.
747     </para>
748    </listitem>
749
750    <listitem>
751<!--
752Author: Alexander Korotkov <akorotkov@postgresql.org>
753Branch: master [177037341] 2026-03-05 19:49:28 +0200
754Branch: REL_18_STABLE [13fab378e] 2026-03-05 19:50:06 +0200
755Branch: REL_17_STABLE [2dcac93c0] 2026-03-05 19:56:42 +0200
756Branch: REL_16_STABLE [f6e63d4b8] 2026-03-05 20:02:08 +0200
757Branch: REL_15_STABLE [8bfaae6fb] 2026-03-05 19:57:32 +0200
758-->
759     <para>
760      Correctly report a serialization failure
761      when <command>MERGE</command> encounters a concurrently-updated
762      tuple in repeatable-read or serializable mode (Tender Wang)
763      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;13fab378e">&sect;</ulink>
764     </para>
765
766     <para>
767      Previously, such cases behaved the same as in lower isolation
768      levels.
769     </para>
770    </listitem>
771
772    <listitem>
773<!--
774Author: Andrew Dunstan <andrew@dunslane.net>
775Branch: master [6cf49e804] 2026-04-30 11:04:57 -0400
776Branch: REL_18_STABLE [149c875fc] 2026-04-30 11:05:29 -0400
777Branch: REL_17_STABLE [a0104b447] 2026-04-30 11:14:37 -0400
778Branch: REL_16_STABLE [7bb519635] 2026-04-30 11:14:26 -0400
779Branch: REL_15_STABLE [76d15a7ee] 2026-04-30 11:14:12 -0400
780Branch: REL_14_STABLE [81b56b47c] 2026-04-30 11:13:50 -0400
781-->
782     <para>
783      Fix <literal>CREATE TABLE ... LIKE ... INCLUDING
784      STATISTICS</literal> for cases where the source table has dropped
785      column(s) (Julien Tachoires)
786      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;149c875fc">&sect;</ulink>
787     </para>
788
789     <para>
790      In such cases, extended statistics objects could be copied
791      incorrectly, or the command could give an incorrect error.
792     </para>
793    </listitem>
794
795    <listitem>
796<!--
797Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
798Branch: master [9d3e094f1] 2026-04-22 10:32:10 +0900
799Branch: REL_18_STABLE [5713ac248] 2026-04-22 10:34:33 +0900
800Branch: REL_17_STABLE [becf6d269] 2026-04-22 10:34:35 +0900
801Branch: REL_16_STABLE [313355d68] 2026-04-22 10:34:37 +0900
802Branch: REL_15_STABLE [0859000d0] 2026-04-22 10:34:38 +0900
803Branch: REL_14_STABLE [d809b16d1] 2026-04-22 10:34:40 +0900
804-->
805     <para>
806      Allow <literal>ALTER INDEX ... ATTACH PARTITION</literal> to mark
807      the parent index valid if appropriate (Sami Imseih)
808      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;5713ac248">&sect;</ulink>
809     </para>
810
811     <para>
812      There are edge cases in which a partitioned index might remain
813      marked as invalid even when all its leaf indexes are valid.  This
814      change provides a mechanism whereby a user can correct such a
815      situation without resorting to manual catalog updates.
816     </para>
817    </listitem>
818
819    <listitem>
820<!--
821Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@kurilemu.de>
822Branch: master [e7975f1c0] 2026-03-20 14:38:50 +0100
823Branch: REL_18_STABLE [6958077ce] 2026-03-20 14:38:50 +0100
824-->
825     <para>
826      Fix <literal>ALTER TABLE ... SET NOT NULL</literal> to invoke
827      object-access hook functions only after completing the catalog
828      change (Artur Zakirov)
829      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6958077ce">&sect;</ulink>
830     </para>
831    </listitem>
832
833    <listitem>
834<!--
835Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
836Branch: master [703fee3b2] 2026-03-19 15:07:43 -0700
837Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c11f87b1a] 2026-03-19 14:59:30 -0700
838Branch: REL_17_STABLE [876fa84a2] 2026-03-19 15:02:18 -0700
839Branch: REL_16_STABLE [a19edb66a] 2026-03-19 15:00:58 -0700
840Branch: REL_15_STABLE [3a35ab1d0] 2026-03-19 15:01:22 -0700
841Branch: REL_14_STABLE [c6f369e58] 2026-03-19 15:01:45 -0700
842-->
843     <para>
844      Fix <command>ALTER FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER</command> to not drop the
845      wrapper object's dependency on its handler function (Jeff Davis)
846      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c11f87b1a">&sect;</ulink>
847     </para>
848    </listitem>
849
850    <listitem>
851<!--
852Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
853Branch: master [2497dac55] 2026-03-30 14:37:33 +0900
854Branch: REL_18_STABLE [5db5e3396] 2026-03-30 14:38:58 +0900
855-->
856     <para>
857      Fix loss of deferrability of foreign-key triggers (Yasuo Honda)
858      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;5db5e3396">&sect;</ulink>
859     </para>
860
861     <para>
862      Previously, a foreign key defined as <literal>DEFERRABLE INITIALLY
863      DEFERRED</literal> would behave as <literal>NOT DEFERRABLE</literal>
864      after being set to <literal>NOT ENFORCED</literal> status and then
865      back to <literal>ENFORCED</literal>.
866     </para>
867
868     <para>
869      If you have a foreign key with this problem, it can be repaired
870      (after installing this update) by again setting it to <literal>NOT
871      ENFORCED</literal> and then back to <literal>ENFORCED</literal>.
872     </para>
873    </listitem>
874
875    <listitem>
876<!--
877Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
878Branch: master [4edd6036d] 2026-04-07 14:45:37 -0400
879Branch: REL_18_STABLE [49f3cb453] 2026-04-07 14:45:33 -0400
880-->
881     <para>
882      Fix <literal>WITHOUT OVERLAPS</literal> to allow domains (Jian He)
883      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;49f3cb453">&sect;</ulink>
884     </para>
885
886     <para>
887      <literal>UNIQUE/PRIMARY KEY ... WITHOUT OVERLAPS</literal> requires
888      the no-overlap column to be a range or multirange, but it should
889      allow a domain over such a type too.
890     </para>
891    </listitem>
892
893    <listitem>
894<!--
895Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
896Branch: master [dd4069197] 2026-04-23 21:28:11 +0300
897Branch: REL_18_STABLE [ff8f27d6e] 2026-04-23 21:28:59 +0300
898Branch: REL_17_STABLE [54343f6f9] 2026-04-23 21:31:32 +0300
899Branch: REL_16_STABLE [06e304524] 2026-04-23 21:33:02 +0300
900Branch: REL_15_STABLE [34ebeb15c] 2026-04-23 21:33:25 +0300
901Branch: REL_14_STABLE [c7a1d5fc6] 2026-04-23 21:33:39 +0300
902-->
903     <para>
904      Disallow making a composite type be a member of itself via a
905      multirange (Heikki Linnakangas)
906      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ff8f27d6e">&sect;</ulink>
907     </para>
908
909     <para>
910      We already forbade such cases when the intermediate type is a
911      domain, array, composite type, or range; but multiranges were
912      overlooked.
913     </para>
914    </listitem>
915
916    <listitem>
917<!--
918Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
919Branch: master [0d866282b] 2026-03-30 16:14:34 +1300
920Branch: REL_18_STABLE [49315de0c] 2026-03-30 16:16:09 +1300
921Branch: REL_17_STABLE [d29808e35] 2026-03-30 16:16:39 +1300
922Branch: REL_16_STABLE [1bd90c887] 2026-03-30 16:17:14 +1300
923Branch: REL_15_STABLE [6b2e091f0] 2026-03-30 16:17:39 +1300
924Branch: REL_14_STABLE [6ce5c310b] 2026-03-30 16:18:13 +1300
925-->
926     <para>
927      Fix datum-image comparisons to be insensitive to sign-extension
928      variations (David Rowley)
929      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;49315de0c">&sect;</ulink>
930     </para>
931
932     <para>
933      This fixes some situations that previously led to <quote>could not
934      find memoization table entry</quote> errors or wrong query results.
935     </para>
936    </listitem>
937
938    <listitem>
939<!--
940Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
941Branch: master [94219a73f] 2026-04-24 14:03:12 +1200
942Branch: REL_18_STABLE [035c520db] 2026-04-24 14:03:41 +1200
943Branch: REL_17_STABLE [3fda3e12f] 2026-04-24 14:04:05 +1200
944Branch: REL_16_STABLE [a2a0060d5] 2026-04-24 14:04:31 +1200
945Branch: REL_15_STABLE [622f8b530] 2026-04-24 14:04:55 +1200
946Branch: REL_14_STABLE [109de35b7] 2026-04-24 14:05:23 +1200
947-->
948     <para>
949      Fix incorrect logic for hashed <literal>IN</literal>/<literal>NOT
950      IN</literal> with non-strict equality operator (Chengpeng Yan)
951      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;035c520db">&sect;</ulink>
952     </para>
953
954     <para>
955      The previous coding could crash or give wrong answers.  All built-in
956      data types have strict equality operators, so that this issue could
957      only arise with an extension data type.
958     </para>
959    </listitem>
960
961    <listitem>
962<!--
963Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
964Branch: master [a50777680] 2026-04-22 12:41:00 -0400
965Branch: REL_18_STABLE [580e7be88] 2026-04-22 12:41:00 -0400
966Branch: REL_17_STABLE [c97a28618] 2026-04-22 12:41:00 -0400
967Branch: REL_16_STABLE [e1e60f148] 2026-04-22 12:41:01 -0400
968Branch: REL_15_STABLE [f60d25986] 2026-04-22 12:41:01 -0400
969Branch: REL_14_STABLE [8a6f08c0c] 2026-04-22 12:41:01 -0400
970-->
971     <para>
972      Truncate overly-long locale-specific numeric symbols
973      in <function>to_char()</function> (Tom Lane)
974      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;580e7be88">&sect;</ulink>
975     </para>
976
977     <para>
978      If a locale specified a currency symbol, thousands separator, or
979      decimal or sign symbol more than 8 bytes long, a buffer overrun was
980      possible.  No such locales exist in the real world, and it's
981      impractical for an unprivileged attacker to install a malicious
982      locale definition underneath a Postgres server; but for safety's
983      sake check for overlength symbols and truncate if needed.
984     </para>
985    </listitem>
986
987    <listitem>
988<!--
989Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
990Branch: master [d7970e7e9] 2026-04-22 12:02:15 -0400
991Branch: REL_18_STABLE [00c6e0819] 2026-04-22 12:02:15 -0400
992Branch: REL_17_STABLE [ea5f0d176] 2026-04-22 12:02:15 -0400
993Branch: REL_16_STABLE [42383d32d] 2026-04-22 12:02:15 -0400
994Branch: REL_15_STABLE [0b196d3db] 2026-04-22 12:02:15 -0400
995Branch: REL_14_STABLE [21a24d709] 2026-04-22 12:02:15 -0400
996Branch: master [844bb90d4] 2026-04-22 10:47:56 -0400
997Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c2bfeb3bb] 2026-04-22 11:04:35 -0400
998Branch: REL_17_STABLE [a5426dbf8] 2026-04-22 11:05:51 -0400
999Branch: REL_16_STABLE [17f72e037] 2026-04-22 11:06:47 -0400
1000Branch: REL_15_STABLE [f852c9093] 2026-04-22 11:09:02 -0400
1001Branch: REL_14_STABLE [6cae0c2bd] 2026-04-22 11:09:21 -0400
1002-->
1003     <para>
1004      Prevent buffer overruns when parsing an affix file for
1005      an <literal>Ispell</literal> dictionary (Tom Lane)
1006      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;00c6e0819">&sect;</ulink>
1007      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c2bfeb3bb">&sect;</ulink>
1008     </para>
1009
1010     <para>
1011      A corrupt or malicious affix file could crash the server.
1012      This is not considered a security issue because text search
1013      configuration files are presumed trustworthy, but it still seems
1014      worth fixing.
1015     </para>
1016    </listitem>
1017
1018    <listitem>
1019<!--
1020Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
1021Branch: master [8b6c89e37] 2026-04-09 19:28:33 +0900
1022Branch: REL_18_STABLE [bfc7dff26] 2026-04-09 19:30:37 +0900
1023Branch: REL_17_STABLE [f8736f8bc] 2026-04-09 19:32:09 +0900
1024Branch: REL_16_STABLE [0fe032e6a] 2026-04-09 19:34:02 +0900
1025Branch: REL_15_STABLE [4da71fc37] 2026-04-09 19:35:08 +0900
1026Branch: REL_14_STABLE [305cf0df0] 2026-04-09 19:36:13 +0900
1027-->
1028     <para>
1029      Guard against integer overflow in calculations of frame start and
1030      end positions for window aggregates (Richard Guo)
1031      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bfc7dff26">&sect;</ulink>
1032     </para>
1033
1034     <para>
1035      Very large user-specified offsets (close to INT64_MAX) could result
1036      in errors or incorrect query results.
1037     </para>
1038    </listitem>
1039
1040    <listitem>
1041<!--
1042Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
1043Branch: master [6582010c8] 2026-04-06 13:14:53 -0400
1044Branch: REL_18_STABLE [14bf2c39e] 2026-04-06 13:14:50 -0400
1045Branch: REL_17_STABLE [d6c9432cb] 2026-04-06 13:14:50 -0400
1046Branch: REL_16_STABLE [bb959269e] 2026-04-06 13:14:50 -0400
1047-->
1048     <para>
1049      Fix <function>array_agg_array_combine()</function> to combine the
1050      arrays' null bitmaps correctly (Dmytro Astapov)
1051      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;14bf2c39e">&sect;</ulink>
1052     </para>
1053
1054     <para>
1055      This mistake resulted in sometimes-incorrect output from
1056      parallelized <function>array_agg(anyarray)</function> calculations.
1057     </para>
1058    </listitem>
1059
1060    <listitem>
1061<!--
1062Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
1063Branch: master [5941e7f09] 2026-04-30 18:44:38 +0900
1064Branch: REL_18_STABLE [6cb307251] 2026-04-30 18:44:41 +0900
1065Branch: REL_17_STABLE [5499be332] 2026-04-30 18:44:43 +0900
1066Branch: REL_16_STABLE [3b35c10a4] 2026-04-30 18:44:45 +0900
1067-->
1068     <para>
1069      Retry <function>sync_file_range()</function> if it returns error
1070      code <literal>EINTR</literal> (DaeMyung Kang)
1071      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6cb307251">&sect;</ulink>
1072     </para>
1073    </listitem>
1074
1075    <listitem>
1076<!--
1077Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
1078Branch: master [80156cee0] 2026-04-11 17:02:52 +0900
1079Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b081c5b07] 2026-04-11 17:03:04 +0900
1080Branch: REL_17_STABLE [a4fefb3e0] 2026-04-11 17:03:06 +0900
1081Branch: REL_16_STABLE [c7cdcbd3e] 2026-04-11 17:03:08 +0900
1082Branch: REL_15_STABLE [c6d3f0585] 2026-04-11 17:03:10 +0900
1083-->
1084     <para>
1085      Fix incorrect behavior
1086      of <function>pg_stat_reset_single_table_counters()</function> on a
1087      shared catalog (Chao Li)
1088      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b081c5b07">&sect;</ulink>
1089     </para>
1090
1091     <para>
1092      Such cases had a side-effect of resetting the
1093      current database's <literal>stat_reset_timestamp</literal>, which
1094      was unintended.
1095     </para>
1096    </listitem>
1097
1098    <listitem>
1099<!--
1100Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
1101Branch: master [090c4297e] 2026-04-20 10:31:11 +0530
1102Branch: REL_18_STABLE [44c8dc280] 2026-04-20 10:23:22 +0530
1103Branch: REL_17_STABLE [88d7fdcc9] 2026-04-20 10:17:47 +0530
1104Branch: REL_16_STABLE [d052f6c7d] 2026-04-20 10:13:55 +0530
1105-->
1106     <para>
1107      Update activity statistics when a parallel apply worker is idle
1108      (Zhijie Hou)
1109      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;44c8dc280">&sect;</ulink>
1110     </para>
1111
1112     <para>
1113      Previously, statistics from a recently-completed transaction might
1114      go unreported for long intervals, particularly if the workload is
1115      light.
1116     </para>
1117    </listitem>
1118
1119    <listitem>
1120<!--
1121Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
1122Branch: master [77d0e82e5] 2026-04-11 16:38:47 +0900
1123Branch: REL_18_STABLE [13e20d1c9] 2026-04-11 16:40:34 +0900
1124Branch: REL_17_STABLE [93ed18720] 2026-04-11 16:41:58 +0900
1125-->
1126     <para>
1127      Fix <quote>no relation entry for relid 0</quote> failure while
1128      estimating array lengths in set operations (Tender Wang)
1129      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;13e20d1c9">&sect;</ulink>
1130     </para>
1131    </listitem>
1132
1133    <listitem>
1134<!--
1135Author: Andrew Dunstan <andrew@dunslane.net>
1136Branch: master [2b5ba2a0a] 2026-04-10 07:13:08 -0400
1137Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c3e436b1c] 2026-04-10 10:28:00 -0400
1138Branch: REL_17_STABLE [c05c3baf1] 2026-04-10 10:27:43 -0400
1139Branch: REL_16_STABLE [e630f65d0] 2026-04-10 10:27:16 -0400
1140Branch: REL_15_STABLE [c88ad3a21] 2026-04-10 10:26:50 -0400
1141Branch: REL_14_STABLE [de32a01e7] 2026-04-10 10:22:48 -0400
1142-->
1143     <para>
1144      Fix buffer overread when <function>pglz_decompress()</function>
1145      receives corrupt input (Andrew Dunstan)
1146      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c3e436b1c">&sect;</ulink>
1147     </para>
1148
1149     <para>
1150      It was possible to read a few bytes past the end of the input, which
1151      in very unlucky cases might cause a crash.
1152     </para>
1153    </listitem>
1154
1155    <listitem>
1156<!--
1157Author: Andrew Dunstan <andrew@dunslane.net>
1158Branch: master [2478bd5db] 2026-04-10 07:13:08 -0400
1159Branch: REL_18_STABLE [3e4955630] 2026-04-10 10:21:38 -0400
1160Branch: REL_17_STABLE [2e373785e] 2026-04-10 10:21:39 -0400
1161-->
1162     <para>
1163      Fix incremental JSON parser's handling of numeric tokens that cross
1164      input buffer boundaries (Andrew Dunstan)
1165      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3e4955630">&sect;</ulink>
1166     </para>
1167
1168     <para>
1169      It was possible to accept an incorrectly-formatted number, leading
1170      to failures later.
1171     </para>
1172    </listitem>
1173
1174    <listitem>
1175<!--
1176Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
1177Branch: master [ffc226ab6] 2026-03-09 06:45:32 -0400
1178Branch: REL_18_STABLE [9540c0e5d] 2026-03-09 06:46:20 -0400
1179Branch: REL_17_STABLE [076bc57fa] 2026-03-09 06:55:10 -0400
1180-->
1181     <para>
1182      Prevent bloating relation visibility maps during restore of an
1183      incremental backup (Robert Haas)
1184      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9540c0e5d">&sect;</ulink>
1185     </para>
1186
1187     <para>
1188      Restore could append many blocks of zeroes to a visibility map, due
1189      to incorrect computation of the expected file length.  This does not
1190      result in data corruption, but it could waste a substantial amount
1191      of disk space.
1192     </para>
1193    </listitem>
1194
1195    <listitem>
1196<!--
1197Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
1198Branch: master [dbf217c1c] 2026-04-22 10:22:44 -0700
1199Branch: REL_18_STABLE [03c4f243e] 2026-04-22 10:23:44 -0700
1200Branch: REL_17_STABLE [9dda30dd3] 2026-04-22 10:23:54 -0700
1201-->
1202     <para>
1203      Use C collation, not the database's default collation, in catalog
1204      cache lookups on text columns (Jeff Davis)
1205      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;03c4f243e">&sect;</ulink>
1206     </para>
1207
1208     <para>
1209      This avoids failures in edge cases such as physical replication
1210      startup, where there is no identified database so that a default
1211      collation cannot be determined.
1212     </para>
1213    </listitem>
1214
1215    <listitem>
1216<!--
1217Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
1218Branch: master [db93032a7] 2026-04-08 11:22:21 +0900
1219Branch: REL_18_STABLE [58c1188a3] 2026-04-08 11:23:13 +0900
1220Branch: REL_17_STABLE [15910b1c3] 2026-04-08 11:24:00 +0900
1221Branch: REL_18_STABLE [acf49bfed] 2026-04-09 15:25:40 +0900
1222Branch: REL_17_STABLE [586f4266f] 2026-04-09 15:30:59 +0900
1223Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
1224Branch: master [1362bc33e] 2025-12-11 03:49:28 +0000
1225Branch: REL_18_STABLE [94efd308b] 2026-04-08 11:19:31 +0900
1226Branch: REL_17_STABLE [4bed04d39] 2026-04-08 11:21:14 +0900
1227-->
1228     <para>
1229      Prevent stuck slotsync worker processes from blocking promotion of a
1230      standby server (Nisha Moond, Ajin Cherian)
1231      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;58c1188a3">&sect;</ulink>
1232      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;acf49bfed">&sect;</ulink>
1233      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;94efd308b">&sect;</ulink>
1234     </para>
1235
1236     <para>
1237      A worker process that was vainly waiting for a response from the
1238      primary would delay promotion for an unreasonable amount of time.
1239     </para>
1240    </listitem>
1241
1242    <listitem>
1243<!--
1244Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
1245Branch: master [85c17f612] 2026-04-13 10:06:50 +0530
1246Branch: REL_18_STABLE [540fe8fb5] 2026-04-13 09:42:51 +0530
1247Branch: REL_17_STABLE [91741b7cb] 2026-04-13 09:21:34 +0530
1248-->
1249     <para>
1250      Fix excessive log output from idle slotsync worker processes
1251      (Zhijie Hou)
1252      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;540fe8fb5">&sect;</ulink>
1253     </para>
1254    </listitem>
1255
1256    <listitem>
1257<!--
1258Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
1259Branch: master [739477345] 2026-03-30 13:59:58 -0400
1260Branch: REL_18_STABLE [adb7873bb] 2026-03-30 13:59:54 -0400
1261Branch: REL_17_STABLE [1f5b6a5e5] 2026-03-30 13:59:54 -0400
1262Branch: REL_16_STABLE [59c139d53] 2026-03-30 13:59:54 -0400
1263Branch: REL_15_STABLE [811f3263a] 2026-03-30 13:59:54 -0400
1264Branch: REL_14_STABLE [7cd23aad2] 2026-03-30 13:59:54 -0400
1265-->
1266     <para>
1267      Ensure that tuplestore data structures are internally consistent
1268      even after an error (Tom Lane)
1269      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;adb7873bb">&sect;</ulink>
1270     </para>
1271
1272     <para>
1273      The code was previously careless about this, which is fine most of
1274      the time but is problematic for the tuplestore backing
1275      a <literal>WITH HOLD</literal> cursor.  In v15 and before this
1276      leads to easily-reproducible crashes; later branches are not known
1277      to be vulnerable, but it seems best to preserve consistency in all.
1278     </para>
1279    </listitem>
1280
1281    <listitem>
1282<!--
1283Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
1284Branch: master [93b76db0a] 2026-03-23 18:13:56 +0900
1285Branch: REL_18_STABLE [882bdcf9f] 2026-03-23 18:14:28 +0900
1286-->
1287     <para>
1288      Make the <structname>pg_aios</structname> system
1289      view's <structfield>pid</structfield> column show NULL not
1290      0 when an entry has no owning process (ChangAo Chen)
1291      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;882bdcf9f">&sect;</ulink>
1292     </para>
1293    </listitem>
1294
1295    <listitem>
1296<!--
1297Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
1298Branch: master [eef1ba704] 2026-03-26 20:49:31 +0900
1299Branch: REL_18_STABLE [98e96e579] 2026-03-26 20:50:22 +0900
1300Branch: REL_17_STABLE [fdce5de55] 2026-03-26 20:50:27 +0900
1301Branch: REL_16_STABLE [f42105001] 2026-03-26 20:50:33 +0900
1302Branch: REL_15_STABLE [246c296f0] 2026-03-26 20:50:38 +0900
1303Branch: REL_14_STABLE [bf7ecf353] 2026-03-26 20:50:43 +0900
1304-->
1305     <para>
1306      Fix premature NULL lag reporting
1307      in <structname>pg_stat_replication</structname> (Shinya Kato)
1308      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;98e96e579">&sect;</ulink>
1309     </para>
1310
1311     <para>
1312      The lag columns frequently read as NULL even while replication
1313      activity was happening.
1314     </para>
1315    </listitem>
1316
1317    <listitem>
1318<!--
1319Author: Peter Geoghegan <pg@bowt.ie>
1320Branch: master [748d871b7] 2026-04-29 11:22:23 -0400
1321Branch: REL_18_STABLE [1e71970d2] 2026-04-29 11:22:21 -0400
1322-->
1323     <para>
1324      Fix under-allocation of shared memory used for parallel btree index
1325      scans (Siddharth Kothari)
1326      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1e71970d2">&sect;</ulink>
1327     </para>
1328
1329     <para>
1330      In edge cases this could result in a server crash.
1331     </para>
1332    </listitem>
1333
1334    <listitem>
1335<!--
1336Author: Tomas Vondra <tomas.vondra@postgresql.org>
1337Branch: master [b1f14c967] 2026-03-13 23:25:24 +0100
1338Branch: REL_18_STABLE [5b3f63a1b] 2026-03-13 23:26:02 +0100
1339Branch: REL_17_STABLE [6ef36bb35] 2026-03-13 23:26:15 +0100
1340Branch: REL_16_STABLE [4f4025eac] 2026-03-13 23:26:25 +0100
1341Branch: REL_15_STABLE [ce06b5740] 2026-03-13 23:26:36 +0100
1342Branch: REL_14_STABLE [c0ffc725f] 2026-03-13 23:26:49 +0100
1343-->
1344     <para>
1345      Avoid rare flush failure when working with non-WAL-logged GiST
1346      indexes (Tomas Vondra)
1347      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;5b3f63a1b">&sect;</ulink>
1348     </para>
1349
1350     <para>
1351      A non-logged GiST index could nonetheless sometimes
1352      produce <quote>xlog flush request <replaceable>n/nnnn</replaceable>
1353      is not satisfied</quote> errors, due to incorrect selection of
1354      a <quote>fake LSN</quote> to represent an insertion point.
1355     </para>
1356    </listitem>
1357
1358    <listitem>
1359<!--
1360Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
1361Branch: master [4da2afd01] 2026-03-09 13:46:27 +0900
1362Branch: REL_18_STABLE [a0f38604d] 2026-03-09 13:46:31 +0900
1363Branch: REL_17_STABLE [2543b9ea9] 2026-03-09 13:46:33 +0900
1364Branch: REL_16_STABLE [0af5e64e9] 2026-03-09 13:46:35 +0900
1365Branch: REL_15_STABLE [46c93b705] 2026-03-09 13:46:37 +0900
1366Branch: REL_14_STABLE [eb11d7a91] 2026-03-09 13:46:38 +0900
1367-->
1368     <para>
1369      Fix underestimate of required size of DSA page maps for odd-size
1370      segments (Paul Bunn)
1371      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a0f38604d">&sect;</ulink>
1372     </para>
1373
1374     <para>
1375      This miscalculation led to out-of-bounds accesses and hence server
1376      crashes.
1377     </para>
1378    </listitem>
1379
1380    <listitem>
1381<!--
1382Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
1383Branch: master [ccae90abd] 2026-03-02 19:19:22 +0200
1384Branch: REL_18_STABLE [0a50ef094] 2026-03-02 19:19:26 +0200
1385Branch: REL_17_STABLE [dcd9c06a4] 2026-03-02 19:19:29 +0200
1386Branch: master [fe08113ae] 2026-03-04 11:29:02 +0200
1387Branch: REL_18_STABLE [fa3b328e6] 2026-03-04 11:30:03 +0200
1388Branch: REL_17_STABLE [969576dab] 2026-03-04 11:30:06 +0200
1389-->
1390     <para>
1391      Fix indexing of oldest-multixact arrays in shared memory
1392      (Yura Sokolov)
1393      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0a50ef094">&sect;</ulink>
1394      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;fa3b328e6">&sect;</ulink>
1395     </para>
1396
1397     <para>
1398      This mistake could cause a prepared-but-not-yet-committed
1399      transaction's row locks to appear invisible to other sessions, or
1400      other visibility issues for the results of such a transaction.
1401      With a very small max_connections setting, memory stomps were also
1402      possible.
1403     </para>
1404    </listitem>
1405
1406    <listitem>
1407<!--
1408Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
1409Branch: master [f7dc17aa9] 2026-03-02 13:14:15 +0900
1410Branch: REL_18_STABLE [730c98d03] 2026-03-02 13:14:18 +0900
1411-->
1412     <para>
1413      Fix array overrun when too many <command>EXPLAIN</command> extension
1414      options are installed (Joel Jacobson)
1415      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;730c98d03">&sect;</ulink>
1416     </para>
1417    </listitem>
1418
1419    <listitem>
1420<!--
1421Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
1422Branch: master [3b7a6fa15] 2026-03-02 09:38:37 +0900
1423Branch: REL_18_STABLE [83671c0da] 2026-03-02 09:38:40 +0900
1424Branch: REL_17_STABLE [530b6b02f] 2026-03-02 09:38:42 +0900
1425Branch: REL_16_STABLE [04745ba9c] 2026-03-02 09:38:44 +0900
1426Branch: REL_15_STABLE [f033abc6c] 2026-03-02 09:38:46 +0900
1427Branch: REL_14_STABLE [038c7d4a3] 2026-03-02 09:38:47 +0900
1428-->
1429     <para>
1430      Fix possible server crash when processing extended statistics on
1431      expressions of extension data types (Michael Paquier)
1432      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;83671c0da">&sect;</ulink>
1433     </para>
1434
1435     <para>
1436      NULL pointer dereferences were possible if the data type's
1437      typanalyze function does not compute any useful statistics.
1438      No in-core typanalyze function behaves that way, but extensions
1439      could.
1440     </para>
1441    </listitem>
1442
1443    <listitem>
1444<!--
1445Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
1446Branch: master [98616ac18] 2026-02-27 12:54:02 -0500
1447Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c2c1962a6] 2026-02-27 12:54:02 -0500
1448-->
1449     <para>
1450      Correctly display join alias Vars that are used in <literal>GROUP
1451      BY</literal> (Tom Lane)
1452      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c2c1962a6">&sect;</ulink>
1453     </para>
1454
1455     <para>
1456      In views containing queries like <literal>SELECT ... t1 LEFT JOIN t2
1457      USING (x) GROUP BY x</literal>, the <literal>GROUP BY</literal>
1458      clause could be displayed incorrectly by deparsing, leading to
1459      dump/restore failures.  Failures occurred only
1460      if <literal>t1.x</literal> and <literal>t2.x</literal> were not of
1461      identical data types and <literal>t1.x</literal> was the side that
1462      required an implicit coercion.
1463     </para>
1464    </listitem>
1465
1466    <listitem>
1467<!--
1468Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
1469Branch: master [d942511f0] 2026-02-26 12:15:01 -0800
1470Branch: REL_18_STABLE [4abf63c62] 2026-02-26 12:15:15 -0800
1471Branch: REL_17_STABLE [4761f2eee] 2026-02-26 12:15:23 -0800
1472Branch: REL_16_STABLE [a4515c9b4] 2026-02-26 12:15:31 -0800
1473-->
1474     <para>
1475      Fix minor memory leaks in ICU-based string processing (Jeff Davis)
1476      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4abf63c62">&sect;</ulink>
1477     </para>
1478    </listitem>
1479
1480    <listitem>
1481<!--
1482Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
1483Branch: master [9b43e6793] 2026-04-21 09:39:59 +0900
1484Branch: REL_18_STABLE [affdb2dd5] 2026-04-21 09:40:03 +0900
1485Branch: REL_17_STABLE [e381843cf] 2026-04-21 09:40:04 +0900
1486Branch: REL_16_STABLE [2d347f2cd] 2026-04-21 09:40:06 +0900
1487Branch: REL_15_STABLE [23cebf672] 2026-04-21 09:40:07 +0900
1488-->
1489     <para>
1490      If the startup process fails, properly shut down other child
1491      processes before exiting the postmaster (Ayush Tiwari)
1492      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;affdb2dd5">&sect;</ulink>
1493     </para>
1494
1495     <para>
1496      The handling of this situation relied on a long-obsolete assumption
1497      that no other postmaster children exist while the startup process is
1498      running, so that immediate postmaster exit is acceptable.
1499      Orphaned children would eventually notice the postmaster's death and
1500      exit on their own, but a cleaner shutdown procedure is desirable.
1501     </para>
1502    </listitem>
1503
1504    <listitem>
1505<!--
1506Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
1507Branch: REL_18_STABLE [0852643e1] 2026-03-23 11:53:32 +0200
1508Branch: REL_17_STABLE [1ca385032] 2026-03-23 11:59:07 +0200
1509Branch: REL_16_STABLE [77dff5d93] 2026-03-23 12:02:27 +0200
1510Branch: REL_15_STABLE [a5f412107] 2026-03-23 12:02:35 +0200
1511Branch: REL_14_STABLE [e35e466f6] 2026-03-23 12:03:30 +0200
1512-->
1513     <para>
1514      Fix race condition between WAL replay of checkpoints and multixact
1515      ID creations (Heikki Linnakangas)
1516      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0852643e1">&sect;</ulink>
1517     </para>
1518
1519     <para>
1520      A standby server following WAL from a primary of an older minor
1521      version could get into a crash-and-restart loop complaining
1522      about <quote>could not access status of transaction</quote>.
1523     </para>
1524    </listitem>
1525
1526    <listitem>
1527<!--
1528Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
1529Branch: master [6eedb2a5f] 2026-03-06 16:43:40 +0900
1530Branch: REL_18_STABLE [3eb2fecdb] 2026-03-06 16:44:40 +0900
1531Branch: REL_17_STABLE [bbbc0888b] 2026-03-06 16:44:45 +0900
1532Branch: REL_16_STABLE [82935467a] 2026-03-06 16:44:51 +0900
1533Branch: REL_15_STABLE [42734f296] 2026-03-06 16:44:59 +0900
1534Branch: REL_14_STABLE [3bf6f22ce] 2026-03-06 16:45:05 +0900
1535Branch: master [d927b4bd9] 2026-03-17 08:10:20 +0900
1536Branch: REL_18_STABLE [980498138] 2026-03-17 08:12:25 +0900
1537Branch: REL_17_STABLE [8ee536c89] 2026-03-17 08:12:32 +0900
1538Branch: REL_16_STABLE [da21ecf57] 2026-03-17 08:12:37 +0900
1539Branch: REL_15_STABLE [fa9f2e317] 2026-03-17 08:12:41 +0900
1540Branch: REL_14_STABLE [f15471464] 2026-03-17 08:12:46 +0900
1541-->
1542     <para>
1543      Prevent indefinite wait in shutdown of a walsender process
1544      (Anthonin Bonnefoy)
1545      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3eb2fecdb">&sect;</ulink>
1546      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;980498138">&sect;</ulink>
1547     </para>
1548
1549     <para>
1550      At shutdown of a cluster that is publishing logical replication
1551      data, the walsender waits for all pending WAL to be written out.
1552      But it did not correctly request that to happen, so that in some
1553      cases this could become an indefinite wait.
1554     </para>
1555    </listitem>
1556
1557    <listitem>
1558<!--
1559Author: Alexander Korotkov <akorotkov@postgresql.org>
1560Branch: master [c06d1a4ba] 2026-05-03 20:23:50 +0300
1561Branch: REL_18_STABLE [ac3b97db3] 2026-05-03 20:27:36 +0300
1562Branch: REL_17_STABLE [1cf010f21] 2026-05-03 20:27:33 +0300
1563Branch: REL_16_STABLE [54537de35] 2026-05-03 20:27:02 +0300
1564Branch: REL_15_STABLE [ca259b084] 2026-05-03 20:26:55 +0300
1565Branch: REL_14_STABLE [f5d1038d9] 2026-05-03 20:26:07 +0300
1566-->
1567     <para>
1568      Ensure that changes to tables' free space maps are persisted during
1569      recovery (Alexey Makhmutov)
1570      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ac3b97db3">&sect;</ulink>
1571     </para>
1572
1573     <para>
1574      Previously, while WAL replay did update the free space map while
1575      replaying operations that should change it, the map page buffer did
1576      not get marked dirty if checksums are enabled, so that the changes
1577      might never get written out.  On a standby server, over time this
1578      would result in a map wildly at variance with the table's actual
1579      contents.  While the map is only used as a hint, this condition
1580      could cause significant performance degradation for some period of
1581      time after the standby server is promoted to be active, until most
1582      of the map has been repaired by updates.
1583     </para>
1584    </listitem>
1585
1586    <listitem>
1587<!--
1588Author: Andrew Dunstan <andrew@dunslane.net>
1589Branch: master [c34a280c8] 2026-05-01 15:12:28 -0400
1590Branch: REL_18_STABLE [e2688ea5e] 2026-05-01 15:15:15 -0400
1591Branch: REL_17_STABLE [5d67549d9] 2026-05-01 15:15:23 -0400
1592Branch: REL_16_STABLE [7e4c871f4] 2026-05-01 15:15:33 -0400
1593Branch: REL_15_STABLE [6916f4410] 2026-05-01 15:16:02 -0400
1594Branch: REL_14_STABLE [f0e3f9732] 2026-05-01 15:16:42 -0400
1595-->
1596     <para>
1597      Fix crashes in some <application>ecpg</application> functions when
1598      called without any established connection (Shruthi Gowda)
1599      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e2688ea5e">&sect;</ulink>
1600     </para>
1601    </listitem>
1602
1603    <listitem>
1604<!--
1605Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
1606Branch: master [bc30c704a] 2026-04-02 12:20:36 -0400
1607Branch: REL_18_STABLE [698eae7db] 2026-04-02 12:20:26 -0400
1608-->
1609     <para>
1610      Harden tar-file parsing logic against archives it can't handle
1611      (Tom Lane)
1612      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;698eae7db">&sect;</ulink>
1613     </para>
1614
1615     <para>
1616      The tar-file reading code used
1617      in <application>pg_basebackup</application>
1618      and <application>pg_verifybackup</application> failed to verify that
1619      the input is a tar file at all, let alone that it fits into the
1620      subset of valid tar files that we can handle.  This isn't a problem
1621      for the normal scenario where the input file was generated
1622      by <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> code, but it can be an
1623      issue if the input has been generated by some
1624      other <application>tar</application> program.
1625     </para>
1626    </listitem>
1627
1628    <listitem>
1629<!--
1630Author: Andrew Dunstan <andrew@dunslane.net>
1631Branch: master [01d58d7e3] 2026-03-29 09:01:47 -0400
1632Branch: REL_18_STABLE [5095f3f4a] 2026-03-29 09:02:15 -0400
1633Branch: REL_17_STABLE [f1298a4c2] 2026-03-29 09:11:37 -0400
1634Branch: REL_16_STABLE [1590723f0] 2026-03-29 09:12:28 -0400
1635Branch: REL_15_STABLE [d3bb7841b] 2026-03-29 09:12:40 -0400
1636Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
1637Branch: master [ac0accafd] 2026-03-05 14:43:21 -0500
1638Branch: REL_18_STABLE [a01a592b1] 2026-03-05 14:43:21 -0500
1639Branch: REL_17_STABLE [8b198b093] 2026-03-05 14:43:21 -0500
1640Branch: REL_16_STABLE [cce939c71] 2026-03-05 14:43:21 -0500
1641Branch: REL_15_STABLE [9a42888a3] 2026-03-05 14:43:21 -0500
1642Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
1643Branch: master [c70f6dc6b] 2026-03-04 12:08:37 -0500
1644Branch: REL_18_STABLE [78dc9a808] 2026-03-04 12:08:37 -0500
1645Branch: REL_17_STABLE [2640c5ba7] 2026-03-04 12:08:37 -0500
1646Branch: REL_16_STABLE [415cc943f] 2026-03-04 12:08:37 -0500
1647Branch: REL_15_STABLE [4548e8746] 2026-03-04 12:08:37 -0500
1648-->
1649     <para>
1650      Fix assorted bugs in backup decompression and tar-parsing code
1651      (Andrew Dunstan, Tom Lane, Chao Li)
1652      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;5095f3f4a">&sect;</ulink>
1653      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a01a592b1">&sect;</ulink>
1654      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;78dc9a808">&sect;</ulink>
1655     </para>
1656
1657     <para>
1658      The decompression and tar-file reading code used
1659      in <application>pg_basebackup</application>
1660      and <application>pg_verifybackup</application> mishandled tar-file
1661      padding data, could corrupt LZ4-compressed data in edge cases,
1662      failed to check for some unusual error conditions, failed to exit
1663      after compression/decompression errors (leading to cascading error
1664      reports), and leaked memory.
1665     </para>
1666    </listitem>
1667
1668    <listitem>
1669<!--
1670Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@kurilemu.de>
1671Branch: master [d0833fdae] 2026-02-26 11:50:26 +0100
1672Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c3c8b63d7] 2026-02-26 11:50:26 +0100
1673-->
1674     <para>
1675      In <application>pg_dump</application>, preserve <literal>NO
1676      INHERIT</literal> property of <literal>NOT NULL</literal>
1677      constraints (Jian He)
1678      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c3c8b63d7">&sect;</ulink>
1679     </para>
1680
1681     <para>
1682      Some cases missed printing the <literal>NO INHERIT</literal> clause.
1683     </para>
1684    </listitem>
1685
1686    <listitem>
1687<!--
1688Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
1689Branch: master [74b4438a7] 2026-03-02 11:15:10 -0500
1690Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b09158cc7] 2026-03-02 11:14:58 -0500
1691Branch: REL_17_STABLE [1cd783d20] 2026-03-02 11:14:58 -0500
1692Branch: REL_16_STABLE [a4649c50a] 2026-03-02 11:14:58 -0500
1693-->
1694     <para>
1695      In <application>pg_dumpall</application>, don't skip
1696      role <literal>GRANT</literal>s with dangling grantor OIDs
1697      (Tom Lane)
1698      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b09158cc7">&sect;</ulink>
1699     </para>
1700
1701     <para>
1702      Instead, handle such cases by emitting <literal>GRANT</literal>
1703      without any <literal>GRANTED BY</literal> clause, as we did before
1704      v16.  This avoids losing the grant in foreseeable cases, since
1705      pre-v16 servers didn't prevent dropping the grantor role.  Continue
1706      to emit a warning about the missing grantor, but only if the source
1707      server is v16 or later.
1708     </para>
1709    </listitem>
1710
1711    <listitem>
1712<!--
1713Author: Jacob Champion <jchampion@postgresql.org>
1714Branch: master [a60a10338] 2026-02-24 14:01:37 -0800
1715Branch: REL_18_STABLE [1b2773179] 2026-02-24 14:01:41 -0800
1716Branch: REL_17_STABLE [ad7fc3f1f] 2026-02-24 14:01:46 -0800
1717Branch: REL_16_STABLE [a38ed212f] 2026-02-24 14:01:51 -0800
1718Branch: REL_15_STABLE [e726620d2] 2026-02-24 14:01:54 -0800
1719Branch: REL_14_STABLE [c47744ede] 2026-02-24 14:01:58 -0800
1720-->
1721     <para>
1722      In <application>pg_upgrade</application>, take care to use the
1723      correct protocol version when connecting to older source servers
1724      (Jacob Champion)
1725      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1b2773179">&sect;</ulink>
1726     </para>
1727
1728     <para>
1729      This could be problematic when attempting to upgrade from a pre-2018
1730      server.
1731     </para>
1732    </listitem>
1733
1734    <listitem>
1735<!--
1736Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
1737Branch: master [cc774c543] 2026-03-02 13:12:25 -0600
1738Branch: REL_18_STABLE [bde9ad315] 2026-03-02 13:12:25 -0600
1739Branch: REL_17_STABLE [f510577de] 2026-03-02 13:12:25 -0600
1740Branch: REL_16_STABLE [28c2b7896] 2026-03-02 13:12:25 -0600
1741Branch: REL_15_STABLE [8fc45ac5d] 2026-03-02 13:12:25 -0600
1742-->
1743     <para>
1744      In <filename>contrib/basic_archive</filename>, allow the archive
1745      directory to be missing at startup (Nathan Bossart)
1746      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bde9ad315">&sect;</ulink>
1747     </para>
1748
1749     <para>
1750      Previously, the setting
1751      of <literal>basic_archive.archive_directory</literal> was rejected
1752      if it didn't point to an existing directory.  This is undesirable
1753      because archiving will be stuck indefinitely, even if the directory
1754      appears later.
1755     </para>
1756    </listitem>
1757
1758    <listitem>
1759<!--
1760Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
1761Branch: master [c8308a984] 2026-02-26 12:23:22 -0800
1762Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b3c2a3d38] 2026-02-26 12:23:51 -0800
1763Branch: REL_18_STABLE [53a57cae1] 2026-02-26 15:19:31 -0800
1764Branch: REL_17_STABLE [d1bd9a7dc] 2026-02-26 12:24:12 -0800
1765Branch: REL_16_STABLE [3ed2c7ef7] 2026-02-26 12:25:00 -0800
1766Branch: REL_15_STABLE [2b993167f] 2026-02-26 12:26:13 -0800
1767Branch: REL_14_STABLE [058710d41] 2026-02-26 12:26:32 -0800
1768-->
1769     <para>
1770      Fix <filename>contrib/ltree</filename> to cope when case-folding
1771      changes a string's byte length (Jeff Davis)
1772      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b3c2a3d38">&sect;</ulink>
1773      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;53a57cae1">&sect;</ulink>
1774     </para>
1775
1776     <para>
1777      Previously, <type>lquery</type> patterns specifying case-insensitive
1778      matching might fail to match labels they should match.
1779     </para>
1780    </listitem>
1781
1782    <listitem>
1783<!--
1784Author: Amit Langote <amitlan@postgresql.org>
1785Branch: master [059cf7f58] 2026-04-16 13:47:07 +0900
1786Branch: REL_18_STABLE [6723d462d] 2026-04-16 13:49:39 +0900
1787-->
1788     <para>
1789      Fix mis-structured output
1790      from <filename>contrib/pg_overexplain</filename>'s
1791      <literal>RANGE_TABLE</literal> option (Satyanarayana Narlapuram)
1792      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6723d462d">&sect;</ulink>
1793     </para>
1794
1795     <para>
1796      Some fields were misplaced in JSON, YAML, and XML formats, resulting
1797      in structurally invalid output.
1798     </para>
1799    </listitem>
1800
1801    <listitem>
1802<!--
1803Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
1804Branch: master [3c74cb576] 2026-03-27 12:25:10 +0200
1805Branch: REL_18_STABLE [25b02320e] 2026-03-27 12:21:29 +0200
1806Branch: REL_17_STABLE [351e59f34] 2026-03-27 12:21:38 +0200
1807Branch: REL_16_STABLE [52edaf9d9] 2026-03-27 12:21:48 +0200
1808Branch: REL_15_STABLE [92cf11171] 2026-03-27 12:22:21 +0200
1809Branch: REL_14_STABLE [a6d03067f] 2026-03-27 12:23:38 +0200
1810-->
1811     <para>
1812      In <filename>contrib/pg_stat_statements</filename>, don't leak
1813      memory if an error occurs while parsing the
1814      <filename>pgss_query_texts.stat</filename> file (Heikki Linnakangas)
1815      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;25b02320e">&sect;</ulink>
1816     </para>
1817    </listitem>
1818
1819    <listitem>
1820<!--
1821Author: Etsuro Fujita <efujita@postgresql.org>
1822Branch: master [648818ba3] 2026-05-05 18:55:00 +0900
1823Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c318777da] 2026-05-05 18:55:02 +0900
1824Branch: REL_17_STABLE [af8f9248f] 2026-05-05 18:55:03 +0900
1825Branch: REL_16_STABLE [1352651c2] 2026-05-05 18:55:04 +0900
1826Branch: REL_15_STABLE [34c18a225] 2026-05-05 18:55:05 +0900
1827Branch: REL_14_STABLE [fd5b36ab1] 2026-05-05 18:55:06 +0900
1828-->
1829     <para>
1830      In <filename>contrib/postgres_fdw</filename>, avoid crash due to
1831      premature cleanup of a failed connection (Etsuro Fujita)
1832      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c318777da">&sect;</ulink>
1833     </para>
1834
1835     <para>
1836      If a remote connection fails abort cleanup, we can't use it any
1837      longer.  But delay closing the connection object until end of
1838      transaction, because there might still be references to it within
1839      data structures such as open cursors.
1840     </para>
1841    </listitem>
1842
1843    <listitem>
1844<!--
1845Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
1846Branch: master [f64f62f5b] 2026-04-24 12:28:35 -0400
1847Branch: REL_18_STABLE [8a431b6d6] 2026-04-24 12:28:35 -0400
1848Branch: REL_17_STABLE [4c0eab6f0] 2026-04-24 12:28:35 -0400
1849Branch: REL_16_STABLE [0465c999e] 2026-04-24 12:28:35 -0400
1850Branch: REL_15_STABLE [e28fc73d5] 2026-04-24 12:28:35 -0400
1851Branch: REL_14_STABLE [affd929c9] 2026-04-24 12:28:35 -0400
1852-->
1853     <para>
1854      Update time zone data files to <application>tzdata</application>
1855      release 2026b (Tom Lane)
1856      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8a431b6d6">&sect;</ulink>
1857     </para>
1858
1859     <para>
1860      British Columbia (America/Vancouver) will be on year-round UTC-07
1861      (effectively, permanent DST) beginning in November 2026.  This
1862      release assumes that their TZ abbreviation will
1863      be <literal>MST</literal> from that time forward.  That seems likely
1864      to change, but it's unclear what new abbreviation will be used.
1865      Also a historical correction for Moldova: they have followed EU DST
1866      transition times since 2022.
1867     </para>
1868    </listitem>
1869
1870   </itemizedlist>
1871
1872  </sect2>
1873 </sect1>
1874
1875 <sect1 id="release-18-3">
1876  <title>Release 18.3</title>
1877
1878  <formalpara>
1879  <title>Release date:</title>
1880  <para>2026-02-26</para>
1881  </formalpara>
1882
1883  <para>
1884   This release contains a small number of fixes from 18.2.
1885   For information about new features in major release 18, see
1886   <xref linkend="release-18"/>.
1887  </para>
1888
1889  <sect2 id="release-18-3-migration">
1890   <title>Migration to Version 18.3</title>
1891
1892   <para>
1893    A dump/restore is not required for those running 18.X.
1894   </para>
1895
1896   <para>
1897    However, if you are upgrading from a version earlier than 18.2,
1898    see <xref linkend="release-18-2"/>.
1899   </para>
1900  </sect2>
1901
1902  <sect2 id="release-18-3-changes">
1903   <title>Changes</title>
1904
1905   <itemizedlist>
1906
1907    <listitem>
1908<!--
1909Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
1910Branch: REL_18_STABLE [817f74600] 2026-02-16 17:16:59 +0200
1911Branch: REL_17_STABLE [4a36c89f1] 2026-02-16 17:18:17 +0200
1912Branch: REL_16_STABLE [23064542f] 2026-02-16 17:20:29 +0200
1913Branch: REL_15_STABLE [899de38d8] 2026-02-16 17:20:51 +0200
1914Branch: REL_14_STABLE [547a8aaa7] 2026-02-16 17:20:56 +0200
1915-->
1916     <para>
1917      Fix failure after replaying a multixid truncation record from WAL
1918      that was generated by an older minor version (Heikki Linnakangas)
1919      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;817f74600">&sect;</ulink>
1920     </para>
1921
1922     <para>
1923      Erroneous logic for coping with the way that previous versions
1924      handled multixid wraparound led to replay failure, with messages
1925      like <quote>could not access status of transaction</quote>.
1926      A typical scenario in which this could occur is a standby server of
1927      the latest minor version consuming WAL from a primary server of an
1928      older version.
1929     </para>
1930    </listitem>
1931
1932    <listitem>
1933<!--
1934Author: Noah Misch <noah@leadboat.com>
1935Branch: master [9f4fd119b] 2026-02-14 12:16:16 -0800
1936Branch: REL_18_STABLE [6e045e1a6] 2026-02-14 12:16:19 -0800
1937Branch: REL_17_STABLE [5d5232bc3] 2026-02-14 12:16:20 -0800
1938Branch: REL_16_STABLE [bdfb37228] 2026-02-14 12:16:20 -0800
1939Branch: REL_15_STABLE [a20eb248c] 2026-02-14 12:16:21 -0800
1940Branch: REL_14_STABLE [14b1fd617] 2026-02-14 12:16:21 -0800
1941Branch: master [8cef93d8a] 2026-02-16 18:04:58 -0800
1942Branch: REL_18_STABLE [d04b34d68] 2026-02-16 18:05:01 -0800
1943Branch: REL_17_STABLE [50d361f62] 2026-02-16 18:05:02 -0800
1944Branch: REL_16_STABLE [2280ab354] 2026-02-16 18:05:02 -0800
1945Branch: REL_15_STABLE [ec86152e0] 2026-02-16 18:05:02 -0800
1946Branch: REL_14_STABLE [a0769e74d] 2026-02-16 18:05:03 -0800
1947Branch: master [4644f8b23] 2026-02-14 12:16:16 -0800
1948Branch: REL_18_STABLE [4174e41b9] 2026-02-14 12:16:19 -0800
1949Branch: REL_17_STABLE [8e73530f1] 2026-02-14 12:16:20 -0800
1950Branch: REL_16_STABLE [45eb47230] 2026-02-14 12:16:20 -0800
1951Branch: REL_15_STABLE [5b305ebcc] 2026-02-14 12:16:20 -0800
1952Branch: REL_14_STABLE [44fc85bbf] 2026-02-14 12:16:21 -0800
1953-->
1954     <para>
1955      Avoid incorrect complaint of invalid encoding
1956      when <function>substring()</function> is applied
1957      to <quote>toasted</quote> data (Noah Misch)
1958      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6e045e1a6">&sect;</ulink>
1959      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d04b34d68">&sect;</ulink>
1960      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4174e41b9">&sect;</ulink>
1961     </para>
1962
1963     <para>
1964      The fix for CVE-2026-2006 was too aggressive and could raise an
1965      error about an incomplete character in cases that are actually
1966      valid.
1967     </para>
1968    </listitem>
1969
1970    <listitem>
1971<!--
1972Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
1973Branch: master [18bcdb75d] 2026-02-20 11:56:42 +0200
1974Branch: REL_18_STABLE [041e02e6a] 2026-02-20 11:56:50 +0200
1975-->
1976     <para>
1977      Fix oversight in the fix for CVE-2026-2007 (Zsolt Parragi)
1978      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;041e02e6a">&sect;</ulink>
1979     </para>
1980
1981     <para>
1982      If the <quote>bounds</quote> array needed to be expanded, because
1983      the input contained more trigrams than the initial guess,
1984      <function>generate_trgm_only</function> didn't return the modified
1985      array pointer to its caller.  That would lead to incorrect output
1986      from <function>strict_word_similarity()</function> and related
1987      functions, or in rare cases a crash.  The faulty code is reached if
1988      the input string becomes longer when it's converted to lower case.
1989      The only known instances of that occur when an ICU locale is used
1990      with certain single-byte encodings.
1991     </para>
1992    </listitem>
1993
1994    <listitem>
1995<!--
1996Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
1997Branch: master [759b03b24] 2026-02-18 14:14:44 -0500
1998Author: Andrew Dunstan <andrew@dunslane.net>
1999Branch: REL_18_STABLE [2f6ee7b38] 2026-02-18 10:31:03 -0500
2000-->
2001     <para>
2002      Fix the volatility marking
2003      of <function>json_strip_nulls()</function>
2004      and <function>jsonb_strip_nulls()</function> (Andrew Dunstan)
2005      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;2f6ee7b38">&sect;</ulink>
2006     </para>
2007
2008     <para>
2009      These functions have always been considered immutable, but
2010      refactoring in version 18 accidentally marked them stable instead.
2011      That prevents their use in index expressions and could cause
2012      unnecessary repeat evaluations in queries.  This fix corrects the
2013      marking in newly-initialized database clusters (including clusters
2014      that are <application>pg_upgrade</application>'d to 18.3 or later).
2015      However it will not help existing clusters made using 18.0 through
2016      18.2.
2017     </para>
2018
2019     <para>
2020      If this mistake affects your usage of these functions, the
2021      recommended fix for an existing cluster is a manual catalog update.
2022      As superuser, perform
2023<programlisting>
2024UPDATE pg_catalog.pg_proc SET provolatile = 'i' WHERE oid IN ('3261','3262');
2025</programlisting>
2026      in each affected database.  Update <literal>template0</literal>
2027      and <literal>template1</literal> as well, so that databases made in
2028      future will have the fix.
2029     </para>
2030    </listitem>
2031
2032    <listitem>
2033<!--
2034Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
2035Branch: master [691977d37] 2026-02-20 17:57:53 +0900
2036Branch: REL_18_STABLE [ed57c207c] 2026-02-20 18:00:02 +0900
2037Branch: REL_17_STABLE [bcaf1b510] 2026-02-20 18:01:56 +0900
2038Branch: REL_16_STABLE [ec20a4552] 2026-02-20 18:04:26 +0900
2039-->
2040     <para>
2041      Fix computation of the set of potentially-nulling outer joins for
2042      the output of a <literal>LATERAL UNION ALL</literal> subquery
2043      (Richard Guo)
2044      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ed57c207c">&sect;</ulink>
2045     </para>
2046
2047     <para>
2048      This error could lead to skipping <literal>NOT NULL</literal> tests
