2049      in the mistaken belief that they were unnecessary, resulting in
2050      wrong query output.
2051     </para>
2052    </listitem>
2053
2054    <listitem>
2055<!--
2056Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@kurilemu.de>
2057Branch: master [0eeffd31b] 2026-02-21 12:22:08 +0100
2058Branch: REL_18_STABLE [8d9a97e0b] 2026-02-21 12:22:08 +0100
2059-->
2060     <para>
2061      Avoid name collisions between user-written constraints and
2062      automatically-named <literal>NOT NULL</literal> constraints
2063      (Laurenz Albe)
2064      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8d9a97e0b">&sect;</ulink>
2065     </para>
2066
2067     <para>
2068      As of version 18, <literal>NOT NULL</literal> constraints have
2069      full-fledged <structname>pg_constraint</structname> entries, and
2070      therefore require names.  The logic for choosing a name for an
2071      unnamed <literal>NOT NULL</literal> constraint failed to avoid
2072      conflicts with user-written constraints elsewhere in the
2073      same <command>CREATE TABLE</command> statement.
2074     </para>
2075    </listitem>
2076
2077    <listitem>
2078<!--
2079Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
2080Branch: master [78a5e3074] 2026-02-11 18:50:57 +0200
2081Branch: REL_18_STABLE [53463b4b2] 2026-02-11 18:51:06 +0200
2082Branch: REL_17_STABLE [842473337] 2026-02-11 18:51:12 +0200
2083Branch: REL_16_STABLE [2332911ae] 2026-02-11 18:51:19 +0200
2084Branch: REL_15_STABLE [ebc53ca7b] 2026-02-11 18:51:25 +0200
2085Branch: REL_14_STABLE [82b495cdd] 2026-02-11 18:51:32 +0200
2086-->
2087     <para>
2088      Fix <function>pg_stat_get_backend_wait_event()</function>
2089      and <function>pg_stat_get_backend_wait_event_type()</function>
2090      to report values for auxiliary processes (Heikki Linnakangas)
2091      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;53463b4b2">&sect;</ulink>
2092     </para>
2093
2094     <para>
2095      Previously these functions returned NULL for auxiliary processes,
2096      but that's inconsistent with
2097      the <structname>pg_stat_activity</structname> view.
2098     </para>
2099    </listitem>
2100
2101    <listitem>
2102<!--
2103Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
2104Branch: master [9863c9075] 2026-02-11 16:53:14 -0500
2105Branch: REL_18_STABLE [ce4b7e3a1] 2026-02-11 16:53:14 -0500
2106Branch: REL_17_STABLE [dfd850980] 2026-02-11 16:53:14 -0500
2107Branch: REL_16_STABLE [c66f4cff1] 2026-02-11 16:53:14 -0500
2108Branch: REL_15_STABLE [254b15cbf] 2026-02-11 16:53:14 -0500
2109Branch: REL_14_STABLE [2b93d3820] 2026-02-11 16:53:14 -0500
2110-->
2111     <para>
2112      Fix casting a composite-type variable to a domain type when
2113      returning its value from a PL/pgSQL function (Tom Lane)
2114      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ce4b7e3a1">&sect;</ulink>
2115     </para>
2116
2117     <para>
2118      If the variable's value is NULL, a <quote>cache lookup failed for
2119      type 0</quote> error resulted.
2120     </para>
2121    </listitem>
2122
2123    <listitem>
2124<!--
2125Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
2126Branch: master [a6f823e77] 2026-02-17 08:41:26 +0900
2127Branch: REL_18_STABLE [4a0843c53] 2026-02-17 08:41:30 +0900
2128Branch: REL_17_STABLE [0dfbe42da] 2026-02-17 08:41:32 +0900
2129Branch: REL_16_STABLE [31d0b917d] 2026-02-17 08:41:34 +0900
2130Branch: REL_15_STABLE [63c05e03b] 2026-02-17 08:41:35 +0900
2131Branch: REL_14_STABLE [f604cc695] 2026-02-17 08:41:37 +0900
2132-->
2133     <para>
2134      Fix potential null pointer dereference
2135      in <filename>contrib/hstore</filename>'s binary input function
2136      (Michael Paquier)
2137      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4a0843c53">&sect;</ulink>
2138     </para>
2139
2140     <para>
2141      <type>hstore</type>'s receive function crashed on input containing
2142      duplicate keys.  <type>hstore</type> values generated by Postgres
2143      would never contain duplicate keys, so this mistake has gone
2144      unnoticed.  The crash could be provoked by malicious or corrupted
2145      data.
2146     </para>
2147    </listitem>
2148
2149   </itemizedlist>
2150
2151  </sect2>
2152 </sect1>
2153
2154 <sect1 id="release-18-2">
2155  <title>Release 18.2</title>
2156
2157  <formalpara>
2158  <title>Release date:</title>
2159  <para>2026-02-12</para>
2160  </formalpara>
2161
2162  <para>
2163   This release contains a variety of fixes from 18.1.
2164   For information about new features in major release 18, see
2165   <xref linkend="release-18"/>.
2166  </para>
2167
2168  <sect2 id="release-18-2-migration">
2169   <title>Migration to Version 18.2</title>
2170
2171   <para>
2172    A dump/restore is not required for those running 18.X.
2173   </para>
2174
2175   <para>
2176    However, if you have any indexes on <type>ltree</type> columns, it may
2177    be necessary to reindex them after updating.  See the sixth changelog
2178    entry below.
2179   </para>
2180  </sect2>
2181
2182  <sect2 id="release-18-2-changes">
2183   <title>Changes</title>
2184
2185   <itemizedlist>
2186
2187    <listitem>
2188<!--
2189Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
2190Branch: master [60e7ae41a] 2026-02-09 09:57:43 -0500
2191Branch: REL_18_STABLE [3b6588cd9] 2026-02-09 09:57:44 -0500
2192Branch: REL_17_STABLE [3d160401b] 2026-02-09 09:57:44 -0500
2193Branch: REL_16_STABLE [595956fc7] 2026-02-09 09:57:44 -0500
2194Branch: REL_15_STABLE [429aeaebd] 2026-02-09 09:57:44 -0500
2195Branch: REL_14_STABLE [b39d38139] 2026-02-09 09:57:44 -0500
2196-->
2197     <para>
2198      Guard against unexpected dimensions
2199      of <type>oidvector</type>/<type>int2vector</type> (Tom Lane)
2200      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3b6588cd9">&sect;</ulink>
2201     </para>
2202
2203     <para>
2204      These data types are expected to be 1-dimensional arrays containing
2205      no nulls, but there are cast pathways that permit violating those
2206      expectations.  Add checks to some functions that were depending on
2207      those expectations without verifying them, and could misbehave in
2208      consequence.
2209     </para>
2210
2211     <para>
2212      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
2213      Altan Birler for reporting this problem.
2214      (CVE-2026-2003)
2215     </para>
2216    </listitem>
2217
2218    <listitem>
2219<!--
2220Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
2221Branch: master [841d42cc4] 2026-02-09 10:07:31 -0500
2222Branch: REL_18_STABLE [66ddac698] 2026-02-09 10:07:31 -0500
2223Branch: REL_17_STABLE [bbf5bcf58] 2026-02-09 10:07:31 -0500
2224Branch: REL_16_STABLE [91d7c0bfd] 2026-02-09 10:07:31 -0500
2225Branch: REL_15_STABLE [b764b26f2] 2026-02-09 10:07:31 -0500
2226Branch: REL_14_STABLE [ea3bf3498] 2026-02-09 10:07:31 -0500
2227Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
2228Branch: master [8ebdf41c2] 2026-02-09 10:14:22 -0500
2229Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b69af3dda] 2026-02-09 10:14:22 -0500
2230Branch: REL_17_STABLE [dd3ad2a4d] 2026-02-09 10:14:22 -0500
2231Branch: REL_16_STABLE [c0887b39d] 2026-02-09 10:14:22 -0500
2232Branch: REL_15_STABLE [deb464a40] 2026-02-09 10:14:22 -0500
2233Branch: REL_14_STABLE [7e82d9a04] 2026-02-09 10:14:22 -0500
2234Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
2235Branch: REL_17_STABLE [dbb09fd8e] 2026-02-09 10:02:23 -0500
2236Branch: REL_16_STABLE [d484bc260] 2026-02-09 10:02:23 -0500
2237Branch: REL_15_STABLE [3ecc84cce] 2026-02-09 10:02:23 -0500
2238Branch: REL_14_STABLE [9fa38c572] 2026-02-09 10:02:23 -0500
2239-->
2240     <para>
2241      Harden selectivity estimators against being attached to operators
2242      that accept unexpected data types (Tom Lane)
2243      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;66ddac698">&sect;</ulink>
2244      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b69af3dda">&sect;</ulink>
2245     </para>
2246
2247     <para>
2248      <filename>contrib/intarray</filename> contained a selectivity
2249      estimation function that could be abused for arbitrary code
2250      execution, because it did not check that its input was of the
2251      expected data type.  Third-party extensions should check for similar
2252      hazards and add defenses using the technique intarray now uses.
2253      Since such extension fixes will take time, we now require superuser
2254      privilege to attach a non-built-in selectivity estimator to an
2255      operator.
2256     </para>
2257
2258     <para>
2259      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
2260      Daniel Firer, as part of zeroday.cloud, for reporting this problem.
2261      (CVE-2026-2004)
2262     </para>
2263    </listitem>
2264
2265    <listitem>
2266<!--
2267Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
2268Branch: master [379695d3c] 2026-02-09 08:00:59 +0900
2269Branch: REL_18_STABLE [209f387b8] 2026-02-09 08:01:05 +0900
2270Branch: REL_17_STABLE [7a7d9693c] 2026-02-09 08:01:07 +0900
2271Branch: REL_16_STABLE [527b730f4] 2026-02-09 08:01:09 +0900
2272Branch: REL_15_STABLE [9a9982ec6] 2026-02-09 08:01:10 +0900
2273Branch: REL_14_STABLE [01de2e32d] 2026-02-09 08:01:12 +0900
2274-->
2275     <para>
2276      Fix buffer overrun in <filename>contrib/pgcrypto</filename>'s
2277      PGP decryption functions (Michael Paquier)
2278      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;209f387b8">&sect;</ulink>
2279     </para>
2280
2281     <para>
2282      Decrypting a crafted message with an overlength session key caused a
2283      buffer overrun, with consequences as bad as arbitrary code
2284      execution.
2285     </para>
2286
2287     <para>
2288      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
2289      Team Xint Code, as part of zeroday.cloud, for reporting this problem.
2290      (CVE-2026-2005)
2291     </para>
2292    </listitem>
2293
2294    <listitem>
2295<!--
2296Author: Thomas Munro <tmunro@postgresql.org>
2297Branch: master [af79c30dc] 2026-02-09 12:08:58 +1300
2298Branch: REL_18_STABLE [df0852fe0] 2026-02-09 12:12:29 +1300
2299Branch: REL_17_STABLE [838248b1b] 2026-02-09 12:23:45 +1300
2300Branch: REL_16_STABLE [70ff9ede5] 2026-02-09 12:28:01 +1300
2301Branch: REL_15_STABLE [b2c81ac86] 2026-02-09 12:34:12 +1300
2302Branch: REL_14_STABLE [2a53db21e] 2026-02-09 12:38:07 +1300
2303Branch: master [74ee636cc] 2026-02-09 12:08:58 +1300
2304Branch: REL_18_STABLE [efef05ba9] 2026-02-09 12:12:33 +1300
2305Branch: REL_17_STABLE [7a522039f] 2026-02-09 12:23:51 +1300
2306Branch: REL_16_STABLE [b0e3f5cf9] 2026-02-09 12:28:07 +1300
2307Branch: REL_15_STABLE [50863be0b] 2026-02-09 12:34:17 +1300
2308Branch: REL_14_STABLE [6ed116046] 2026-02-09 12:38:12 +1300
2309Branch: master [1e7fe06c1] 2026-02-09 12:44:04 +1300
2310Branch: REL_18_STABLE [7b5fc85be] 2026-02-09 12:43:42 +1300
2311Branch: REL_17_STABLE [319e8a644] 2026-02-09 12:42:47 +1300
2312Branch: REL_16_STABLE [d837fb029] 2026-02-09 12:29:15 +1300
2313Branch: REL_15_STABLE [fd82ddb67] 2026-02-09 12:34:24 +1300
2314Branch: REL_14_STABLE [cecedb912] 2026-02-09 12:39:01 +1300
2315Branch: master [c67bef3f3] 2026-02-09 12:44:12 +1300
2316Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b0f5d25bc] 2026-02-09 12:43:50 +1300
2317Branch: REL_17_STABLE [10ebc4bd6] 2026-02-09 12:42:59 +1300
2318Branch: REL_16_STABLE [4c08960d9] 2026-02-09 12:29:41 +1300
2319Branch: REL_15_STABLE [757bf8145] 2026-02-09 12:35:19 +1300
2320Branch: REL_14_STABLE [e7591254c] 2026-02-09 12:39:16 +1300
2321Author: Noah Misch <noah@leadboat.com>
2322Branch: master [d536aee55] 2026-02-09 06:14:47 -0800
2323Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b42709194] 2026-02-09 06:14:50 -0800
2324Branch: REL_17_STABLE [dc072a09a] 2026-02-09 06:14:51 -0800
2325Branch: REL_16_STABLE [0c33d5608] 2026-02-09 06:14:51 -0800
2326Branch: REL_15_STABLE [8f8b1ffac] 2026-02-09 06:14:52 -0800
2327Branch: REL_14_STABLE [8373ed094] 2026-02-09 06:14:52 -0800
2328Branch: master [c5dc75479] 2026-02-09 09:08:10 -0800
2329Branch: REL_18_STABLE [4543b02af] 2026-02-09 09:08:13 -0800
2330Branch: REL_17_STABLE [955433ebd] 2026-02-09 09:08:13 -0800
2331Branch: REL_16_STABLE [763671b74] 2026-02-09 09:08:13 -0800
2332Branch: REL_15_STABLE [6f741bcb6] 2026-02-09 09:08:14 -0800
2333Branch: REL_14_STABLE [5301b2b7d] 2026-02-09 09:08:14 -0800
2334-->
2335     <para>
2336      Fix inadequate validation of multibyte character lengths
2337      (Thomas Munro, Noah Misch)
2338      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;df0852fe0">&sect;</ulink>
2339      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;efef05ba9">&sect;</ulink>
2340      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7b5fc85be">&sect;</ulink>
2341      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b0f5d25bc">&sect;</ulink>
2342      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b42709194">&sect;</ulink>
2343      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4543b02af">&sect;</ulink>
2344     </para>
2345
2346     <para>
2347      Assorted bugs allowed an attacker able to issue crafted SQL to
2348      overrun string buffers, with consequences as bad as arbitrary code
2349      execution.  After these fixes, applications may
2350      observe <quote>invalid byte sequence for encoding</quote> errors
2351      when string functions process invalid text that has been stored in
2352      the database.
2353     </para>
2354
2355     <para>
2356      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks Paul Gerste
2357      and Moritz Sanft, as part of zeroday.cloud, for reporting this
2358      problem.
2359      (CVE-2026-2006)
2360     </para>
2361    </listitem>
2362
2363    <listitem>
2364<!--
2365Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
2366Branch: master [00896ddaf] 2026-02-09 12:08:58 +1300
2367Branch: REL_18_STABLE [e0965fb1a] 2026-02-09 12:12:24 +1300
2368Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
2369Branch: master [54598670f] 2026-02-09 12:08:58 +1300
2370Branch: REL_18_STABLE [18548681d] 2026-02-09 12:12:18 +1300
2371-->
2372     <para>
2373      Harden <filename>contrib/pg_trgm</filename> against changes in
2374      string lowercasing behavior (Heikki Linnakangas)
2375      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e0965fb1a">&sect;</ulink>
2376      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;18548681d">&sect;</ulink>
2377     </para>
2378
2379     <para>
2380      Fix potential buffer overruns arising from the fact that in some
2381      locales lower-casing a string can produce more characters (not
2382      bytes) than were in the original.  That behavior is new in version
2383      18, and so is the bug.
2384     </para>
2385
2386     <para>
2387      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
2388      Heikki Linnakangas for reporting this problem.
2389      (CVE-2026-2007)
2390     </para>
2391    </listitem>
2392
2393    <listitem>
2394<!--
2395Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
2396Branch: master [7f007e4a0] 2025-12-16 12:57:00 -0800
2397Branch: REL_18_STABLE [806555e30] 2025-12-16 12:57:12 -0800
2398Branch: REL_18_STABLE [8993bf099] 2026-01-27 08:16:07 -0800
2399-->
2400     <para>
2401      Fix inconsistent case-insensitive matching
2402      in <filename>contrib/ltree</filename> (Jeff Davis)
2403      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;806555e30">&sect;</ulink>
2404      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8993bf099">&sect;</ulink>
2405     </para>
2406
2407     <para>
2408      Index-related routines in <filename>ltree</filename> used a
2409      different implementation of case-folding than the primary operators
2410      did.  Their behavior was equivalent only if the default collation
2411      provider was libc and the encoding was single-byte.
2412     </para>
2413
2414     <para>
2415      To fix, change the code to use case-folding with the database's
2416      default collation.
2417      This change will require reindexing indexes on <type>ltree</type>
2418      columns (regardless of the index access method) unless the database
2419      uses libc as collation provider and its encoding is single-byte.
2420      Without that, searches of such indexes will fail to locate relevant
2421      entries.
2422     </para>
2423    </listitem>
2424
2425    <listitem>
2426<!--
2427Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@kurilemu.de>
2428Branch: master [96e2af605] 2026-02-03 12:33:29 +0100
2429Branch: REL_18_STABLE [492a69e14] 2026-02-03 12:33:29 +0100
2430-->
2431     <para>
2432      When using <command>ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT</command> to add
2433      a not-null constraint with an explicit name, if the column is
2434      already marked NOT NULL, require that the provided name match the
2435      existing constraint name (Álvaro Herrera, Srinath Reddy Sadipiralla)
2436      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;492a69e14">&sect;</ulink>
2437     </para>
2438    </listitem>
2439
2440    <listitem>
2441<!--
2442Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
2443Branch: master [35b5c62c3] 2025-11-18 12:56:55 -0500
2444Branch: REL_18_STABLE [12bc32917] 2025-11-18 12:56:55 -0500
2445Branch: REL_17_STABLE [075a763e2] 2025-11-18 12:56:55 -0500
2446Branch: REL_16_STABLE [1c8c3206f] 2025-11-18 12:56:55 -0500
2447Branch: REL_15_STABLE [9f5a58aac] 2025-11-18 12:56:55 -0500
2448Branch: REL_14_STABLE [b85397481] 2025-11-18 12:56:55 -0500
2449-->
2450     <para>
2451      Don't allow CTE references in sub-selects to determine semantic
2452      levels of aggregate functions (Tom Lane)
2453      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;12bc32917">&sect;</ulink>
2454     </para>
2455
2456     <para>
2457      This change undoes a change made two minor releases ago, instead
2458      throwing an error if a sub-select references a CTE that's below the
2459      semantic level that standard SQL rules would assign to the aggregate
2460      based on contained column references and aggregates.  The attempted
2461      fix turned out to cause problems of its own, and it's unclear what
2462      to do instead.  Since sub-selects within aggregates are disallowed
2463      altogether by the SQL standard, treating such cases as errors seems
2464      sufficient.
2465     </para>
2466    </listitem>
2467
2468    <listitem>
2469<!--
2470Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
2471Branch: master [b4307ae2e] 2026-01-24 11:30:48 +0000
2472Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c6ce4dcf9] 2026-01-24 11:30:48 +0000
2473Branch: REL_17_STABLE [c5fc17dda] 2026-01-24 11:30:49 +0000
2474Branch: REL_16_STABLE [e7391bbf1] 2026-01-24 11:30:50 +0000
2475Branch: REL_15_STABLE [c5824536e] 2026-01-24 11:30:51 +0000
2476-->
2477     <para>
2478      Fix trigger transition table capture for <command>MERGE</command>
2479      in CTE queries (Dean Rasheed)
2480      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c6ce4dcf9">&sect;</ulink>
2481     </para>
2482
2483     <para>
2484      When executing a data-modifying CTE query containing both
2485      a <command>MERGE</command> and another DML operation on a table with
2486      statement-level <literal>AFTER</literal> triggers, the transition
2487      tables passed to the triggers would not include the rows affected by
2488      the <command>MERGE</command>, only those affected by the other
2489      operation(s).
2490     </para>
2491    </listitem>
2492
2493    <listitem>
2494<!--
2495Author: Amit Langote <amitlan@postgresql.org>
2496Branch: master [889676a0d] 2026-01-16 14:53:50 +0900
2497Branch: REL_18_STABLE [f335457e8] 2026-01-16 14:53:32 +0900
2498-->
2499     <para>
2500      Fix incorrect pruning of rowmarks belonging to non-relation
2501      rangetable entries, such as subqueries (Dean Rasheed)
2502      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f335457e8">&sect;</ulink>
2503     </para>
2504
2505     <para>
2506      This led to incorrect results if a proposed row update needed to be
2507      modified by EvalPlanQual rechecking, as could happen if there was a
2508      concurrent update to that row.
2509     </para>
2510    </listitem>
2511
2512    <listitem>
2513<!--
2514Author: Amit Langote <amitlan@postgresql.org>
2515Branch: master [f9a468c66] 2026-01-23 10:23:30 +0900
2516Branch: REL_18_STABLE [9f4b7bfc5] 2026-01-23 10:23:26 +0900
2517Branch: REL_17_STABLE [933f67fb6] 2026-01-23 10:23:22 +0900
2518Branch: REL_16_STABLE [fab386f74] 2026-01-23 10:23:18 +0900
2519Branch: REL_15_STABLE [687533b39] 2026-01-23 10:23:14 +0900
2520Branch: REL_14_STABLE [6d2fa44d2] 2026-01-23 10:23:10 +0900
2521-->
2522     <para>
2523      Fix failure when all children of a partitioned target table
2524      of an update or delete have been pruned (Amit Langote)
2525      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9f4b7bfc5">&sect;</ulink>
2526     </para>
2527
2528     <para>
2529      In such cases, the executor could report <quote>could not find junk
2530      ctid column</quote> errors, even though nothing needs to be done.
2531     </para>
2532    </listitem>
2533
2534    <listitem>
2535<!--
2536Author: Andres Freund <andres@anarazel.de>
2537Branch: master [75609fded] 2026-01-06 19:51:10 -0500
2538Branch: REL_18_STABLE [bdc5dedfc] 2026-01-06 19:51:19 -0500
2539-->
2540     <para>
2541      Fix expression evaluation bug for a sub-select within an array
2542      subscript (Andres Freund)
2543      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bdc5dedfc">&sect;</ulink>
2544     </para>
2545    </listitem>
2546
2547    <listitem>
2548<!--
2549Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
2550Branch: master [6dfce8420] 2025-12-05 20:10:33 -0500
2551Branch: REL_18_STABLE [18b349315] 2025-12-05 20:10:33 -0500
2552-->
2553     <para>
2554      Fix text substring search for non-deterministic collations
2555      (Laurenz Albe)
2556      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;18b349315">&sect;</ulink>
2557     </para>
2558
2559     <para>
2560      When using a non-deterministic collation, we failed to detect a
2561      match occurring at the very end of the searched string.
2562     </para>
2563    </listitem>
2564
2565    <listitem>
2566<!--
2567Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
2568Branch: master [7027dd499] 2026-01-26 23:27:15 +1300
2569Branch: REL_18_STABLE [ccde5be68] 2026-01-26 23:46:23 +1300
2570Branch: REL_17_STABLE [cae812741] 2026-01-26 23:47:07 +1300
2571Branch: REL_16_STABLE [4297a3519] 2026-01-26 23:47:37 +1300
2572-->
2573     <para>
2574      Avoid possible planner failure when a query contains duplicate
2575      window function calls (Meng Zhang, David Rowley)
2576      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ccde5be68">&sect;</ulink>
2577     </para>
2578
2579     <para>
2580      Confusion over de-duplication of such calls could result in errors
2581      like <quote>WindowFunc with winref 2 assigned to WindowAgg with
2582      winref 1</quote>.
2583     </para>
2584    </listitem>
2585
2586    <listitem>
2587<!--
2588Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
2589Branch: master [325808cac] 2025-12-25 12:12:52 +0900
2590Branch: REL_18_STABLE [382ce9cb7] 2025-12-25 12:15:09 +0900
2591-->
2592     <para>
2593      Fix planner error with set-returning functions and grouping sets
2594      (Richard Guo)
2595      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;382ce9cb7">&sect;</ulink>
2596     </para>
2597
2598     <para>
2599      When constructing a ProjectSet plan node, the planner failed to
2600      detect that subexpressions involving grouping expressions were
2601      already computed by the input plan.  This led to inefficient plans
2602      or errors such as <quote>variable not found in subplan target
2603      list</quote>.
2604     </para>
2605    </listitem>
2606
2607    <listitem>
2608<!--
2609Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
2610Branch: master [34740b90b] 2026-01-19 11:13:23 +0900
2611Branch: REL_18_STABLE [7650eabb6] 2026-01-19 11:14:51 +0900
2612-->
2613     <para>
2614      Avoid incorrect optimization when a subquery's grouping clause
2615      contains a volatile or set-returning function (Richard Guo)
2616      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7650eabb6">&sect;</ulink>
2617     </para>
2618
2619     <para>
2620      The planner was willing to push down outer-query restrictions
2621      referencing such a grouping column, leading to incorrect behavior
2622      due to multiple evaluation of a volatile function, or errors caused
2623      by introduction of a set-returning function into the
2624      subquery's <literal>WHERE</literal>/<literal>HAVING</literal>
2625      clauses.
2626     </para>
2627    </listitem>
2628
2629    <listitem>
2630<!--
2631Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
2632Branch: master [559f9e90d] 2025-12-29 11:40:45 +0900
2633Branch: REL_18_STABLE [7e9f852a7] 2025-12-29 11:44:14 +0900
2634-->
2635     <para>
2636      Look through PlaceHolderVar nodes when searching for statistics
2637      about an expression (Richard Guo)
2638      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7e9f852a7">&sect;</ulink>
2639     </para>
2640
2641     <para>
2642      This change allows the planner to find relevant statistics about
2643      expressions pulled up from subqueries or used in <literal>GROUP
2644      BY</literal>, avoiding falling back to a default estimate.
2645      (Arguably we should adjust any found statistics to account for an
2646      increased probability of the value being NULL, but we've never done
2647      the equivalent thing for plain Vars either.)  While this restriction
2648      is old, changes in <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> version 18
2649      made PlaceHolderVars more common than before, so make the change to
2650      avoid plan regressions in affected cases.
2651     </para>
2652    </listitem>
2653
2654    <listitem>
2655<!--
2656Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
2657Branch: master [ad66f705f] 2025-12-29 11:38:49 +0900
2658Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b4cf74420] 2025-12-29 11:43:06 +0900
2659-->
2660     <para>
2661      Look through no-op PlaceHolderVar nodes when matching expressions to
2662      indexes (Richard Guo)
2663      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b4cf74420">&sect;</ulink>
2664     </para>
2665
2666     <para>
2667      Because <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> version 18 uses
2668      PlaceHolderVars in more cases than before, some queries that
2669      formerly could use an index failed to do so.  Add logic to prevent
2670      that regression.
2671     </para>
2672    </listitem>
2673
2674    <listitem>
2675<!--
2676Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
2677Branch: master [ed931377a] 2025-11-17 13:54:52 -0500
2678Branch: REL_18_STABLE [bf5b13a8a] 2025-11-17 13:55:26 -0500
2679-->
2680     <para>
2681      Fix planner's conversion of <literal>OR</literal> clauses to
2682      ScalarArrayOp index conditions (Tender Wang, Tom Lane)
2683      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bf5b13a8a">&sect;</ulink>
2684     </para>
2685
2686     <para>
2687      The code did not handle RelabelType nodes correctly, and could
2688      generate invalid expressions or fail to perform a valid conversion.
2689     </para>
2690    </listitem>
2691
2692    <listitem>
2693<!--
2694Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
2695Branch: master [5528e8d10] 2025-11-27 13:09:59 -0500
2696Branch: REL_18_STABLE [a212877dc] 2025-11-27 13:09:59 -0500
2697Branch: REL_17_STABLE [e79b27662] 2025-11-27 13:09:59 -0500
2698Branch: REL_16_STABLE [b497766a8] 2025-11-27 13:09:59 -0500
2699Branch: REL_15_STABLE [f19502f04] 2025-11-27 13:09:59 -0500
2700Branch: REL_14_STABLE [9e77323fc] 2025-11-27 13:09:59 -0500
2701-->
2702     <para>
2703      Allow indexscans on partial hash indexes even when the index's
2704      predicate implies the truth of the WHERE clause (Tom Lane)
2705      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a212877dc">&sect;</ulink>
2706     </para>
2707
2708     <para>
2709      Normally we drop a WHERE clause that is implied by the predicate,
2710      since it's pointless to test it; it must hold for every index
2711      entry.  However that can prevent creation of an indexscan plan if
2712      the index is one that requires a WHERE clause on the leading index
2713      key, as hash indexes do.  Don't drop implied clauses when
2714      considering such an index.
2715     </para>
2716    </listitem>
2717
2718    <listitem>
2719<!--
2720Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
2721Branch: master [951b60f7a] 2025-12-18 15:08:48 +0200
2722Branch: REL_18_STABLE [d77a5f981] 2025-12-18 15:09:26 +0200
2723Branch: REL_17_STABLE [4b6d096a0] 2025-12-18 15:09:23 +0200
2724Branch: REL_16_STABLE [a5277700e] 2025-12-18 15:09:21 +0200
2725Branch: REL_15_STABLE [460d97020] 2025-12-18 15:09:19 +0200
2726Branch: REL_14_STABLE [ab4256869] 2025-12-18 15:09:17 +0200
2727-->
2728     <para>
2729      Do not emit WAL for unlogged BRIN indexes (Kirill Reshke)
2730      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d77a5f981">&sect;</ulink>
2731     </para>
2732
2733     <para>
2734      One seldom-taken code path incorrectly emitted a WAL record
2735      relating to a BRIN index even if the index was marked unlogged.
2736      Crash recovery would then fail to replay that record, complaining
2737      that the file already exists.
2738     </para>
2739    </listitem>
2740
2741    <listitem>
2742<!--
2743Author: Tomas Vondra <tomas.vondra@postgresql.org>
2744Branch: master [db14dcdec] 2026-01-26 20:05:17 +0100
2745Branch: REL_18_STABLE [eee71a66c] 2026-01-26 20:05:00 +0100
2746-->
2747     <para>
2748      Use the correct ordering function in parallel GIN index builds
2749      (Tomas Vondra)
2750      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;eee71a66c">&sect;</ulink>
2751     </para>
2752
2753     <para>
2754      The parallel code used the default ordering operator (which is
2755      determined by the column data type's btree opclass), whereas it
2756      should use the ordering function specified by the GIN opclass, if
2757      any.  This led to a failure if the data type has no btree opclass,
2758      or to an invalid index if the opclass specifies an ordering function
2759      that doesn't agree with the btree opclass.
2760     </para>
2761    </listitem>
2762
2763    <listitem>
2764<!--
2765Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
2766Branch: master [8eeb4a0f7] 2025-11-12 20:59:36 +0200
2767Branch: REL_18_STABLE [321ec5462] 2025-11-12 21:00:42 +0200
2768Branch: REL_17_STABLE [d02c03ddc] 2025-11-12 21:01:13 +0200
2769Branch: REL_16_STABLE [053e1868b] 2025-11-12 21:01:42 +0200
2770Branch: REL_15_STABLE [1a469d7b5] 2025-11-12 21:02:12 +0200
2771Branch: REL_14_STABLE [eba917d36] 2025-11-12 21:02:36 +0200
2772Branch: master [797e9ea6e] 2025-11-12 20:59:44 +0200
2773Branch: REL_18_STABLE [7b069a187] 2025-11-12 21:00:46 +0200
2774Branch: REL_17_STABLE [c2682810a] 2025-11-12 21:01:16 +0200
2775Branch: REL_16_STABLE [44e8c60be] 2025-11-12 21:01:45 +0200
2776Branch: REL_15_STABLE [0c862646c] 2025-11-12 21:02:14 +0200
2777Branch: REL_14_STABLE [c2e58c071] 2025-11-12 21:02:38 +0200
2778Branch: master [0bdc777e8] 2025-11-12 21:19:03 +0200
2779Branch: REL_18_STABLE [82fa6b78d] 2025-11-12 21:26:58 +0200
2780Branch: REL_17_STABLE [d80d5f099] 2025-11-12 21:27:02 +0200
2781Branch: REL_16_STABLE [0e8eaa218] 2025-11-12 21:27:05 +0200
2782Branch: REL_15_STABLE [21a9014cf] 2025-11-12 21:27:07 +0200
2783Branch: REL_14_STABLE [84f1bf4af] 2025-11-12 21:27:11 +0200
2784-->
2785     <para>
2786      Prevent truncation of CLOG that is still needed by
2787      unread <command>NOTIFY</command> messages (Joel Jacobson, Heikki
2788      Linnakangas)
2789      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;321ec5462">&sect;</ulink>
2790      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7b069a187">&sect;</ulink>
2791      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;82fa6b78d">&sect;</ulink>
2792     </para>
2793
2794     <para>
2795      This fix prevents <quote>could not access status of
2796      transaction</quote> errors when a backend is slow to
2797      absorb <command>NOTIFY</command> messages.
2798     </para>
2799    </listitem>
2800
2801    <listitem>
2802<!--
2803Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
2804Branch: master [1b4699090] 2025-11-12 20:59:28 +0200
2805Branch: REL_18_STABLE [aab4a84bb] 2025-11-12 21:00:25 +0200
2806Branch: REL_17_STABLE [b821c9292] 2025-11-12 21:01:08 +0200
2807Branch: REL_16_STABLE [c1a5bde00] 2025-11-12 21:01:39 +0200
2808Branch: REL_15_STABLE [b1da37de2] 2025-11-12 21:02:07 +0200
2809Branch: REL_14_STABLE [7cb05dd2d] 2025-11-12 21:02:32 +0200
2810-->
2811     <para>
2812      Escalate errors occurring during <command>NOTIFY</command> message
2813      processing to FATAL, i.e. close the connection (Heikki Linnakangas)
2814      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;aab4a84bb">&sect;</ulink>
2815     </para>
2816
2817     <para>
2818      Formerly, if a backend got an error while absorbing
2819      a <command>NOTIFY</command> message, it would advance past that
2820      message, report the error to the client, and move on.  That behavior
2821      was fraught with problems though.  One big concern is that the
2822      client has no good way to know that a notification was lost, and
2823      certainly no way to know what was in it.  Depending on the
2824      application logic, missing a notification could cause the
2825      application to get stuck waiting, for example.  Also, any remaining
2826      messages would not get processed until someone sent a
2827      new <command>NOTIFY</command>.
2828     </para>
2829
2830     <para>
2831      Also, if the connection is idle at the time of receiving
2832      a <command>NOTIFY</command> signal, any ERROR would be escalated to
2833      FATAL anyway, due to unrelated concerns.  Therefore, we've chosen to
2834      make that happen in all cases, for consistency and to provide a
2835      clear signal to the application that it might have missed some
2836      notifications.
2837     </para>
2838    </listitem>
2839
2840    <listitem>
2841<!--
2842Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
2843Branch: master [e217dc748] 2026-01-14 08:44:12 +0900
2844Branch: REL_18_STABLE [9c3caad02] 2026-01-14 08:44:52 +0900
2845-->
2846     <para>
2847      Consider grouping expressions when computing a query ID hash
2848      (Jian He)
2849      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9c3caad02">&sect;</ulink>
2850     </para>
2851
2852     <para>
2853      Previously, two queries that were the same except in <literal>GROUP
2854      BY</literal> expressions would be merged
2855      by <filename>contrib/pg_stat_statements</filename> and other users
2856      of query IDs.
2857     </para>
2858    </listitem>
2859
2860    <listitem>
2861<!--
2862Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
2863Branch: master [1b92fe7bb] 2025-11-16 22:14:06 +0000
2864Branch: REL_18_STABLE [5749d95d4] 2025-11-16 22:15:10 +0000
2865Branch: REL_17_STABLE [d6c415c4b] 2025-11-16 22:15:45 +0000
2866-->
2867     <para>
2868      Fix erroneous counting of updates in <command>EXPLAIN ANALYZE
2869      MERGE</command> with a concurrent update (Dean Rasheed)
2870      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;5749d95d4">&sect;</ulink>
2871     </para>
2872
2873     <para>
2874      This situation led to an incorrect count of <quote>skipped</quote>
2875      tuples in <command>EXPLAIN</command>'s output, or to an assertion
2876      failure in an assert-enabled build.
2877     </para>
2878    </listitem>
2879
2880    <listitem>
2881<!--
2882Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
2883Branch: master [955f55068] 2025-12-23 13:37:16 +0200
2884Branch: REL_18_STABLE [3e3a80f62] 2025-12-23 13:37:23 +0200
2885Branch: REL_17_STABLE [bb87d7fef] 2025-12-23 13:37:25 +0200
2886Branch: REL_16_STABLE [7efef18ff] 2025-12-23 13:37:27 +0200
2887Branch: REL_15_STABLE [5d5487cd2] 2025-12-23 13:37:29 +0200
2888Branch: REL_14_STABLE [b6201c76e] 2025-12-23 13:37:31 +0200
2889-->
2890     <para>
2891      Fix bug in following update chain when locking a tuple (Jasper
2892      Smit)
2893      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3e3a80f62">&sect;</ulink>
2894     </para>
2895
2896     <para>
2897      This code path neglected to check the xmin of the first new tuple in
2898      the update chain, making it possible to lock an unrelated tuple if
2899      the original updater aborted and the space was immediately reclaimed
2900      by <command>VACUUM</command> and then re-used.
2901      That could cause unexpected transaction delays or deadlocks.
2902      Errors associated with having identified the wrong tuple have also
2903      been observed.
2904     </para>
2905    </listitem>
2906
2907    <listitem>
2908<!--
2909Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
2910Branch: master [ecd275718] 2026-01-19 12:09:32 -0500
2911Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c80b0c9d6] 2026-01-19 12:09:48 -0500
2912Branch: REL_17_STABLE [ad569b54a] 2026-01-19 12:10:00 -0500
2913-->
2914     <para>
2915      Fix incorrect handling of incremental backups of large tables
2916      (Robert Haas, Oleg Tkachenko)
2917      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c80b0c9d6">&sect;</ulink>
2918     </para>
2919
2920     <para>
2921      If a table exceeding 1GB (or in general, the installation's segment
2922      size) is truncated by <literal>VACUUM</literal> between the base
2923      backup and the incremental
2924      backup, <application>pg_combinebackup</application> could fail with
2925      an error about <quote>truncation block length in excess of segment
2926      size</quote>.  This prevented restoring the incremental backup.
2927     </para>
2928    </listitem>
2929
2930    <listitem>
2931<!--
2932Author: Amit Langote <amitlan@postgresql.org>
2933Branch: master [9cbb1d21d] 2026-01-16 13:02:42 +0900
2934Branch: REL_18_STABLE [1943ceb38] 2026-01-16 13:14:21 +0900
2935Branch: REL_17_STABLE [4071fe900] 2026-01-16 13:19:53 +0900
2936Branch: REL_16_STABLE [980b7c736] 2026-01-16 13:19:59 +0900
2937Branch: REL_15_STABLE [b926ff137] 2026-01-16 13:20:05 +0900
2938Branch: REL_14_STABLE [f6df78173] 2026-01-16 13:20:11 +0900
2939-->
2940     <para>
2941      Fix potential backend process crash at process exit due to trying to
2942      release a lock in an already-unmapped shared memory segment
2943      (Rahila Syed)
2944      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1943ceb38">&sect;</ulink>
2945     </para>
2946    </listitem>
2947
2948    <listitem>
2949<!--
2950Author: Andres Freund <andres@anarazel.de>
2951Branch: master [807764990] 2026-01-15 11:09:07 -0500
2952Branch: REL_18_STABLE [7f1b3a4ce] 2026-01-15 11:09:49 -0500
2953-->
2954     <para>
2955      Fix race condition in async I/O code (Andres Freund)
2956      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7f1b3a4ce">&sect;</ulink>
2957     </para>
2958
2959     <para>
2960      It was possible for the result code of an asynchronous I/O operation
2961      to be overwritten before it was fetched.
2962     </para>
2963    </listitem>
2964
2965    <listitem>
2966<!--
2967Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
2968Branch: master [102bdaa9b] 2026-01-15 16:48:45 +0200
2969Branch: REL_18_STABLE [09532a78b] 2026-01-15 16:49:42 +0200
2970Branch: REL_17_STABLE [d3ad4cef6] 2026-01-15 16:50:30 +0200
2971Branch: REL_16_STABLE [c7946e6f3] 2026-01-15 16:50:37 +0200
2972Branch: REL_15_STABLE [a563ac619] 2026-01-15 16:50:42 +0200
2973Branch: REL_14_STABLE [46be454ca] 2026-01-15 16:50:46 +0200
2974-->
2975     <para>
2976      Guard against incorrect truncation of the multixact log after a
2977      crash (Heikki Linnakangas)
2978      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;09532a78b">&sect;</ulink>
2979     </para>
2980    </listitem>
2981
2982    <listitem>
2983<!--
2984Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
2985Branch: master [36b8f4974] 2025-12-27 17:23:30 +0900
2986Branch: REL_18_STABLE [06907e864] 2025-12-27 17:23:51 +0900
2987Branch: REL_17_STABLE [52b27f585] 2025-12-27 17:23:53 +0900
2988Branch: REL_16_STABLE [c48829ed8] 2025-12-27 17:23:54 +0900
2989Branch: REL_15_STABLE [300575fe2] 2025-12-27 17:23:56 +0900
2990Branch: REL_14_STABLE [ea3524cff] 2025-12-27 17:23:58 +0900
2991-->
2992     <para>
2993      Fix possibly mis-encoded result
2994      of <function>pg_stat_get_backend_activity()</function> (Chao Li)
2995      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;06907e864">&sect;</ulink>
2996     </para>
2997
2998     <para>
2999      The shared-memory buffer holding a session's activity string can
3000      end with an incomplete multibyte character.  Readers are supposed
3001      to truncate off any such incomplete character, but this function
3002      failed to do so.
3003     </para>
3004    </listitem>
3005
3006    <listitem>
3007<!--
3008Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
3009Branch: master [b3ccb0a2c] 2025-12-19 12:05:37 +0900
3010Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b863d8d87] 2025-12-19 12:07:10 +0900
3011Branch: REL_17_STABLE [699293d27] 2025-12-19 12:07:21 +0900
3012Branch: REL_16_STABLE [3853f6168] 2025-12-19 12:08:20 +0900
3013Branch: REL_15_STABLE [0fc2f533a] 2025-12-19 12:09:10 +0900
3014Branch: REL_14_STABLE [1a9a49699] 2025-12-19 12:09:16 +0900
3015-->
3016     <para>
3017      Guard against recursive memory context logging (Fujii Masao)
3018      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b863d8d87">&sect;</ulink>
3019     </para>
3020
3021     <para>
3022      A constant flow of signals requesting memory context logging could
3023      cause recursive execution of the logging code, which in theory could
3024      lead to stack overflow.
3025     </para>
3026    </listitem>
3027
3028    <listitem>
3029<!--
3030Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
3031Branch: master [f1a6e622b] 2025-12-16 12:24:55 -0500
3032Branch: REL_18_STABLE [57df5ab80] 2025-12-16 10:59:05 -0500
3033Branch: REL_17_STABLE [1d0fc2499] 2025-12-16 10:59:04 -0500
3034Branch: REL_16_STABLE [12c2f843c] 2025-12-16 10:59:02 -0500
3035Branch: REL_15_STABLE [bd2726d3e] 2025-12-16 10:59:01 -0500
3036Branch: REL_14_STABLE [1d9f7fbba] 2025-12-16 10:59:00 -0500
3037-->
3038     <para>
3039      Fix memory context usage when reinitializing a parallel execution
3040      context (Jakub Wartak, Jeevan Chalke)
3041      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;57df5ab80">&sect;</ulink>
3042     </para>
3043
3044     <para>
3045      This error could result in a crash due to a subsidiary data
3046      structure having a shorter lifespan than the parallel context.
3047      The problem is not known to be reachable using only
3048      core <productname>PostgreSQL</productname>, but we have reports of
3049      trouble in extensions.
3050     </para>
3051    </listitem>
3052
3053    <listitem>
3054<!--
3055Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
3056Branch: master [789d65364] 2025-12-03 19:15:08 +0200
3057Branch: REL_18_STABLE [e46041fd9] 2025-12-03 19:15:18 +0200
3058Branch: REL_17_STABLE [8ba61bc06] 2025-12-03 19:15:21 +0200
3059Branch: REL_16_STABLE [635166913] 2025-12-03 19:15:24 +0200
3060Branch: REL_15_STABLE [8cfb174a6] 2025-12-03 19:15:26 +0200
3061Branch: REL_14_STABLE [81416e101] 2025-12-03 19:15:29 +0200
3062Branch: master [4d936c3ff] 2025-12-05 11:32:38 +0200
3063Branch: REL_18_STABLE [02ba5e3be] 2025-12-05 11:35:20 +0200
3064Branch: REL_17_STABLE [cad40cec2] 2025-12-05 11:35:44 +0200
3065Branch: REL_16_STABLE [4d689a176] 2025-12-05 11:36:36 +0200
3066Branch: REL_15_STABLE [b9a02b978] 2025-12-05 11:36:52 +0200
3067Branch: REL_14_STABLE [489695554] 2025-12-05 11:37:06 +0200
3068-->
3069     <para>
3070      Set next multixid's offset when creating a new multixid, to remove
3071      the wait loop that was needed in corner cases (Andrey Borodin)
3072      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e46041fd9">&sect;</ulink>
3073      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;02ba5e3be">&sect;</ulink>
3074     </para>
3075
3076     <para>
3077      The previous logic could get stuck waiting for an update that would
3078      never occur.
3079     </para>
3080    </listitem>
3081
3082    <listitem>
3083<!--
3084Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
3085Branch: master [3881561d7] 2025-11-29 12:28:59 +0000
3086Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b880d9a02] 2025-11-29 12:31:30 +0000
3087Branch: REL_17_STABLE [c09096503] 2025-11-29 12:32:12 +0000
3088Branch: REL_16_STABLE [4d288e33b] 2025-11-29 12:33:04 +0000
3089Branch: REL_15_STABLE [134a8ee22] 2025-11-29 12:33:35 +0000
3090Branch: REL_14_STABLE [2d5b97b8c] 2025-11-29 12:34:45 +0000
3091-->
3092     <para>
3093      Avoid rewriting data-modifying CTEs more than once (Bernice Southey,
3094      Dean Rasheed)
3095      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b880d9a02">&sect;</ulink>
3096     </para>
3097
3098     <para>
3099      Formerly, when updating an auto-updatable view or a relation with
3100      rules, if the original query had any data-modifying CTEs, the rewriter
3101      would rewrite those CTEs multiple times due to recursion.  This was
3102      inefficient and could produce false errors if a CTE included an
3103      update of an always-generated column.
3104     </para>
3105    </listitem>
3106
3107    <listitem>
3108<!--
3109Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
3110Branch: master [dbdc717ac] 2025-11-26 15:12:25 -0600
3111Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b83bcc0df] 2025-11-26 15:12:25 -0600
3112Branch: REL_17_STABLE [2fc5c5062] 2025-11-26 15:12:25 -0600
3113-->
3114     <para>
3115      Allow retrying initialization of a DSM registry entry
3116      (Nathan Bossart)
3117      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b83bcc0df">&sect;</ulink>
3118     </para>
3119
3120     <para>
3121      If we fail partway through initialization of a dynamic shared memory
3122      entry, allow the next attempt to use that entry to retry
3123      initialization.  Previously the entry was left in a
3124      permanently-failed state.
3125     </para>
3126    </listitem>
3127
3128    <listitem>
3129<!--
3130Author: Tomas Vondra <tomas.vondra@postgresql.org>
3131Branch: master [3fccbd94c] 2026-01-27 00:21:40 +0100
3132Branch: REL_18_STABLE [9796c4f56] 2026-01-26 23:37:03 +0100
3133-->
3134     <para>
3135      Avoid failure of NUMA status views when a page has been swapped out
3136      (Tomas Vondra)
3137      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9796c4f56">&sect;</ulink>
3138     </para>
3139    </listitem>
3140
3141    <listitem>
3142<!--
3143Author: Tomas Vondra <tomas.vondra@postgresql.org>
3144Branch: master [599336c64] 2025-11-20 13:26:49 +0100
3145Branch: REL_18_STABLE [482e98ac4] 2025-11-20 13:26:14 +0100
3146-->
3147     <para>
3148      Avoid <quote>operation not permitted</quote> errors when querying
3149      NUMA page status with older libnuma versions (Tomas Vondra)
3150      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;482e98ac4">&sect;</ulink>
3151     </para>
3152    </listitem>
3153
3154    <listitem>
3155<!--
3156Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
3157Branch: master [dc7c77f82] 2025-12-16 13:29:28 +0900
3158Branch: REL_18_STABLE [68ebdf2b0] 2025-12-16 13:29:36 +0900
3159Branch: REL_17_STABLE [f5927da4f] 2025-12-16 13:29:39 +0900
3160Branch: REL_16_STABLE [1aa57e9ed] 2025-12-16 13:29:41 +0900
3161Branch: REL_15_STABLE [95ef6f490] 2025-12-16 13:29:42 +0900
3162Branch: REL_14_STABLE [14161bca4] 2025-12-16 13:29:44 +0900
3163-->
3164     <para>
3165      Fail recovery if WAL does not exist back to the redo point indicated
3166      by the checkpoint record (Nitin Jadhav)
3167      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;68ebdf2b0">&sect;</ulink>
3168     </para>
3169
3170     <para>
3171      Add an explicit check for this before starting recovery, so that no
3172      harm is done and a useful error message is provided.  Previously,
3173      recovery might crash or corrupt the database in this situation.
3174     </para>
3175    </listitem>
3176
3177    <listitem>
3178<!--
3179Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
3180Branch: master [6ca8506ea] 2026-01-06 17:29:12 +1300
3181Branch: REL_18_STABLE [bea57a6b4] 2026-01-06 17:29:44 +1300
3182Branch: REL_17_STABLE [bb08ac7ac] 2026-01-06 17:30:08 +1300
3183Branch: REL_16_STABLE [0687a6eb0] 2026-01-06 17:30:32 +1300
3184Branch: REL_15_STABLE [f20b79059] 2026-01-06 17:30:57 +1300
3185Branch: REL_14_STABLE [ae45c7296] 2026-01-06 17:31:20 +1300
3186-->
3187     <para>
3188      Avoid scribbling on the source query tree during <command>ALTER
3189      PUBLICATION</command> (Sunil S)
3190      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bea57a6b4">&sect;</ulink>
3191     </para>
3192
3193     <para>
3194      This error had the visible effect that an event trigger fired for
3195      the query would see only the first <literal>publish</literal>
3196      option, even if several had been specified.  If such a query were
3197      set up as a prepared statement, re-executions would misbehave too.
3198     </para>
3199    </listitem>
3200
3201    <listitem>
3202<!--
3203Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
3204Branch: master [d926462d8] 2026-01-06 11:52:22 +0900
3205Branch: REL_18_STABLE [797fc5d1b] 2026-01-06 11:54:09 +0900
3206Branch: REL_17_STABLE [7a990e801] 2026-01-06 11:54:14 +0900
3207Branch: REL_16_STABLE [75f3428f2] 2026-01-06 11:54:46 +0900
3208Branch: REL_15_STABLE [f7eb44e0f] 2026-01-06 11:55:46 +0900
3209-->
3210     <para>
3211      Pass connection options specified in <literal>CREATE SUBSCRIPTION
3212      ... CONNECTION</literal> to the publisher's walsender (Fujii Masao)
3213      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;797fc5d1b">&sect;</ulink>
3214     </para>
3215
3216     <para>
3217      Before this fix, the <literal>options</literal> connection option
3218      (if any) was ignored, thus for example preventing setting custom
3219      server parameter values in the walsender session.  It was intended
3220      for that to work, and it did work before refactoring
3221      in <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> version 15 broke it, so
3222      restore the previous behavior.
3223     </para>
3224    </listitem>
3225
3226    <listitem>
3227<!--
3228Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
3229Branch: master [851f6649c] 2026-01-27 05:06:29 +0000
3230Branch: REL_18_STABLE [919c9fa13] 2026-01-27 05:45:25 +0000
3231Branch: REL_17_STABLE [3243c0177] 2026-01-27 05:49:23 +0000
3232Branch: master [3a98f989e] 2026-01-29 03:22:02 +0000
3233Branch: REL_18_STABLE [1c60f7236] 2026-01-29 03:34:55 +0000
3234Branch: REL_17_STABLE [9649f1adf] 2026-01-29 02:57:02 +0000
3235Branch: master [006dd4b2e] 2025-12-08 05:21:22 +0000
3236Branch: REL_18_STABLE [d3ceb2084] 2025-12-08 05:33:14 +0000
3237Branch: REL_17_STABLE [3510ebeb0] 2026-01-08 07:17:56 +0000
3238Branch: REL_16_STABLE [24cce33c3] 2026-01-08 07:07:23 +0000
3239Branch: REL_15_STABLE [aae05622a] 2026-01-08 06:54:52 +0000
3240Branch: REL_14_STABLE [7406df605] 2026-01-08 06:44:28 +0000
3241-->
3242     <para>
3243      Prevent invalidation of newly created or newly synced replication
3244      slots (Zhijie Hou)
3245      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;919c9fa13">&sect;</ulink>
3246      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1c60f7236">&sect;</ulink>
3247      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d3ceb2084">&sect;</ulink>
3248     </para>
3249
3250     <para>
3251      A race condition with a concurrent checkpoint could allow WAL to be
3252      removed that is needed by the replication slot, causing the slot to
3253      immediately get marked invalid.
3254     </para>
3255    </listitem>
3256
3257    <listitem>
3258<!--
3259Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
3260Branch: master [2a5225b99] 2025-12-30 10:56:30 -0800
3261Branch: REL_18_STABLE [fd7c86cfa] 2025-12-30 10:56:28 -0800
3262Branch: REL_17_STABLE [123b851ab] 2025-12-30 10:56:25 -0800
3263Branch: REL_16_STABLE [821466722] 2025-12-30 10:56:23 -0800
3264Branch: REL_15_STABLE [fa557d300] 2025-12-30 10:56:21 -0800
3265Branch: REL_14_STABLE [57048d6e1] 2025-12-30 10:56:18 -0800
3266-->
3267     <para>
3268      Fix race condition in computing a replication slot's required xmin
3269      (Zhijie Hou)
3270      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;fd7c86cfa">&sect;</ulink>
3271     </para>
3272
3273     <para>
3274      This could lead to the error <quote>cannot build an initial slot
3275      snapshot as oldest safe xid follows snapshot's xmin</quote>.
3276     </para>
3277    </listitem>
3278
3279    <listitem>
3280<!--
3281Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
3282Branch: master [8e0d32a4a] 2025-12-23 14:32:14 +0900
3283Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b07c32619] 2025-12-23 14:32:19 +0900
3284Branch: REL_17_STABLE [e063ccc72] 2025-12-23 14:32:21 +0900
3285Branch: REL_16_STABLE [e22e9ab0c] 2025-12-23 14:32:22 +0900
3286Branch: REL_15_STABLE [90d1beef6] 2025-12-23 14:32:24 +0900
3287Branch: REL_14_STABLE [25a04aa52] 2025-12-23 14:32:25 +0900
3288-->
3289     <para>
3290      During initial synchronization of a logical replication
3291      subscription, commit the addition of
3292      a <structname>pg_replication_origin</structname> entry before
3293      starting to copy data (Zhijie Hou)
3294      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b07c32619">&sect;</ulink>
3295     </para>
3296
3297     <para>
3298      Previously, if the copy step failed, the
3299      new <structname>pg_replication_origin</structname> entry would be
3300      lost due to transaction rollback.  This led to inconsistent state in
3301      shared memory.
3302     </para>
3303    </listitem>
3304
3305    <listitem>
3306<!--
3307Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
3308Branch: master [1528b0d89] 2025-12-24 04:36:39 +0000
3309Branch: REL_18_STABLE [2f7ffe124] 2025-12-24 04:21:43 +0000
3310Branch: REL_17_STABLE [0ed8f1afb] 2025-12-24 04:05:06 +0000
3311Branch: REL_16_STABLE [63a65adf4] 2025-12-24 04:19:57 +0000
3312-->
3313     <para>
3314      Don't advance logical replication progress after a parallel worker
3315      apply failure (Zhijie Hou)
3316      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;2f7ffe124">&sect;</ulink>
3317     </para>
3318
3319     <para>
3320      The previous behavior allowed transactions to be lost by a
3321      subscriber.
3322     </para>
3323    </listitem>
3324
3325    <listitem>
3326<!--
3327Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
3328Branch: master [04396eacd] 2025-12-09 07:25:20 +0000
3329Branch: REL_18_STABLE [6c61c69d5] 2025-12-09 07:12:37 +0000
3330Branch: REL_17_STABLE [f2818868a] 2025-12-09 07:02:08 +0000
3331-->
3332     <para>
3333      Fix logical replication slotsync worker processes to handle
3334      LOCK_TIMEOUT signals correctly (Zhijie Hou)
3335      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6c61c69d5">&sect;</ulink>
3336     </para>
3337
3338     <para>
3339      Previously, timeout signals were effectively ignored.
3340     </para>
3341    </listitem>
3342
3343    <listitem>
3344<!--
3345Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
3346Branch: master [23b25586d] 2026-01-15 21:02:49 +0200
3347Branch: REL_18_STABLE [9ed411e08] 2026-01-15 21:02:54 +0200
3348Branch: REL_17_STABLE [c3770181c] 2026-01-15 21:02:59 +0200
3349Branch: REL_16_STABLE [a2eeb04f3] 2026-01-15 21:03:12 +0200
3350Branch: REL_15_STABLE [ef8465588] 2026-01-15 21:03:16 +0200
3351Branch: REL_14_STABLE [2514f1c77] 2026-01-15 20:58:05 +0200
3352-->
3353     <para>
3354      Fix possible failure with <quote>unexpected data beyond EOF</quote>
3355      during restart of a streaming replica server (Anthonin Bonnefoy)
3356      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9ed411e08">&sect;</ulink>
3357     </para>
3358    </listitem>
3359
3360    <listitem>
3361<!--
3362Author: Amit Langote <amitlan@postgresql.org>
3363Branch: master [519fa0433] 2025-11-27 12:07:01 +0900
3364Branch: REL_18_STABLE [15ba0702c] 2025-11-27 11:59:40 +0900
3365Branch: REL_17_STABLE [b5511fed5] 2025-11-27 11:59:36 +0900
3366-->
3367     <para>
3368      Fix error reporting for SQL/JSON path type mismatches (Jian He)
3369      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;15ba0702c">&sect;</ulink>
3370     </para>
3371
3372     <para>
3373      The code could produce a <quote>cache lookup failed for type 0</quote>
3374      error instead of the intended complaint about the path expression
3375      not being of the right type.
3376     </para>
3377    </listitem>
3378
3379    <listitem>
3380<!--
3381Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
3382Branch: master [349107537] 2026-01-09 11:01:36 +1300
3383Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c35e5dd9a] 2026-01-09 11:02:59 +1300
3384Branch: REL_17_STABLE [84b787ae6] 2026-01-09 11:03:24 +1300
3385Branch: REL_16_STABLE [821c4d27b] 2026-01-09 11:03:48 +1300
3386Branch: REL_15_STABLE [3ad05640b] 2026-01-09 11:04:12 +1300
3387Branch: REL_14_STABLE [a65995947] 2026-01-09 11:04:39 +1300
3388-->
3389     <para>
3390      Fix erroneous tracking of column position when parsing partition
3391      range bounds (myzhen)
3392      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c35e5dd9a">&sect;</ulink>
3393     </para>
3394
3395     <para>
3396      This could, for example, lead to the wrong column name being cited
3397      in error messages about casting partition bound values to the
3398      column's data type.
3399     </para>
3400    </listitem>
3401
3402    <listitem>
3403<!--
3404Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
3405Branch: master [74a116a79] 2026-02-06 15:38:16 +0900
3406Branch: REL_18_STABLE [acfa422c3] 2026-02-06 15:38:21 +0900
3407Branch: REL_17_STABLE [67ad4387b] 2026-02-06 15:38:23 +0900
3408Branch: REL_16_STABLE [977a17a3e] 2026-02-06 15:38:25 +0900
3409Branch: REL_15_STABLE [4ec943f7d] 2026-02-06 15:38:27 +0900
3410Branch: REL_14_STABLE [d32e17160] 2026-02-06 15:38:29 +0900
3411Branch: master [c8ec74713] 2026-02-04 16:38:06 +0900
3412Branch: REL_18_STABLE [2ca4464b6] 2026-02-04 16:38:10 +0900
3413Branch: REL_17_STABLE [263af458e] 2026-02-04 16:38:12 +0900
3414Branch: master [a9afa021e] 2026-02-02 10:21:04 +0900
3415Branch: REL_18_STABLE [ab61f0087] 2026-02-02 10:21:07 +0900
3416Branch: REL_17_STABLE [5995135f1] 2026-02-02 10:21:10 +0900
3417Branch: master [6bca4b50d] 2026-01-18 17:24:25 +0900
3418Branch: REL_18_STABLE [69ee81932] 2026-01-18 17:24:58 +0900
3419Branch: REL_17_STABLE [05ef2371a] 2026-01-18 17:25:00 +0900
3420Branch: REL_16_STABLE [e8fd6c9fd] 2026-01-18 17:25:01 +0900
3421Branch: REL_15_STABLE [fbf8df580] 2026-01-18 17:25:03 +0900
3422Branch: REL_14_STABLE [f1dc7a5b7] 2026-01-18 17:25:04 +0900
3423Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
3424Branch: master [1653ce523] 2026-02-07 22:37:02 +0100
3425Branch: REL_18_STABLE [cff2ef984] 2026-02-07 22:53:16 +0100
3426Branch: REL_17_STABLE [5449fd261] 2026-02-07 22:53:21 +0100
3427Branch: REL_16_STABLE [a7bdbbada] 2026-02-07 22:53:25 +0100
3428Branch: REL_15_STABLE [9dcd0b3de] 2026-02-07 22:53:34 +0100
3429Branch: REL_14_STABLE [3e7bb39df] 2026-02-07 22:53:38 +0100
3430-->
3431     <para>
3432      Fix assorted minor errors in error messages (Man Zeng, Tianchen Zhang)
3433      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;acfa422c3">&sect;</ulink>
3434      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;2ca4464b6">&sect;</ulink>
3435      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ab61f0087">&sect;</ulink>
3436      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;69ee81932">&sect;</ulink>
3437      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cff2ef984">&sect;</ulink>
3438     </para>
3439
3440     <para>
3441      For example, an error report about mismatched timeline number in a
3442      backup manifest showed the starting timeline number where it meant
3443      to show the ending timeline number.
3444     </para>
3445    </listitem>
3446
3447    <listitem>
3448<!--
3449Author: Thomas Munro <tmunro@postgresql.org>
3450Branch: master [e5d99b4d9] 2026-01-22 16:03:47 +1300
3451Branch: REL_18_STABLE [f1c6b153c] 2026-01-22 16:10:58 +1300
3452Branch: REL_17_STABLE [d0bb0e5b3] 2026-01-22 16:10:28 +1300
3453Branch: REL_16_STABLE [7600dc79c] 2026-01-22 16:10:08 +1300
3454Branch: REL_15_STABLE [cbdd09ae1] 2026-01-22 16:09:29 +1300
3455Branch: REL_14_STABLE [de8081c30] 2026-01-22 16:06:06 +1300
3456-->
3457     <para>
3458      Fix failure to perform function inlining when doing JIT compilation
3459      with LLVM version 17 or later (Anthonin Bonnefoy)
3460      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f1c6b153c">&sect;</ulink>
3461     </para>
3462    </listitem>
3463
3464    <listitem>
3465<!--
3466Author: Thomas Munro <tmunro@postgresql.org>
3467Branch: master [0dceba21d] 2025-11-22 21:21:11 +1300
3468Branch: REL_18_STABLE [912cfa314] 2025-11-22 21:21:54 +1300
3469Branch: REL_17_STABLE [60215eae7] 2025-11-22 21:22:37 +1300
3470Branch: REL_16_STABLE [600acd34b] 2025-11-22 21:23:23 +1300
3471Branch: REL_15_STABLE [55164852d] 2025-11-22 21:23:45 +1300
3472Branch: REL_14_STABLE [035a1f5ac] 2025-11-22 21:20:27 +1300
3473-->
3474     <para>
3475      Adjust our JIT code to work with LLVM 21 (Holger Hoffstätte)
3476      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;912cfa314">&sect;</ulink>
3477     </para>
3478
3479     <para>
3480      The previous coding failed to compile on aarch64 machines.
3481     </para>
3482    </listitem>
3483
3484    <listitem>
3485<!--
3486Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
3487Branch: master [6d969ca68] 2025-11-17 15:24:34 -0500
3488Branch: REL_18_STABLE [db4eba152] 2025-11-17 15:24:40 -0500
3489Branch: master [3e83bdd35] 2025-11-18 16:16:46 -0500
3490Branch: REL_18_STABLE [6a5170755] 2025-11-18 16:16:51 -0500
3491-->
3492     <para>
3493      Fix aarch64-specific code to build with old (RHEL7-era) system
3494      header files (Tom Lane)
3495      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;db4eba152">&sect;</ulink>
3496      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6a5170755">&sect;</ulink>
3497     </para>
3498    </listitem>
3499
3500    <listitem>
3501<!--
3502Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
3503Branch: master [85d5bd308] 2025-12-31 11:18:14 -0800
3504Branch: REL_18_STABLE [640772c6d] 2025-12-31 11:18:17 -0800
3505-->
3506     <para>
3507      Fix incorrect configure probe
3508      for <function>io_uring_queue_init_mem()</function> (Masahiko Sawada)
3509      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;640772c6d">&sect;</ulink>
3510     </para>
3511
3512     <para>
3513      This error resulted in failure to optimize async I/O buffer
3514      allocations in autotools-based builds, though the code did work when
3515      building with meson.  The main impact of the omission was
3516      slower-than-necessary backend process exits.
3517     </para>
3518    </listitem>
3519
3520    <listitem>
3521<!--
3522Author: Thomas Munro <tmunro@postgresql.org>
3523Branch: master [f94e9141a] 2026-02-06 17:38:49 +1300
3524Branch: REL_18_STABLE [33e3de6d7] 2026-02-06 17:38:39 +1300
3525Branch: REL_17_STABLE [4dac22aa1] 2026-02-06 17:41:42 +1300
3526Branch: REL_16_STABLE [e37b59802] 2026-02-06 17:43:49 +1300
3527-->
3528     <para>
3529      Add new server parameter <xref linkend="guc-file-extend-method"/> to
3530      control use of <function>posix_fallocate()</function> (Thomas Munro)
3531      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;33e3de6d7">&sect;</ulink>
3532     </para>
3533
3534     <para>
3535      <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> version 16 and later will
3536      use <function>posix_fallocate()</function>, if the platform provides
3537      it, to extend relation files.  However, this has been reported to
3538      interact poorly with some file systems: BTRFS compression is
3539      disabled by the use of <function>posix_fallocate()</function>, and
3540      XFS could produce spurious <literal>ENOSPC</literal> errors in older
3541      Linux kernel versions.  To provide a workaround, introduce this new
3542      server parameter.  Setting <varname>file_extend_method</varname>
3543      to <literal>write_zeros</literal> will cause the server to return to
3544      the old method of extending files by writing blocks of zeroes.
3545     </para>
3546    </listitem>
3547
3548    <listitem>
3549<!--
3550Author: Thomas Munro <tmunro@postgresql.org>
3551Branch: master [c507ba55f] 2025-12-10 09:01:35 +1300
3552Branch: REL_18_STABLE [bebb281b0] 2025-12-10 09:05:21 +1300
3553Branch: REL_17_STABLE [f24af0e04] 2025-12-10 09:10:31 +1300
3554Branch: REL_16_STABLE [d62a258cd] 2025-12-10 09:11:19 +1300
3555Branch: master [bec2a0aa3] 2025-12-21 17:34:20 +1300
3556Branch: REL_18_STABLE [a7d06e74d] 2025-12-21 17:34:49 +1300
3557Branch: REL_17_STABLE [045185913] 2025-12-21 17:35:46 +1300
3558Branch: REL_16_STABLE [0666ccc16] 2025-12-21 17:36:04 +1300
3559Branch: master [45d92b76d] 2025-12-29 15:47:31 +1300
3560Branch: REL_18_STABLE [4da5c33a3] 2025-12-29 15:48:27 +1300
3561Branch: REL_17_STABLE [b3c8119e2] 2025-12-29 15:28:17 +1300
3562Branch: REL_16_STABLE [80e8ec772] 2025-12-29 15:31:39 +1300
3563-->
3564     <para>
3565      Honor <function>open()</function>'s <literal>O_CLOEXEC</literal>
3566      flag on Windows (Bryan Green, Thomas Munro)
3567      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bebb281b0">&sect;</ulink>
3568      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a7d06e74d">&sect;</ulink>
3569      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4da5c33a3">&sect;</ulink>
3570     </para>
3571
3572     <para>
3573      Make this flag work like it does on POSIX platforms, so that we
3574      don't leak file handles into child processes such as <literal>COPY
3575      TO/FROM PROGRAM</literal>.  While that leakage hasn't caused many
3576      problems, it seems undesirable.
3577     </para>
3578    </listitem>
3579
3580    <listitem>
3581<!--
3582Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
3583Branch: master [c0bf15729] 2026-02-07 20:05:52 -0500
3584Branch: REL_18_STABLE [5eac1d68f] 2026-02-07 20:05:52 -0500
3585Branch: REL_17_STABLE [59c2f7efa] 2026-02-07 20:05:52 -0500
3586Branch: REL_16_STABLE [7369656fa] 2026-02-07 20:05:52 -0500
3587-->
3588     <para>
3589      Fix failure to parse long options on the server command line in
3590      Solaris executables built with meson (Tom Lane)
3591      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;5eac1d68f">&sect;</ulink>
3592     </para>
3593    </listitem>
3594
3595    <listitem>
3596<!--
3597Author: Thomas Munro <tmunro@postgresql.org>
3598Branch: master [32b236644] 2025-11-17 12:48:55 +1300
3599Branch: REL_18_STABLE [bcfca332f] 2025-11-17 12:48:47 +1300
3600Branch: REL_17_STABLE [d66a922f9] 2025-11-17 12:48:37 +1300
3601Branch: REL_16_STABLE [a1407dade] 2025-11-17 12:48:22 +1300
3602Branch: REL_15_STABLE [3995e4a9d] 2025-11-18 11:19:56 +1300
3603Branch: REL_14_STABLE [29a3e22f3] 2025-11-18 11:21:15 +1300
3604-->
3605     <para>
3606      Support process title changes on GNU/Hurd (Michael Banck)
3607      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bcfca332f">&sect;</ulink>
3608     </para>
3609    </listitem>
3610
3611    <listitem>
3612<!--
3613Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
3614Branch: master [c5c808f9b] 2025-12-24 13:55:29 -0800
3615Branch: REL_18_STABLE [4e1376900] 2025-12-24 13:55:32 -0800
3616-->
3617     <para>
3618      Fix <application>psql</application>'s tab completion
3619      for <command>VACUUM</command> option values (Yugo Nagata)
3620      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4e1376900">&sect;</ulink>
3621     </para>
3622    </listitem>
3623
3624    <listitem>
3625<!--
3626Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
3627Branch: master [4b77282f2] 2026-01-29 16:20:45 +0900
3628Branch: REL_18_STABLE [d42735b1e] 2026-01-29 16:20:50 +0900
3629-->
3630     <para>
3631      In <application>psql</application> command prompts,
3632      do not show a value for <literal>%P</literal> (pipeline status) when
3633      there is no server connection (Chao Li)
3634      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d42735b1e">&sect;</ulink>
3635     </para>
3636
3637     <para>
3638      This makes <literal>%P</literal> act like other prompt escape
3639      sequences whose values depend on the active connection.
3640     </para>
3641    </listitem>
3642
3643    <listitem>
3644<!--
3645Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
3646Branch: master [7a485bd64] 2026-01-09 10:12:54 -0600
3647Branch: REL_18_STABLE [39d555576] 2026-01-09 10:12:54 -0600
3648Branch: master [5d1f5079a] 2026-01-11 13:52:50 -0600
3649Branch: REL_18_STABLE [56e1f5010] 2026-01-11 13:52:50 -0600
3650-->
3651     <para>
3652      Fix <application>pg_dump</application>'s logic for collecting
3653      sequence values (Nathan Bossart)
3654      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;39d555576">&sect;</ulink>
3655      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;56e1f5010">&sect;</ulink>
3656     </para>
3657
3658     <para>
3659      <application>pg_dump</application> failed if a sequence was dropped
3660      concurrently with the dump, even if the sequence was not among the
3661      database objects to be dumped.  Also, if the calling user lacks
3662      privileges to read a sequence's
3663      value, <application>pg_dump</application> emitted incorrect values
3664      rather than failing as expected.
3665     </para>
3666    </listitem>
3667
3668    <listitem>
3669<!--
3670Author: Daniel Gustafsson <dgustafsson@postgresql.org>
3671Branch: master [ec0da9b89] 2025-12-27 23:05:48 +0100
3672Branch: REL_18_STABLE [61c78e1f4] 2025-12-27 23:05:48 +0100
3673-->
3674     <para>
3675      Fix potentially-incorrect quoting
3676      of <varname>oauth_validator_libraries</varname> values
3677      by <application>pg_dump</application> (ChangAo Chen)
3678      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;61c78e1f4">&sect;</ulink>
3679     </para>
3680
3681     <para>
3682      <application>pg_dump</application> applied the wrong quoting rule if
3683      it needed to dump a value of this setting.
3684     </para>
3685    </listitem>
3686
3687    <listitem>
3688<!--
3689Author: Noah Misch <noah@leadboat.com>
3690Branch: master [d49936f30] 2025-12-18 10:23:47 -0800
3691Branch: REL_18_STABLE [573e679a2] 2025-12-18 10:23:51 -0800
3692Branch: REL_17_STABLE [1cdc07ad5] 2025-12-18 10:23:51 -0800
3693-->
3694     <para>
3695      Avoid <application>pg_dump</application> assertion failure in
3696      binary-upgrade mode (Vignesh C)
3697      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;573e679a2">&sect;</ulink>
3698     </para>
3699
3700     <para>
3701      Failure to handle subscription-relation objects in the object
3702      sorting code triggered an assertion, though there were no serious
3703      ill effects in production builds.
3704     </para>
3705    </listitem>
3706
3707    <listitem>
3708<!--
3709Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
3710Branch: master [4aa0ac057] 2025-11-14 22:40:39 +0900
3711Branch: REL_18_STABLE [00e64e35c] 2025-11-14 22:41:55 +0900
3712Branch: REL_17_STABLE [5bc251b28] 2025-11-14 22:42:02 +0900
3713-->
3714     <para>
3715      Fix incorrect error handling in <application>pgbench</application>
3716      with multiple <command>\syncpipeline</command> commands
3717      in pipeline mode (Yugo Nagata)
3718      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;00e64e35c">&sect;</ulink>
3719     </para>
3720
3721     <para>
3722      If multiple <command>\syncpipeline</command> commands are
3723      encountered after a query error, <application>pgbench</application>
3724      would report <quote>failed to exit pipeline mode</quote>, or get an
3725      assertion failure in an assert-enabled build.
3726     </para>
3727    </listitem>
3728
3729    <listitem>
3730<!--
3731Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
3732Branch: master [d5b4f3a6d] 2025-11-19 18:05:42 +0200
3733Branch: REL_18_STABLE [19594271c] 2025-11-19 18:06:04 +0200
3734Branch: REL_17_STABLE [f2e0ca0af] 2025-11-19 18:06:23 +0200
3735Branch: REL_16_STABLE [890cc81b6] 2025-11-19 18:06:24 +0200
3736Branch: REL_15_STABLE [7c494072b] 2025-11-19 18:06:26 +0200
3737Branch: REL_14_STABLE [11cc0f452] 2025-11-19 18:06:27 +0200
3738-->
3739     <para>
3740      Make <application>pg_resetwal</application> print the updated value
3741      when changing OldestXID (Heikki Linnakangas)
3742      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;19594271c">&sect;</ulink>
3743     </para>
3744
3745     <para>
3746      It already did that for every other variable it can change.
3747     </para>
3748    </listitem>
3749
3750    <listitem>
3751<!--
3752Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
3753Branch: master [94939c5f3] 2025-11-12 12:20:16 +0200
3754Branch: REL_18_STABLE [8747b969f] 2025-11-12 12:21:30 +0200
3755Branch: REL_17_STABLE [cb2ef0e92] 2025-11-12 12:23:55 +0200
3756Branch: REL_16_STABLE [e039b09f8] 2025-11-12 12:45:16 +0200
3757Branch: REL_15_STABLE [97cd4b65a] 2025-11-12 12:46:19 +0200
3758-->
3759     <para>
3760      Make <application>pg_resetwal</application> allow setting next
3761      multixact xid to 0 or next multixact offset to UINT32_MAX (Maxim
3762      Orlov)
3763      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8747b969f">&sect;</ulink>
3764     </para>
3765
3766     <para>
3767      These are valid values, so rejecting them was incorrect.  In the
3768      worst case, if a pg_upgrade is attempted when exactly at the point
3769      of multixact wraparound, the upgrade would fail.
3770     </para>
3771    </listitem>
3772
3773    <listitem>
3774<!--
3775Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@kurilemu.de>
3776Branch: master [6bd469d26] 2025-12-04 18:12:08 +0100
3777Branch: REL_18_STABLE [df93f94dd] 2025-12-04 18:12:08 +0100
3778Branch: REL_17_STABLE [ce2f575b7] 2025-12-04 18:12:08 +0100
3779Branch: master [1f28982e4] 2026-01-21 18:55:43 +0100
3780Branch: REL_18_STABLE [3c83a2a0a] 2026-01-21 18:55:43 +0100
3781Branch: REL_17_STABLE [e1a327dc4] 2026-01-21 18:55:43 +0100
3782Branch: REL_16_STABLE [098a1fab8] 2026-01-21 18:55:43 +0100
3783Branch: REL_15_STABLE [dcddd6987] 2026-01-21 18:55:43 +0100
3784Branch: REL_14_STABLE [607a67c5e] 2026-01-21 18:55:43 +0100
3785-->
3786     <para>
3787      In <filename>contrib/amcheck</filename>, use the correct snapshot
3788      for btree index parent checks (Mihail Nikalayeu)
3789      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;df93f94dd">&sect;</ulink>
3790      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3c83a2a0a">&sect;</ulink>
3791     </para>
3792
3793     <para>
3794      The previous coding caused spurious errors when examining indexes
3795      created with <command>CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY</command>.
3796     </para>
3797    </listitem>
3798
3799    <listitem>
3800<!--
3801Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
3802Branch: master [cbe04e5d7] 2025-12-02 21:11:15 +0200
3803Branch: REL_18_STABLE [19e786727] 2025-12-02 21:15:04 +0200
3804Branch: REL_17_STABLE [e8ae59445] 2025-12-02 21:15:28 +0200
3805Branch: REL_16_STABLE [182901626] 2025-12-02 21:15:48 +0200
3806Branch: REL_15_STABLE [7792bdc45] 2025-12-02 21:16:06 +0200
3807Branch: REL_14_STABLE [fbb4b6078] 2025-12-02 21:16:17 +0200
3808-->
3809     <para>
3810      Fix <filename>contrib/amcheck</filename> to
3811      handle <quote>half-dead</quote> btree index pages correctly
3812      (Heikki Linnakangas)
3813      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;19e786727">&sect;</ulink>
3814     </para>
3815
3816     <para>
3817      <filename>amcheck</filename> expected such a page to have a parent
3818      downlink, but it does not, leading to a false error report
3819      about <quote>mismatch between parent key and child high key</quote>.
3820     </para>
3821    </listitem>
3822
3823    <listitem>
3824<!--
3825Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
3826Branch: master [6c05ef572] 2025-12-02 21:10:51 +0200
3827Branch: REL_18_STABLE [50c63ebb0] 2025-12-02 21:14:53 +0200
3828Branch: REL_17_STABLE [5a2d1df00] 2025-12-02 21:15:19 +0200
3829Branch: REL_16_STABLE [f2a6df9fd] 2025-12-02 21:15:43 +0200
3830Branch: REL_15_STABLE [721b58fbe] 2025-12-02 21:16:01 +0200
3831Branch: REL_14_STABLE [a4cb21ea9] 2025-12-02 21:16:13 +0200
3832-->
3833     <para>
3834      Fix <filename>contrib/amcheck</filename> to
3835      handle incomplete btree root page splits correctly
3836      (Heikki Linnakangas)
3837      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;50c63ebb0">&sect;</ulink>
3838     </para>
3839
3840     <para>
3841      <filename>amcheck</filename> could report a false error
3842      about <quote>block is not true root</quote>.
3843     </para>
3844    </listitem>
3845
3846    <listitem>
3847<!--
3848Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
3849Branch: master [3f83de20b] 2025-12-11 14:11:06 +0900
3850Branch: REL_18_STABLE [580b5c2f3] 2025-12-11 14:11:25 +0900
3851-->
3852     <para>
3853      Fix excessive memory allocation
3854      in <filename>contrib/pg_buffercache</filename> (David Geier)
3855      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;580b5c2f3">&sect;</ulink>
3856     </para>
3857
3858     <para>
3859      The code allocated twice as much memory as it needed for NUMA
3860      page status.
3861     </para>
3862    </listitem>
3863
3864    <listitem>
3865<!--
3866Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
3867Branch: master [4f49e4b55] 2026-01-04 20:32:40 +1300
3868Branch: REL_18_STABLE [07c1c6ec5] 2026-01-04 20:33:14 +1300
3869Branch: REL_17_STABLE [a5f2dc421] 2026-01-04 20:33:39 +1300
3870Branch: REL_16_STABLE [54f82c4aa] 2026-01-04 20:34:01 +1300
3871Branch: REL_15_STABLE [c89510431] 2026-01-04 20:34:22 +1300
3872Branch: REL_14_STABLE [67a6fd507] 2026-01-04 20:34:45 +1300
3873-->
3874     <para>
3875      Fix edge-case integer overflow
3876      in <filename>contrib/intarray</filename>'s selectivity estimator
3877      for <literal>@@</literal> (Chao Li)
3878      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;07c1c6ec5">&sect;</ulink>
3879     </para>
3880
3881     <para>
3882      This could cause poor selectivity estimates to be produced for cases
3883      involving the maximum integer value.
3884     </para>
3885    </listitem>
3886
3887    <listitem>
3888<!--
3889Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
3890Branch: master [84d5efa7e] 2025-12-16 10:35:40 -0800
3891Branch: REL_18_STABLE [f79e239e0] 2025-12-16 10:36:09 -0800
3892Branch: REL_17_STABLE [b8cfe9dc2] 2025-12-16 10:36:29 -0800
3893Branch: REL_16_STABLE [b80227c0a] 2025-12-16 10:36:48 -0800
3894Branch: REL_15_STABLE [335b2f30b] 2025-12-16 10:37:22 -0800
3895Branch: REL_14_STABLE [898991966] 2025-12-16 10:38:15 -0800
3896-->
3897     <para>
3898      Fix multibyte-encoding issue in <filename>contrib/ltree</filename>
3899      (Jeff Davis)
3900      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f79e239e0">&sect;</ulink>
3901     </para>
3902
3903     <para>
3904      The previous coding could pass an incomplete multibyte character
3905      to <function>lower()</function>, probably resulting in incorrect
3906      behavior.
3907     </para>
3908    </listitem>
3909
3910    <listitem>
3911<!--
3912Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
3913Branch: master [5d95219fa] 2026-01-20 08:11:12 +0900
3914Branch: REL_18_STABLE [3304e97b1] 2026-01-20 08:11:16 +0900
3915-->
3916     <para>
3917      Avoid crash in <filename>contrib/pg_stat_statements</filename> when
3918      an <literal>IN</literal> list contains both constants and
3919      non-constant expressions (Sami Imseih)
3920      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3304e97b1">&sect;</ulink>
3921     </para>
3922    </listitem>
3923
3924    <listitem>
3925<!--
3926Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
3927Branch: master [228fe0c3e] 2026-01-18 14:54:33 -0500
3928Branch: REL_18_STABLE [6574bee64] 2026-01-18 14:54:47 -0500
3929Branch: REL_17_STABLE [f87c0b84e] 2026-01-18 14:54:54 -0500
3930Branch: REL_16_STABLE [d852d105e] 2026-01-18 14:55:01 -0500
3931Branch: REL_15_STABLE [00410b76d] 2026-01-18 14:55:07 -0500
3932Branch: REL_14_STABLE [8bbf28191] 2026-01-18 14:55:13 -0500
3933-->
3934     <para>
3935      Update time zone data files to <application>tzdata</application>
3936      release 2025c (Tom Lane)
3937      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6574bee64">&sect;</ulink>
3938     </para>
3939
3940     <para>
3941      The only change is in historical data for pre-1976 timestamps in
3942      Baja California.
3943     </para>
3944    </listitem>
3945
3946   </itemizedlist>
3947
3948  </sect2>
3949 </sect1>
3950
3951 <sect1 id="release-18-1">
3952  <title>Release 18.1</title>
3953
3954  <formalpara>
3955  <title>Release date:</title>
3956  <para>2025-11-13</para>
3957  </formalpara>
3958
3959  <para>
3960   This release contains a variety of fixes from 18.0.
3961   For information about new features in major release 18, see
3962   <xref linkend="release-18"/>.
3963  </para>
3964
3965  <sect2 id="release-18-1-migration">
3966   <title>Migration to Version 18.1</title>
3967
3968   <para>
3969    A dump/restore is not required for those running 18.X.
3970   </para>
3971  </sect2>
3972
3973  <sect2 id="release-18-1-changes">
3974   <title>Changes</title>
3975
3976   <itemizedlist>
3977
3978    <listitem>
3979<!--
3980Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
3981Branch: master [5e4fcbe53] 2025-11-10 09:00:00 -0600
3982Branch: REL_18_STABLE [00eb646ea] 2025-11-10 09:00:00 -0600
3983Branch: REL_17_STABLE [e2fb3dfa8] 2025-11-10 09:00:00 -0600
3984Branch: REL_16_STABLE [d20abb587] 2025-11-10 09:00:00 -0600
3985Branch: REL_15_STABLE [2393d374a] 2025-11-10 09:00:00 -0600
3986Branch: REL_14_STABLE [95cce5669] 2025-11-10 09:00:00 -0600
3987Branch: REL_13_STABLE [8a2530ebc] 2025-11-10 09:00:00 -0600
3988-->
3989     <para>
3990      Check for <literal>CREATE</literal> privileges on the schema
3991      in <command>CREATE STATISTICS</command> (Jelte Fennema-Nio)
