3992      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;00eb646ea">&sect;</ulink>
3993     </para>
3994
3995     <para>
3996      This omission allowed table owners to create statistics in any
3997      schema, potentially leading to unexpected naming conflicts.
3998     </para>
3999
4000     <para>
4001      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks
4002      Jelte Fennema-Nio for reporting this problem.
4003      (CVE-2025-12817)
4004     </para>
4005    </listitem>
4006
4007    <listitem>
4008<!--
4009Author: Jacob Champion <jchampion@postgresql.org>
4010Branch: master [600086f47] 2025-11-10 06:20:33 -0800
4011Branch: REL_18_STABLE [7eb8fcad8] 2025-11-10 06:03:01 -0800
4012Branch: REL_17_STABLE [f5999f018] 2025-11-10 06:03:03 -0800
4013Branch: REL_16_STABLE [585fd9b3c] 2025-11-10 06:03:04 -0800
4014Branch: REL_15_STABLE [91421565f] 2025-11-10 06:03:05 -0800
4015Branch: REL_14_STABLE [96d2c7e96] 2025-11-10 06:03:05 -0800
4016Branch: REL_13_STABLE [d6f0c0d6d] 2025-11-10 06:03:06 -0800
4017-->
4018     <para>
4019      Avoid integer overflow in allocation-size calculations
4020      within <application>libpq</application> (Jacob Champion)
4021      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7eb8fcad8">&sect;</ulink>
4022     </para>
4023
4024     <para>
4025      Several places in <application>libpq</application> were not
4026      sufficiently careful about computing the required size of a memory
4027      allocation.  Sufficiently large inputs could cause integer overflow,
4028      resulting in an undersized buffer, which would then lead to writing
4029      past the end of the buffer.
4030     </para>
4031
4032     <para>
4033      The <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> Project thanks Aleksey
4034      Solovev of Positive Technologies for reporting this problem.
4035      (CVE-2025-12818)
4036     </para>
4037    </listitem>
4038
4039    <listitem>
4040<!--
4041Author: Amit Langote <amitlan@postgresql.org>
4042Branch: master [ef5e60a9d] 2025-10-09 01:07:59 -0400
4043Branch: REL_18_STABLE [dc9125111] 2025-10-09 01:07:52 -0400
4044Branch: REL_17_STABLE [09f86a42f] 2025-10-09 01:07:36 -0400
4045Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
4046Branch: master [c09a06918] 2025-11-04 12:25:04 +0100
4047Branch: REL_18_STABLE [1baae827e] 2025-11-04 12:25:14 +0100
4048Branch: REL_17_STABLE [1e6dfdaa0] 2025-11-04 12:25:20 +0100
4049-->
4050     <para>
4051      Prevent <quote>unrecognized node type</quote> errors when a SQL/JSON
4052      function such as <function>JSON_VALUE</function> has
4053      a <literal>DEFAULT</literal> clause containing
4054      a <literal>COLLATE</literal> expression (Jian He)
4055      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;dc9125111">&sect;</ulink>
4056      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1baae827e">&sect;</ulink>
4057     </para>
4058    </listitem>
4059
4060    <listitem>
4061<!--
4062Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
4063Branch: master [18d261409] 2025-10-21 12:35:36 +0900
4064Branch: REL_18_STABLE [40c242830] 2025-10-21 12:38:16 +0900
4065Branch: REL_18_STABLE [ee49f2cf4] 2025-10-21 14:12:13 +0900
4066-->
4067     <para>
4068      Avoid incorrect optimization of
4069      variable-free <literal>HAVING</literal> clauses with grouping sets
4070      (Richard Guo)
4071      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;40c242830">&sect;</ulink>
4072      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ee49f2cf4">&sect;</ulink>
4073     </para>
4074    </listitem>
4075
4076    <listitem>
4077<!--
4078Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
4079Branch: master [257ee7834] 2025-10-30 11:58:45 +0900
4080Branch: REL_18_STABLE [ef6168baf] 2025-10-30 12:03:15 +0900
4081-->
4082     <para>
4083      Do not use parallelism in hash right semi joins (Richard Guo)
4084      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ef6168baf">&sect;</ulink>
4085     </para>
4086
4087     <para>
4088      The case does not work reliably due to a race condition in updating
4089      the join's shared hash table.
4090     </para>
4091    </listitem>
4092
4093    <listitem>
4094<!--
4095Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
4096Branch: master [c1777f2d6] 2025-11-05 18:09:21 +0900
4097Branch: REL_18_STABLE [500f64636] 2025-11-05 18:15:02 +0900
4098-->
4099     <para>
4100      Avoid possible division-by-zero when creating ordered-append plans
4101      (Richard Guo)
4102      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;500f64636">&sect;</ulink>
4103     </para>
4104
4105     <para>
4106      This mistake could result in incorrect selection of the cheapest
4107      path, or in an assertion failure in debug builds.
4108     </para>
4109    </listitem>
4110
4111    <listitem>
4112<!--
4113Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
4114Branch: master [35e53b684] 2025-10-28 10:07:29 +0100
4115Branch: REL_18_STABLE [74197bdc8] 2025-10-28 10:11:35 +0100
4116-->
4117     <para>
4118      Fix planner failure with index types that can do ordered access but
4119      not index-only scans (Maxime Schoemans)
4120      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;74197bdc8">&sect;</ulink>
4121     </para>
4122
4123     <para>
4124      This oversight resulted in errors like <quote>no data returned for
4125      index-only scan</quote>.  The case does not arise with any in-core
4126      index type, but some extensions encountered the problem.
4127     </para>
4128    </listitem>
4129
4130    <listitem>
4131<!--
4132Author: Peter Geoghegan <pg@bowt.ie>
4133Branch: master [843e50208] 2025-10-10 14:52:25 -0400
4134Branch: REL_18_STABLE [61de81a49] 2025-10-10 14:52:23 -0400
4135Branch: REL_17_STABLE [ae15cebc2] 2025-10-10 14:52:21 -0400
4136Branch: REL_16_STABLE [c160fd469] 2025-10-10 14:52:19 -0400
4137Branch: REL_15_STABLE [ca0c93908] 2025-10-10 14:52:17 -0400
4138Branch: REL_14_STABLE [314004021] 2025-10-10 14:52:14 -0400
4139Branch: REL_13_STABLE [af302ff6b] 2025-10-10 14:52:12 -0400
4140-->
4141     <para>
4142      Remove faulty assertion in btree index cleanup (Peter Geoghegan)
4143      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;61de81a49">&sect;</ulink>
4144     </para>
4145    </listitem>
4146
4147    <listitem>
4148<!--
4149Author: Tomas Vondra <tomas.vondra@postgresql.org>
4150Branch: master [c98dffcb7] 2025-11-04 18:51:17 +0100
4151Branch: REL_18_STABLE [a26b753a0] 2025-11-04 18:47:14 +0100
4152-->
4153     <para>
4154      Avoid possible out-of-memory or <quote>invalid memory alloc request
4155      size</quote> failures during parallel GIN index build (Tomas Vondra)
4156      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a26b753a0">&sect;</ulink>
4157     </para>
4158    </listitem>
4159
4160    <listitem>
4161<!--
4162Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@kurilemu.de>
4163Branch: master [a95e3d84c] 2025-11-04 13:23:26 +0100
4164Branch: REL_18_STABLE [419ffde23] 2025-11-04 13:23:26 +0100
4165Branch: REL_17_STABLE [f4b68b033] 2025-11-04 13:23:26 +0100
4166Branch: REL_16_STABLE [6ef33c805] 2025-11-04 13:23:26 +0100
4167Branch: REL_15_STABLE [23ddadf68] 2025-11-04 13:23:26 +0100
4168Branch: REL_14_STABLE [f0ad41716] 2025-11-04 13:23:26 +0100
4169Branch: REL_13_STABLE [3c7b47974] 2025-11-04 13:23:26 +0100
4170Branch: master [0a3d27bfe] 2025-11-04 20:31:43 +0100
4171Branch: REL_18_STABLE [8733f0b54] 2025-11-04 20:31:43 +0100
4172Branch: REL_17_STABLE [3b5007347] 2025-11-04 20:31:43 +0100
4173Branch: REL_16_STABLE [20442cf50] 2025-11-04 20:31:43 +0100
4174Branch: REL_15_STABLE [bcfbd3f74] 2025-11-04 20:31:43 +0100
4175Branch: REL_14_STABLE [65b0d1f74] 2025-11-04 20:31:43 +0100
4176Branch: REL_13_STABLE [ef81697fe] 2025-11-04 20:31:43 +0100
4177-->
4178     <para>
4179      Ensure that BRIN autosummarization provides a snapshot for index
4180      expressions that need one (Álvaro Herrera)
4181      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;419ffde23">&sect;</ulink>
4182      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8733f0b54">&sect;</ulink>
4183     </para>
4184
4185     <para>
4186      Previously, autosummarization would fail for such indexes, and then
4187      leave placeholder index tuples behind, causing the index to bloat
4188      over time.
4189     </para>
4190    </listitem>
4191
4192    <listitem>
4193<!--
4194Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
4195Branch: master [9fd29d7ff] 2025-10-21 20:46:14 +1300
4196Branch: REL_18_STABLE [715983a81] 2025-10-21 20:46:49 +1300
4197Branch: REL_17_STABLE [c4f5a59ab] 2025-10-21 20:47:10 +1300
4198Branch: REL_16_STABLE [ef915bf93] 2025-10-21 20:47:35 +1300
4199Branch: REL_15_STABLE [810aaf7f2] 2025-10-21 20:48:06 +1300
4200Branch: REL_14_STABLE [eea24eb0a] 2025-10-21 20:48:34 +1300
4201Branch: REL_13_STABLE [c28f8ca29] 2025-10-21 20:49:00 +1300
4202-->
4203     <para>
4204      Fix integer-overflow hazard in BRIN index scans when the table
4205      contains close to 2<superscript>32</superscript> pages (Sunil S)
4206      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;715983a81">&sect;</ulink>
4207     </para>
4208
4209     <para>
4210      This oversight could result in an infinite loop or scanning of
4211      unneeded table pages.
4212     </para>
4213    </listitem>
4214
4215    <listitem>
4216<!--
4217Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
4218Branch: master [6911f8037] 2025-10-23 13:11:02 +1300
4219Branch: REL_18_STABLE [ceb51d09b] 2025-10-23 13:12:03 +1300
4220Branch: REL_17_STABLE [10945148e] 2025-10-23 13:12:49 +1300
4221Branch: REL_16_STABLE [3398b0d02] 2025-10-23 13:13:19 +1300
4222Branch: REL_15_STABLE [b8ecfbe5a] 2025-10-23 13:13:44 +1300
4223Branch: REL_14_STABLE [e4316ec4d] 2025-10-23 13:14:08 +1300
4224Branch: REL_13_STABLE [4afab175b] 2025-10-23 13:14:33 +1300
4225-->
4226     <para>
4227      Fix incorrect zero-extension of stored values in JIT-generated tuple
4228      deforming code (David Rowley)
4229      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ceb51d09b">&sect;</ulink>
4230     </para>
4231
4232     <para>
4233      When not using JIT, the equivalent code does sign-extension not
4234      zero-extension, leading to a different Datum representation of small
4235      integer data types.  This inconsistency was masked in most cases,
4236      but it is known to lead to <quote>could not find memoization table
4237      entry</quote> errors when using Memoize plan nodes, and there might
4238      be other symptoms.
4239     </para>
4240    </listitem>
4241
4242    <listitem>
4243<!--
4244Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
4245Branch: master [5c0a20003] 2025-10-18 16:07:04 +1300
4246Branch: REL_18_STABLE [0b6a02f03] 2025-10-18 16:07:41 +1300
4247-->
4248     <para>
4249      Fix rare crash when processing hashed <literal>GROUPING
4250      SETS</literal> queries (David Rowley)
4251      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0b6a02f03">&sect;</ulink>
4252     </para>
4253    </listitem>
4254
4255    <listitem>
4256<!--
4257Author: Tomas Vondra <tomas.vondra@postgresql.org>
4258Branch: master [b85c4700f] 2025-10-17 22:21:50 +0200
4259Branch: REL_18_STABLE [aa151022e] 2025-10-17 22:27:49 +0200
4260-->
4261     <para>
4262      Repair faulty hash-table-size-choosing logic in hash joins
4263      (Tomas Vondra)
4264      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;aa151022e">&sect;</ulink>
4265     </para>
4266
4267     <para>
4268      Hash joins sometimes used more memory than intended, or failed to
4269      divide it in an efficient way.
4270     </para>
4271    </listitem>
4272
4273    <listitem>
4274<!--
4275Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
4276Branch: master [688dc6299] 2025-10-15 12:47:33 -0500
4277Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c8af5019b] 2025-10-15 12:47:33 -0500
4278Branch: REL_18_STABLE [15d7dded0] 2025-10-15 13:14:00 -0500
4279-->
4280     <para>
4281      Improve relation lookup logic in statistics manipulation functions
4282      (Nathan Bossart)
4283      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c8af5019b">&sect;</ulink>
4284      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;15d7dded0">&sect;</ulink>
4285     </para>
4286
4287     <para>
4288      Fix <function>pg_restore_relation_stats()</function>,
4289      <function>pg_clear_relation_stats()</function>,
4290      <function>pg_restore_attribute_stats()</function>, and
4291      <function>pg_clear_attribute_stats()</function> to check
4292      privileges before acquiring lock on the target relation
4293      rather than after.
4294     </para>
4295    </listitem>
4296
4297    <listitem>
4298<!--
4299Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
4300Branch: master [39dcfda2d] 2025-10-26 10:59:50 +1300
4301Branch: REL_18_STABLE [a2387c32f] 2025-10-26 11:01:32 +1300
4302Branch: REL_17_STABLE [0d3074615] 2025-10-26 11:01:53 +1300
4303Branch: REL_16_STABLE [a546964db] 2025-10-26 11:02:15 +1300
4304Branch: REL_15_STABLE [2992b9a07] 2025-10-26 11:02:36 +1300
4305-->
4306     <para>
4307      Fix incorrect logic for caching result-relation information for
4308      triggers (David Rowley, Amit Langote)
4309      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a2387c32f">&sect;</ulink>
4310     </para>
4311
4312     <para>
4313      In cases where partitions' column sets aren't physically identical
4314      to their parent partitioned tables' column sets, this oversight
4315      could lead to crashes.
4316     </para>
4317    </listitem>
4318
4319    <listitem>
4320<!--
4321Author: Amit Langote <amitlan@postgresql.org>
4322Branch: master [905e932f0] 2025-10-16 14:01:44 +0900
4323Branch: REL_18_STABLE [1296dcf18] 2025-10-16 14:01:50 +0900
4324-->
4325     <para>
4326      Fix crash during EvalPlanQual rechecks on partitioned tables (David
4327      Rowley, Amit Langote)
4328      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1296dcf18">&sect;</ulink>
4329     </para>
4330    </listitem>
4331
4332    <listitem>
4333<!--
4334Author: Etsuro Fujita <efujita@postgresql.org>
4335Branch: master [12609fbac] 2025-10-15 17:15:00 +0900
4336Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b14144325] 2025-10-15 17:15:01 +0900
4337Branch: REL_17_STABLE [2bb84ea7e] 2025-10-15 17:15:02 +0900
4338Branch: REL_16_STABLE [5a9af4868] 2025-10-15 17:15:03 +0900
4339Branch: REL_15_STABLE [4a08603a2] 2025-10-15 17:15:04 +0900
4340Branch: REL_14_STABLE [24e74b962] 2025-10-15 17:15:05 +0900
4341Branch: REL_13_STABLE [12b0c0e51] 2025-10-15 17:15:05 +0900
4342-->
4343     <para>
4344      Fix EvalPlanQual handling of foreign or custom joins that do not
4345      have an alternative local-join plan prepared for EPQ (Masahiko
4346      Sawada, Etsuro Fujita)
4347      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b14144325">&sect;</ulink>
4348     </para>
4349
4350     <para>
4351      In such cases the foreign or custom access method should be invoked
4352      normally, but that did not happen, typically leading to a crash.
4353     </para>
4354    </listitem>
4355
4356    <listitem>
4357<!--
4358Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@kurilemu.de>
4359Branch: master [3231fd045] 2025-10-11 20:30:12 +0200
4360Branch: REL_18_STABLE [08c037dff] 2025-10-11 20:30:12 +0200
4361Branch: REL_17_STABLE [ea06f97ee] 2025-10-11 20:30:12 +0200
4362Branch: REL_16_STABLE [b835759ec] 2025-10-11 20:30:12 +0200
4363Branch: REL_15_STABLE [bdae98495] 2025-10-11 20:30:12 +0200
4364Branch: REL_14_STABLE [b753be38a] 2025-10-11 20:30:12 +0200
4365-->
4366     <para>
4367      Avoid duplicating hash partition constraints during <literal>DETACH
4368      CONCURRENTLY</literal> (Haiyang Li)
4369      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;08c037dff">&sect;</ulink>
4370     </para>
4371
4372     <para>
4373      <command>ALTER TABLE DETACH PARTITION CONCURRENTLY</command> was
4374      written to add a copy of the partitioning constraint to the
4375      now-detached partition.  This was misguided, partially because
4376      non-concurrent <literal>DETACH</literal> doesn't do that, but mostly
4377      because in the case of hash partitioning the constraint expression
4378      contains references to the parent table's OID.  That causes problems
4379      during dump/restore, or if the parent table is dropped
4380      after <literal>DETACH</literal>.  In v19 and later, we'll no longer
4381      create any such copied constraints at all.  In released branches, to
4382      minimize the risk of unforeseen consequences, only skip adding a
4383      copied constraint if it is for hash partitioning.
4384     </para>
4385    </listitem>
4386
4387    <listitem>
4388<!--
4389Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
4390Branch: master [040cc5f3c] 2025-11-04 14:46:58 +0100
4391Branch: REL_18_STABLE [ba99c9491] 2025-11-04 14:47:15 +0100
4392Branch: REL_17_STABLE [0b44f2443] 2025-11-04 15:18:09 +0100
4393Branch: REL_16_STABLE [7180d56c5] 2025-11-04 15:22:17 +0100
4394Branch: REL_15_STABLE [643a5e96c] 2025-11-04 15:28:46 +0100
4395Branch: REL_14_STABLE [04345d7a4] 2025-11-04 15:37:08 +0100
4396Branch: REL_13_STABLE [7f239c72a] 2025-11-04 15:37:19 +0100
4397-->
4398     <para>
4399      Disallow generated columns in partition keys
4400      (Jian He, Ashutosh Bapat)
4401      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ba99c9491">&sect;</ulink>
4402     </para>
4403
4404     <para>
4405      This was already not allowed, but the check missed some cases, such
4406      as where the column reference is implicit in a whole-row reference.
4407     </para>
4408    </listitem>
4409
4410    <listitem>
4411<!--
4412Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
4413Branch: master [aa606b931] 2025-11-06 13:54:42 +0100
4414Branch: REL_18_STABLE [0f9e0068b] 2025-11-06 13:55:08 +0100
4415Branch: REL_17_STABLE [07f787e57] 2025-11-06 14:01:42 +0100
4416Branch: REL_16_STABLE [26958f4d9] 2025-11-06 14:01:57 +0100
4417Branch: REL_15_STABLE [8278737bf] 2025-11-06 14:02:03 +0100
4418Branch: REL_14_STABLE [ccfe28eb4] 2025-11-06 14:02:09 +0100
4419Branch: REL_13_STABLE [3717849e6] 2025-11-06 14:02:13 +0100
4420-->
4421     <para>
4422      Disallow generated columns in <literal>COPY ... FROM
4423      ... WHERE</literal> clauses (Peter Eisentraut, Jian He)
4424      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0f9e0068b">&sect;</ulink>
4425     </para>
4426
4427     <para>
4428      Previously, incorrect behavior or an obscure error message resulted
4429      from attempting to reference such a column, since generated columns
4430      have not yet been computed at the point
4431      where <literal>WHERE</literal> filtering is done.
4432     </para>
4433    </listitem>
4434
4435    <listitem>
4436<!--
4437Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@kurilemu.de>
4438Branch: master [cf8be0225] 2025-11-03 15:58:19 +0100
4439Branch: REL_18_STABLE [d9ffc2729] 2025-11-03 15:58:19 +0100
4440-->
4441     <para>
4442      Prevent setting a column as identity if it has a not-null constraint
4443      but the constraint is marked as invalid (Jian He)
4444      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d9ffc2729">&sect;</ulink>
4445     </para>
4446
4447     <para>
4448      Identity columns must be not-null, but the check for that missed
4449      this edge case.
4450     </para>
4451    </listitem>
4452
4453    <listitem>
4454<!--
4455Author: John Naylor <john.naylor@postgresql.org>
4456Branch: master [54ab74865] 2025-10-03 16:05:02 +0700
4457Branch: REL_18_STABLE [76613b539] 2025-10-03 16:07:42 +0700
4458Branch: REL_17_STABLE [3549ffb6a] 2025-10-03 16:07:34 +0700
4459-->
4460     <para>
4461      Avoid potential use-after-free in parallel vacuum (Kevin Oommen
4462      Anish)
4463      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;76613b539">&sect;</ulink>
4464     </para>
4465
4466     <para>
4467      This bug seems to have no consequences in standard builds, but it's
4468      theoretically a hazard.
4469     </para>
4470    </listitem>
4471
4472    <listitem>
4473<!--
4474Author: Noah Misch <noah@leadboat.com>
4475Branch: master [a95393ecd] 2025-09-29 11:15:44 -0700
4476Branch: REL_18_STABLE [d024160ff] 2025-09-29 11:15:47 -0700
4477Branch: REL_17_STABLE [6778fbca6] 2025-09-29 11:15:48 -0700
4478Branch: REL_16_STABLE [ab16418ee] 2025-09-29 11:15:48 -0700
4479Branch: REL_15_STABLE [d202ec1fb] 2025-09-29 11:15:48 -0700
4480Branch: REL_14_STABLE [5816baa9b] 2025-09-29 11:15:49 -0700
4481Branch: REL_13_STABLE [afb2cce74] 2025-09-29 11:15:49 -0700
4482-->
4483     <para>
4484      Fix visibility checking for statistics objects
4485      in <literal>pg_temp</literal> (Noah Misch)
4486      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d024160ff">&sect;</ulink>
4487     </para>
4488
4489     <para>
4490      A statistics object located in a temporary schema cannot be named
4491      without schema qualification,
4492      but <function>pg_statistics_obj_is_visible()</function> missed that
4493      memo and could return <quote>true</quote> regardless.  In turn,
4494      functions such as <function>pg_describe_object()</function> could
4495      fail to schema-qualify the object's name as expected.
4496     </para>
4497    </listitem>
4498
4499    <listitem>
4500<!--
4501Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@kurilemu.de>
4502Branch: master [ff47f9c16] 2025-10-11 16:39:22 +0200
4503Branch: REL_18_STABLE [33e7b4a7c] 2025-10-11 16:39:22 +0200
4504Branch: REL_17_STABLE [f9993ac64] 2025-10-11 16:39:22 +0200
4505Branch: REL_16_STABLE [c72b5c536] 2025-10-11 16:39:22 +0200
4506Branch: REL_15_STABLE [23b316c36] 2025-10-11 16:39:22 +0200
4507-->
4508     <para>
4509      Fix minor memory leak during WAL replay of database creation
4510      (Nathan Bossart)
4511      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;33e7b4a7c">&sect;</ulink>
4512     </para>
4513    </listitem>
4514
4515    <listitem>
4516<!--
4517Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
4518Branch: master [883a95646] 2025-10-22 11:27:15 +0900
4519Branch: REL_18_STABLE [9670032cc] 2025-10-22 11:28:48 +0900
4520Branch: REL_17_STABLE [62d5ee75b] 2025-10-22 11:28:57 +0900
4521Branch: REL_16_STABLE [2e55cf4ef] 2025-10-22 11:29:02 +0900
4522Branch: REL_15_STABLE [59b215f72] 2025-10-22 11:29:07 +0900
4523Branch: REL_14_STABLE [a7ab6ce6a] 2025-10-22 11:29:13 +0900
4524Branch: REL_13_STABLE [7d7ebdb80] 2025-10-22 11:29:18 +0900
4525-->
4526     <para>
4527      Fix incorrect reporting of replication lag
4528      in <structname>pg_stat_replication</structname> view (Fujii Masao)
4529      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9670032cc">&sect;</ulink>
4530     </para>
4531
4532     <para>
4533      If any standby server's replay LSN stopped advancing,
4534      the <structfield>write_lag</structfield>
4535      and <structfield>flush_lag</structfield> columns would eventually
4536      stop updating.
4537     </para>
4538    </listitem>
4539
4540    <listitem>
4541<!--
4542Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
4543Branch: master [f33e60a53] 2025-10-22 20:09:43 +0900
4544Branch: REL_18_STABLE [6ff7ba9fe] 2025-10-22 20:10:58 +0900
4545Branch: REL_17_STABLE [1db2870bb] 2025-10-22 20:11:47 +0900
4546Branch: REL_16_STABLE [4fd916eab] 2025-10-22 20:12:36 +0900
4547Branch: REL_15_STABLE [caf529aba] 2025-10-22 20:13:15 +0900
4548Branch: REL_14_STABLE [f106f53df] 2025-10-22 20:14:02 +0900
4549Branch: REL_13_STABLE [b00a16bae] 2025-10-22 20:14:07 +0900
4550-->
4551     <para>
4552      Avoid duplicative log messages about
4553      invalid <varname>primary_slot_name</varname> settings (Fujii Masao)
4554      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6ff7ba9fe">&sect;</ulink>
4555     </para>
4556    </listitem>
4557
4558    <listitem>
4559<!--
4560Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
4561Branch: master [e0dc4bbfb] 2025-10-27 06:48:32 +0000
4562Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b45a8d7d8] 2025-10-27 06:37:35 +0000
4563Branch: REL_17_STABLE [0024f5a10] 2025-10-27 06:34:29 +0000
4564-->
4565     <para>
4566      Avoid failures when <varname>synchronized_standby_slots</varname>
4567      references nonexistent replication slots (Shlok Kyal)
4568      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b45a8d7d8">&sect;</ulink>
4569     </para>
4570    </listitem>
4571
4572    <listitem>
4573<!--
4574Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
4575Branch: master [912af1c7e] 2025-10-10 09:23:59 +0900
4576Branch: REL_18_STABLE [9a6ea00ac] 2025-10-10 09:24:48 +0900
4577Branch: REL_17_STABLE [42348839d] 2025-10-10 09:24:50 +0900
4578Branch: REL_16_STABLE [bfdd1a12d] 2025-10-10 09:24:53 +0900
4579Branch: REL_15_STABLE [0adf424b4] 2025-10-10 09:24:54 +0900
4580Branch: REL_14_STABLE [f57f2760e] 2025-10-10 09:24:57 +0900
4581Branch: REL_13_STABLE [c878d9808] 2025-10-10 09:24:59 +0900
4582-->
4583     <para>
4584      Remove the unfinished slot state file after failing to write a
4585      replication slot's state to disk (Michael Paquier)
4586      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9a6ea00ac">&sect;</ulink>
4587     </para>
4588
4589     <para>
4590      Previously, a failure such as out-of-disk-space resulted in leaving
4591      a temporary <filename>state.tmp</filename> file behind.  That's
4592      problematic because it would block all subsequent attempts to
4593      write the state, requiring manual intervention to clean up.
4594     </para>
4595    </listitem>
4596
4597    <listitem>
4598<!--
4599Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
4600Branch: master [e41d954da] 2025-09-24 04:11:53 +0000
4601Branch: REL_18_STABLE [37fc5de43] 2025-09-24 04:00:15 +0000
4602Branch: REL_17_STABLE [2f6e1a490] 2025-09-24 03:49:02 +0000
4603Branch: REL_16_STABLE [a54c7a113] 2025-09-24 03:38:27 +0000
4604-->
4605     <para>
4606      Fix mishandling of lock timeout signals in parallel apply workers
4607      for logical replication (Hayato Kuroda)
4608      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;37fc5de43">&sect;</ulink>
4609     </para>
4610
4611     <para>
4612      The same signal number was being used for both worker shutdown and
4613      lock timeout, leading to confusion.
4614     </para>
4615    </listitem>
4616
4617    <listitem>
4618<!--
4619Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
4620Branch: master [17b2d5ec7] 2025-11-04 10:47:38 +0900
4621Branch: REL_18_STABLE [a14201073] 2025-11-04 10:52:33 +0900
4622Branch: REL_17_STABLE [e7340b484] 2025-11-04 10:52:35 +0900
4623Branch: REL_16_STABLE [9b6109607] 2025-11-04 10:52:38 +0900
4624Branch: REL_15_STABLE [da5ea6c70] 2025-11-04 10:52:41 +0900
4625Branch: REL_14_STABLE [a1b8b3b2e] 2025-11-04 10:52:42 +0900
4626Branch: REL_13_STABLE [25b484080] 2025-11-04 10:52:44 +0900
4627-->
4628     <para>
4629      Avoid unwanted WAL receiver shutdown when switching from streaming
4630      to archive WAL source (Xuneng Zhou)
4631      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a14201073">&sect;</ulink>
4632     </para>
4633
4634     <para>
4635      During a timeline change, a standby server's WAL receiver should
4636      remain alive, waiting for a new WAL streaming start point.  Instead
4637      it was repeatedly shutting down and immediately getting restarted,
4638      which could confuse status monitoring code.
4639     </para>
4640    </listitem>
4641
4642    <listitem>
4643<!--
4644Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
4645Branch: master [b46efe904] 2025-10-09 10:59:27 -0700
4646Branch: REL_18_STABLE [32b95fc71] 2025-10-09 10:59:29 -0700
4647Branch: REL_17_STABLE [a61592253] 2025-10-09 10:59:31 -0700
4648Branch: REL_16_STABLE [b07682bce] 2025-10-09 10:59:34 -0700
4649Branch: REL_15_STABLE [c40761759] 2025-10-09 10:59:36 -0700
4650-->
4651     <para>
4652      Fix use-after-free issue in the relation synchronization cache
4653      maintained by the <application>pgoutput</application> logical
4654      decoding plugin (Vignesh C, Masahiko Sawada)
4655      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;32b95fc71">&sect;</ulink>
4656     </para>
4657
4658     <para>
4659      An error during logical decoding could result in crashes in
4660      subsequent logical decoding attempts in the same session.
4661      The case is only reachable when <application>pgoutput</application>
4662      is invoked via SQL functions.
4663     </para>
4664    </listitem>
4665
4666    <listitem>
4667<!--
4668Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
4669Branch: master [d43209468] 2025-10-30 13:13:28 +0900
4670Branch: REL_18_STABLE [bf3dba508] 2025-10-30 13:13:31 +0900
4671Branch: REL_17_STABLE [f3fb6bc9f] 2025-10-30 13:13:34 +0900
4672Branch: REL_16_STABLE [e3714dc05] 2025-10-30 13:13:37 +0900
4673-->
4674     <para>
4675      Avoid unnecessary invalidation of logical replication slots
4676      (Bertrand Drouvot)
4677      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bf3dba508">&sect;</ulink>
4678     </para>
4679    </listitem>
4680
4681    <listitem>
4682<!--
4683Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
4684Branch: master [d115de9d8] 2025-11-04 16:48:16 -0800
4685Branch: REL_18_STABLE [3ebea75f9] 2025-11-04 16:49:00 -0800
4686-->
4687     <para>
4688      Re-establish special case for <literal>C</literal> collation in
4689      locale setup (Jeff Davis)
4690      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3ebea75f9">&sect;</ulink>
4691     </para>
4692
4693     <para>
4694      This fixes a regression in access to shared catalogs early in
4695      backend startup, before a database has been selected.  It is not
4696      known to be a problem for any
4697      core <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> code, but some extensions
4698      were broken.
4699     </para>
4700    </listitem>
4701
4702    <listitem>
4703<!--
4704Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
4705Branch: master [03bf7a12c] 2025-10-13 17:56:45 -0400
4706Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b48ae226e] 2025-10-13 17:56:45 -0400
4707Branch: REL_17_STABLE [4c53519e1] 2025-10-13 17:56:45 -0400
4708Branch: REL_16_STABLE [9883e3cd1] 2025-10-13 17:56:45 -0400
4709Branch: REL_15_STABLE [f91666c83] 2025-10-13 17:56:45 -0400
4710Branch: REL_14_STABLE [e91eb4ad6] 2025-10-13 17:56:45 -0400
4711Branch: REL_13_STABLE [75a555d61] 2025-10-13 17:56:45 -0400
4712-->
4713     <para>
4714      Fix incorrect printing of messages about failures in checking
4715      whether the user has Windows administrator privilege (Bryan Green)
4716      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b48ae226e">&sect;</ulink>
4717     </para>
4718
4719     <para>
4720      This code would have crashed or at least printed garbage.
4721      No such cases have been reported though, indicating that failure of
4722      these system calls is extremely rare.
4723     </para>
4724    </listitem>
4725
4726    <listitem>
4727<!--
4728Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
4729Branch: master [d4baa327a] 2025-11-05 11:09:45 -0500
4730Branch: REL_18_STABLE [6d8acb777] 2025-11-05 11:09:30 -0500
4731Branch: REL_17_STABLE [a9515f294] 2025-11-05 11:09:30 -0500
4732Branch: REL_16_STABLE [c775bf048] 2025-11-05 11:09:30 -0500
4733-->
4734     <para>
4735      Avoid crash when attempting to
4736      test <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> with certain libsanitizer
4737      options (Emmanuel Sibi, Jacob Champion)
4738      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6d8acb777">&sect;</ulink>
4739     </para>
4740    </listitem>
4741
4742    <listitem>
4743<!--
4744Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
4745Branch: master [50eb4e118] 2025-10-30 14:48:10 +1300
4746Branch: REL_18_STABLE [af3a79e08] 2025-10-30 14:49:07 +1300
4747Branch: REL_17_STABLE [bd6f986c9] 2025-10-30 14:49:42 +1300
4748Branch: REL_16_STABLE [cdc04a6c3] 2025-10-30 14:50:05 +1300
4749Branch: REL_15_STABLE [f3420e006] 2025-10-30 14:50:26 +1300
4750Branch: REL_14_STABLE [86bd9497f] 2025-10-30 14:50:46 +1300
4751Branch: REL_13_STABLE [7b955c227] 2025-10-30 14:51:08 +1300
4752-->
4753     <para>
4754      Fix false memory-context-checking warnings in debug builds
4755      on 64-bit Windows (David Rowley)
4756      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;af3a79e08">&sect;</ulink>
4757     </para>
4758    </listitem>
4759
4760    <listitem>
4761<!--
4762Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
4763Branch: master [7504d2be9] 2025-09-27 14:29:41 -0400
4764Branch: REL_18_STABLE [78a284b0b] 2025-09-27 14:29:41 -0400
4765Branch: REL_17_STABLE [3fc9aa5b0] 2025-09-27 14:29:41 -0400
4766Branch: REL_16_STABLE [b7f6798c0] 2025-09-27 14:29:41 -0400
4767Branch: REL_15_STABLE [9ca79896a] 2025-09-27 14:29:41 -0400
4768Branch: REL_14_STABLE [0be39b4b1] 2025-09-27 14:29:41 -0400
4769-->
4770     <para>
4771      Correctly handle <literal>GROUP BY DISTINCT</literal> in PL/pgSQL
4772      assignment statements (Tom Lane)
4773      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;78a284b0b">&sect;</ulink>
4774     </para>
4775
4776     <para>
4777      The parser failed to record the <literal>DISTINCT</literal> option
4778      in this context, so that the command would act as if it were
4779      plain <literal>GROUP BY</literal>.
4780     </para>
4781    </listitem>
4782
4783    <listitem>
4784<!--
4785Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
4786Branch: master [798b19d27] 2025-10-23 11:47:46 -0400
4787Branch: REL_18_STABLE [447a794f6] 2025-10-23 11:47:46 -0400
4788Branch: REL_17_STABLE [fbc41a145] 2025-10-23 11:47:46 -0400
4789Branch: REL_16_STABLE [cbfd4d0f8] 2025-10-23 11:47:46 -0400
4790Branch: REL_15_STABLE [4cde73259] 2025-10-23 11:47:46 -0400
4791Branch: REL_14_STABLE [7fb8c9240] 2025-10-23 11:47:46 -0400
4792Branch: REL_13_STABLE [d90c92d1c] 2025-10-23 11:47:46 -0400
4793-->
4794     <para>
4795      Avoid leaking memory when handling a SQL error within PL/Python
4796      (Tom Lane)
4797      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;447a794f6">&sect;</ulink>
4798     </para>
4799
4800     <para>
4801      This fixes a session-lifespan memory leak introduced in our previous
4802      minor releases.
4803     </para>
4804    </listitem>
4805
4806    <listitem>
4807<!--
4808Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
4809Branch: master [ea78bd6d5] 2025-10-05 16:27:47 -0400
4810Branch: REL_18_STABLE [d83879a32] 2025-10-05 16:27:47 -0400
4811Branch: REL_17_STABLE [1c4671f7b] 2025-10-05 16:27:47 -0400
4812Branch: REL_16_STABLE [46c4478db] 2025-10-05 16:27:47 -0400
4813Branch: REL_15_STABLE [771b106d1] 2025-10-05 16:27:47 -0400
4814Branch: REL_14_STABLE [f46d77377] 2025-10-05 16:27:47 -0400
4815Branch: REL_13_STABLE [fc287cf3c] 2025-10-05 16:27:47 -0400
4816-->
4817     <para>
4818      Fix <application>libpq</application>'s handling of socket-related
4819      errors on Windows within its GSSAPI logic (Ning Wu, Tom Lane)
4820      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d83879a32">&sect;</ulink>
4821     </para>
4822
4823     <para>
4824      The code for encrypting/decrypting transmitted data using GSSAPI did
4825      not correctly recognize error conditions on the connection socket,
4826      since Windows reports those differently than other platforms.  This
4827      led to failure to make such connections on Windows.
4828     </para>
4829    </listitem>
4830
4831    <listitem>
4832<!--
4833Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@kurilemu.de>
4834Branch: master [615ff828e] 2025-10-18 18:18:19 +0200
4835Branch: REL_18_STABLE [0fe07fa11] 2025-10-18 18:18:19 +0200
4836Branch: REL_17_STABLE [c945b06d5] 2025-10-18 18:18:19 +0200
4837-->
4838     <para>
4839      Fix dumping of non-inherited not-null constraints on inherited table
4840      columns (Dilip Kumar)
4841      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0fe07fa11">&sect;</ulink>
4842     </para>
4843
4844     <para>
4845      <application>pg_dump</application> failed to preserve such
4846      constraints when dumping from a pre-v18 server.
4847     </para>
4848    </listitem>
4849
4850    <listitem>
4851<!--
4852Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@kurilemu.de>
4853Branch: master [4921a5972] 2025-10-18 17:50:10 +0200
4854Branch: REL_18_STABLE [162e70ea0] 2025-10-18 17:50:10 +0200
4855Branch: REL_17_STABLE [7419c99a2] 2025-10-18 17:50:10 +0200
4856Branch: REL_16_STABLE [06c1ee6b7] 2025-10-18 17:50:10 +0200
4857Branch: REL_15_STABLE [4cc3b4445] 2025-10-18 17:50:10 +0200
4858Branch: REL_14_STABLE [6062c3db3] 2025-10-18 17:50:10 +0200
4859Branch: REL_13_STABLE [d20df9590] 2025-10-18 17:50:10 +0200
4860-->
4861     <para>
4862      Fix <application>pg_dump</application>'s sorting of
4863      foreign key constraints (Álvaro Herrera)
4864      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;162e70ea0">&sect;</ulink>
4865     </para>
4866
4867     <para>
4868      Ensure consistent ordering of these database objects, as was
4869      already done for other object types.
4870     </para>
4871    </listitem>
4872
4873    <listitem>
4874<!--
4875Author: Daniel Gustafsson <dgustafsson@postgresql.org>
4876Branch: master [e686010c5] 2025-08-29 19:28:46 +0200
4877Branch: REL_18_STABLE Release: REL_18_0 [8980c724b] 2025-08-29 19:28:46 +0200
4878Branch: REL_17_STABLE [92268b35d] 2025-08-29 19:28:46 +0200
4879Branch: REL_16_STABLE [ec017a305] 2025-08-29 19:28:46 +0200
4880Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
4881Branch: master [a239c4a0c] 2025-10-13 12:44:20 -0400
4882Branch: REL_18_STABLE [6a4009747] 2025-10-13 12:44:20 -0400
4883Branch: REL_17_STABLE [bf18e9bd7] 2025-10-13 12:44:20 -0400
4884Branch: REL_16_STABLE [1518b7d76] 2025-10-13 12:44:20 -0400
4885Branch: master [277dec651] 2025-10-19 14:36:58 -0400
4886Branch: REL_18_STABLE [aa1fcd087] 2025-10-19 14:36:58 -0400
4887Branch: REL_17_STABLE [2efca1633] 2025-10-19 14:36:58 -0400
4888Branch: REL_16_STABLE [c865f5b9f] 2025-10-19 14:36:58 -0400
4889Branch: REL_15_STABLE [8b9924bce] 2025-10-19 14:36:58 -0400
4890Branch: REL_14_STABLE [40dc710a6] 2025-10-19 14:36:58 -0400
4891Branch: REL_13_STABLE [33276cd02] 2025-10-19 14:36:58 -0400
4892-->
4893     <para>
4894      Fix assorted errors in the data compression logic
4895      in <application>pg_dump</application>
4896      and <application>pg_restore</application>
4897      (Daniel Gustafsson, Tom Lane)
4898      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8980c724b">&sect;</ulink>
4899      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6a4009747">&sect;</ulink>
4900      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;aa1fcd087">&sect;</ulink>
4901     </para>
4902
4903     <para>
4904      Error checking was missing or incorrect in several places, and there
4905      were also portability issues that would manifest on big-endian
4906      hardware.  These problems had been missed because this code is only
4907      used to read compressed TOC files within directory-format
4908      dumps.   <application>pg_dump</application> never produces such a
4909      dump; the case can be reached only by manually compressing the TOC
4910      file after the fact, which is a supported thing to do but very
4911      uncommon.
4912     </para>
4913    </listitem>
4914
4915    <listitem>
4916<!--
4917Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
4918Branch: master [902c08887] 2025-10-03 14:03:55 +0900
4919Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c00637b5f] 2025-10-03 14:04:00 +0900
4920Branch: REL_17_STABLE [de6de069d] 2025-10-03 14:04:01 +0900
4921Branch: REL_16_STABLE [640590bb4] 2025-10-03 14:04:03 +0900
4922Branch: REL_15_STABLE [b5cefc197] 2025-10-03 14:04:05 +0900
4923Branch: REL_14_STABLE [9b3beb7c6] 2025-10-03 14:04:06 +0900
4924Branch: REL_13_STABLE [f1a2f3a0c] 2025-10-03 14:04:08 +0900
4925-->
4926     <para>
4927      Fix <application>pgbench</application> to error out cleanly if
4928      a <literal>COPY</literal> operation is started (Anthonin Bonnefoy)
4929      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c00637b5f">&sect;</ulink>
4930     </para>
4931
4932     <para>
4933      <application>pgbench</application> doesn't intend to support this
4934      case, but previously it went into an infinite loop.
4935     </para>
4936    </listitem>
4937
4938    <listitem>
4939<!--
4940Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
4941Branch: master [19d4f9ffc] 2025-09-30 23:52:28 +0900
4942Branch: REL_18_STABLE [29aabbc43] 2025-09-30 23:53:32 +0900
4943Branch: REL_17_STABLE [a912118c6] 2025-09-30 23:53:46 +0900
4944Branch: REL_16_STABLE [36c4d30c8] 2025-09-30 23:53:53 +0900
4945Branch: REL_15_STABLE [bdccb6302] 2025-09-30 23:54:01 +0900
4946Branch: REL_14_STABLE [cb21e1f92] 2025-09-30 23:55:02 +0900
4947Branch: REL_13_STABLE [7d7e81d1e] 2025-09-30 23:55:33 +0900
4948-->
4949     <para>
4950      Fix <application>pgbench</application>'s reporting of multiple
4951      errors (Yugo Nagata)
4952      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;29aabbc43">&sect;</ulink>
4953     </para>
4954
4955     <para>
4956      In cases where two successive <function>PQgetResult</function> calls
4957      both fail, <application>pgbench</application> might report the wrong
4958      error message.
4959     </para>
4960    </listitem>
4961
4962    <listitem>
4963<!--
4964Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
4965Branch: master [8bb174295] 2025-09-26 21:23:43 +0900
4966Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c736808e0] 2025-09-26 21:24:59 +0900
4967Branch: REL_17_STABLE [f39d9164b] 2025-09-26 21:25:05 +0900
4968Branch: REL_16_STABLE [8b2e290bd] 2025-09-26 21:25:11 +0900
4969Branch: REL_15_STABLE [704f51771] 2025-09-26 21:25:17 +0900
4970-->
4971     <para>
4972      In <application>pgbench</application>, fix faulty assertion about
4973      errors in pipeline mode (Yugo Nagata)
4974      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c736808e0">&sect;</ulink>
4975     </para>
4976    </listitem>
4977
4978    <listitem>
4979<!--
4980Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
4981Branch: master [9224c3025] 2025-10-22 13:38:40 -0400
4982Branch: REL_18_STABLE [e2072b47b] 2025-10-22 13:38:37 -0400
4983Branch: REL_17_STABLE [4eb6992af] 2025-10-22 13:38:37 -0400
4984-->
4985     <para>
4986      Fix per-file memory leakage
4987      in <application>pg_combinebackup</application> (Tom Lane)
4988      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e2072b47b">&sect;</ulink>
4989     </para>
4990    </listitem>
4991
4992    <listitem>
4993<!--
4994Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
4995Branch: master [8ae0f6a0c] 2025-11-04 15:47:25 -0800
4996Branch: REL_18_STABLE [71aa2e114] 2025-11-04 15:47:22 -0800
4997Branch: master [eab9e4e27] 2025-08-19 12:11:42 -0700
4998Branch: REL_18_STABLE Release: REL_18_0 [0beb7e933] 2025-08-19 12:11:39 -0700
4999Branch: REL_17_STABLE [b6090ed96] 2025-08-19 12:11:37 -0700
5000Branch: REL_16_STABLE [815fcfb20] 2025-08-19 12:11:34 -0700
5001Branch: REL_15_STABLE [eb9ee4d18] 2025-08-19 12:11:32 -0700
5002Branch: REL_14_STABLE [79643105a] 2025-08-19 12:11:29 -0700
5003Branch: REL_13_STABLE [7d8d36289] 2025-08-19 12:11:25 -0700
5004-->
5005     <para>
5006      Ensure that <filename>contrib/pg_buffercache</filename> functions
5007      can be canceled (Satyanarayana Narlapuram, Yuhang Qiu)
5008      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;71aa2e114">&sect;</ulink>
5009      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0beb7e933">&sect;</ulink>
5010     </para>
5011
5012     <para>
5013      Some code paths were capable of running for a long time without
5014      checking for interrupts.
5015     </para>
5016    </listitem>
5017
5018    <listitem>
5019<!--
5020Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
5021Branch: master [208927e65] 2025-10-17 11:36:50 -0500
5022Branch: REL_18_STABLE [3ccf8e9ac] 2025-10-17 11:36:50 -0500
5023Branch: REL_17_STABLE [a0551bc57] 2025-10-17 11:36:50 -0500
5024Branch: REL_16_STABLE [fae0ce5e3] 2025-10-17 11:36:50 -0500
5025Branch: REL_15_STABLE [6c03ae8d6] 2025-10-17 11:36:50 -0500
5026Branch: REL_14_STABLE [f146eb45c] 2025-10-17 11:36:50 -0500
5027Branch: REL_13_STABLE [19a64f567] 2025-10-17 11:36:50 -0500
5028Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
5029Branch: master [da44d71e7] 2025-10-18 18:36:21 -0400
5030Branch: REL_18_STABLE [c29d32d27] 2025-10-18 18:36:21 -0400
5031Branch: REL_17_STABLE [d4e8c37cc] 2025-10-18 18:36:21 -0400
5032Branch: REL_16_STABLE [c26a8eaf6] 2025-10-18 18:36:21 -0400
5033-->
5034     <para>
5035      Fix <filename>contrib/pg_prewarm</filename>'s privilege checks for
5036      indexes (Ayush Vatsa, Nathan Bossart)
5037      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3ccf8e9ac">&sect;</ulink>
5038      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c29d32d27">&sect;</ulink>
5039     </para>
5040
5041     <para>
5042      <function>pg_prewarm()</function> requires <literal>SELECT</literal>
5043      privilege on relations to be prewarmed.  However, since indexes have
5044      no SQL privileges of their own, this resulted in non-superusers
5045      being unable to prewarm indexes.  Instead, check
5046      for <literal>SELECT</literal> privilege on the index's table.
5047     </para>
5048    </listitem>
5049
5050    <listitem>
5051<!--
5052Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@kurilemu.de>
5053Branch: master [16edc1b94] 2025-10-29 12:35:02 +0100
5054Branch: REL_18_STABLE [b1635c166] 2025-10-29 12:35:02 +0100
5055-->
5056     <para>
5057      In <filename>contrib/pg_stat_statements</filename>, avoid
5058      crash when two or more constants are marked as having the same
5059      location in the SQL statement text (Sami Imseih, Dmitry Dolgov)
5060      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b1635c166">&sect;</ulink>
5061     </para>
5062    </listitem>
5063
5064    <listitem>
5065<!--
5066Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
5067Branch: master [684a745f5] 2025-10-02 11:07:30 +0900
5068Branch: REL_18_STABLE [fc295beb7] 2025-10-02 11:09:10 +0900
5069Branch: REL_17_STABLE [036decbba] 2025-10-02 11:09:12 +0900
5070Branch: REL_16_STABLE [c0f9fe877] 2025-10-02 11:09:13 +0900
5071Branch: REL_15_STABLE [49b5f0b53] 2025-10-02 11:09:15 +0900
5072Branch: REL_14_STABLE [ee2dbcad0] 2025-10-02 11:09:17 +0900
5073Branch: REL_13_STABLE [c207bf473] 2025-10-02 11:09:19 +0900
5074-->
5075     <para>
5076      Make <filename>contrib/pgstattuple</filename> more robust about
5077      empty or invalid index pages (Nitin Motiani)
5078      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;fc295beb7">&sect;</ulink>
5079     </para>
5080
5081     <para>
5082      Count all-zero pages as free space, and ignore pages that are
5083      invalid according to a check of the page's special-space size.
5084      The code for btree indexes already counted all-zero pages as free,
5085      but the hash and gist code would error out, which has been found to
5086      be much less user-friendly.  Similarly, make all three cases agree
5087      on ignoring corrupted pages rather than throwing errors.
5088     </para>
5089    </listitem>
5090
5091    <listitem>
5092<!--
5093Author: Thomas Munro <tmunro@postgresql.org>
5094Branch: master [c5d34f4a5] 2025-11-08 12:26:43 +1300
5095Branch: REL_18_STABLE [f8ccab0e9] 2025-11-08 12:28:15 +1300
5096Branch: REL_17_STABLE [03d9140cb] 2025-11-08 12:28:52 +1300
5097Branch: REL_16_STABLE [2f76ffe5e] 2025-11-08 12:29:15 +1300
5098Branch: REL_15_STABLE [1c7cba4c5] 2025-11-08 12:30:08 +1300
5099Branch: REL_14_STABLE [d8ba910b0] 2025-11-08 12:32:42 +1300
5100Branch: REL_13_STABLE [77b5b2c6f] 2025-11-08 12:33:01 +1300
5101-->
5102     <para>
5103      Harden our read and write barrier macros to satisfy Clang
5104      (Thomas Munro)
5105      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f8ccab0e9">&sect;</ulink>
5106     </para>
5107
5108     <para>
5109      We supposed that <function>__atomic_thread_fence()</function> is a
5110      sufficient barrier to prevent the C compiler from re-ordering memory
5111      accesses around it, but it appears that that's not true for Clang,
5112      allowing it to generate incorrect code for at least RISC-V, MIPS,
5113      and LoongArch machines.  Add explicit compiler barriers to fix that.
5114     </para>
5115    </listitem>
5116
5117    <listitem>
5118<!--
5119Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@kurilemu.de>
5120Branch: master [6ad9378c9] 2025-10-16 20:21:05 +0200
5121Branch: REL_18_STABLE [6aa04a60c] 2025-10-16 20:21:05 +0200
5122Branch: REL_17_STABLE [a8933194e] 2025-10-16 20:21:05 +0200
5123Branch: REL_16_STABLE [a506b0c0a] 2025-10-16 20:21:05 +0200
5124Branch: REL_15_STABLE [33202cba8] 2025-10-16 20:21:05 +0200
5125Branch: REL_14_STABLE [56cd332f8] 2025-10-16 20:21:05 +0200
5126Branch: REL_13_STABLE [f768f5a7d] 2025-10-16 20:21:05 +0200
5127-->
5128     <para>
5129      Fix PGXS build infrastructure to support building
5130      NLS <filename>po</filename> files for extensions (Ryo Matsumura)
5131      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6aa04a60c">&sect;</ulink>
5132     </para>
5133    </listitem>
5134
5135   </itemizedlist>
5136
5137  </sect2>
5138 </sect1>
5139
5140 <sect1 id="release-18">
5141  <title>Release 18</title>
5142
5143  <formalpara>
5144   <title>Release date:</title>
5145   <para>2025-09-25</para>
5146  </formalpara>
5147
5148  <sect2 id="release-18-highlights">
5149   <title>Overview</title>
5150
5151   <para>
5152    <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> 18 contains many new features
5153    and enhancements, including:
5154   </para>
5155
5156   <itemizedlist>
5157
5158    <listitem>
5159     <para>
5160      An asynchronous I/O (AIO) subsystem that can improve performance of
5161      sequential scans, bitmap heap scans, vacuums, and other operations.
5162     </para>
5163    </listitem>
5164
5165    <listitem>
5166     <para>
5167      <link linkend="pgupgrade"><application>pg_upgrade</application></link>
5168      now retains optimizer statistics.
5169     </para>
5170    </listitem>
5171
5172    <listitem>
5173     <para>
5174      Support for "skip scan" lookups that allow using
5175      <link linkend="indexes-multicolumn">multicolumn B-tree indexes</link> in
5176      more cases.
5177     </para>
5178    </listitem>
5179
5180    <listitem>
5181     <para>
5182      <link linkend="func_uuid_gen_table"><function>uuidv7()</function></link>
5183      function for generating timestamp-ordered
5184      <link linkend="datatype-uuid">UUIDs</link>.
5185     </para>
5186    </listitem>
5187
5188    <listitem>
5189     <para>
5190      Virtual
5191      <link linkend="sql-createtable-parms-generated-stored">generated columns</link>
5192      that compute their values during read operations.  This is now the
5193      default for generated columns.
5194     </para>
5195    </listitem>
5196
5197    <listitem>
5198     <para>
5199      <link linkend="auth-oauth">OAuth authentication</link> support.
5200     </para>
5201    </listitem>
5202
5203    <listitem>
5204     <para>
5205      <literal>OLD</literal> and <literal>NEW</literal> support for
5206      <link linkend="dml-returning"><literal>RETURNING</literal></link> clauses
5207      in <xref linkend="sql-insert"/>, <xref linkend="sql-update"/>,
5208      <xref linkend="sql-delete"/>, and <xref linkend="sql-merge"/> commands.
5209     </para>
5210    </listitem>
5211
5212    <listitem>
5213     <para>
5214      Temporal constraints, or constraints over ranges, for
5215      <link linkend="sql-createtable-parms-primary-key">PRIMARY KEY</link>,
5216      <link linkend="sql-createtable-parms-unique">UNIQUE</link>, and
5217      <link linkend="sql-createtable-parms-references">FOREIGN KEY</link>
5218      constraints.
5219     </para>
5220    </listitem>
5221   </itemizedlist>
5222
5223   <para>
5224    The above items and other new features of
5225    <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> 18 are explained in more detail
5226    in the sections below.
5227   </para>
5228
5229  </sect2>
5230
5231  <sect2  id="release-18-migration">
5232
5233   <title>Migration to Version 18</title>
5234
5235   <para>
5236    A dump/restore using <xref linkend="app-pg-dumpall"/> or use of
5237    <xref linkend="pgupgrade"/> or logical replication is required for
5238    those wishing to migrate data from any previous release.  See <xref
5239    linkend="upgrading"/> for general information on migrating to new
5240    major releases.
5241   </para>
5242
5243   <para>
5244    Version 18 contains a number of changes that may affect compatibility
5245    with previous releases.  Observe the following incompatibilities:
5246   </para>
5247
5248   <itemizedlist>
5249
5250<!--
5251Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
52522024-10-16 [04bec894a04] initdb: Change default to using data checksums.
5253-->
5254
5255    <listitem>
5256    <para>
5257    Change <xref linkend="app-initdb"/> default to enable data checksums
5258    (Greg Sabino Mullane)
5259    <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;04bec894a04">&sect;</ulink>
5260    </para>
5261
5262    <para>
5263    Checksums can be disabled with the
5264    new <application>initdb</application> option
5265    <option>--no-data-checksums</option>.  <xref linkend="pgupgrade"/>
5266    requires matching cluster checksum settings, so this new option can
5267    be useful to upgrade non-checksum old clusters.
5268    </para>
5269    </listitem>
5270
5271<!--
5272Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
52732025-01-16 [d7674c9fa] Seek zone abbreviations in the IANA data before timezone
5274-->
5275
5276    <listitem>
5277    <para>
5278    Change time zone abbreviation handling (Tom Lane)
5279    <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d7674c9fa">&sect;</ulink>
5280    </para>
5281
5282    <para>
5283    The system will now favor the current session's time
5284    zone abbreviations before checking the server variable
5285    <xref linkend="guc-timezone-abbreviations"/>. Previously
5286    <varname>timezone_abbreviations</varname> was checked first.
5287    </para>
5288    </listitem>
5289
5290<!--
5291Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
52922024-12-02 [db6a4a985] Deprecate MD5 passwords.
5293-->
5294
5295    <listitem>
5296    <para>
5297    Deprecate <link linkend="auth-password">MD5 password</link>
5298    authentication (Nathan Bossart)
5299    <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;db6a4a985">&sect;</ulink>
5300    </para>
5301
5302    <para>
5303    Support for MD5 passwords will be removed in a future major
5304    version release.  <xref linkend="sql-createrole"/> and <xref
5305    linkend="sql-alterrole"/> now emit deprecation warnings when
5306    setting MD5 passwords.  These warnings can be disabled by setting
5307    the <xref linkend="guc-md5-password-warnings"/> parameter to
5308    <literal>off</literal>.
5309    </para>
5310    </listitem>
5311
5312<!--
5313Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
53142024-09-24 [62ddf7ee9] Add ONLY support for VACUUM and ANALYZE
5315-->
5316
5317    <listitem>
5318    <para>
5319    Change <xref linkend="sql-vacuum"/> and <xref linkend="sql-analyze"/>
5320    to process the inheritance children of a parent (Michael Harris)
5321    <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;62ddf7ee9">&sect;</ulink>
5322    </para>
5323
5324    <para>
5325    The previous behavior can be performed by using the new
5326    <literal>ONLY</literal> option.
5327    </para>
5328    </listitem>
5329
5330<!--
5331Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
53322024-09-30 [770233748] Do not treat \. as an EOF marker in CSV mode for COPY IN
5333Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
53342024-10-01 [da8a4c166] Reject a copy EOF marker that has data ahead of it on th
5335-->
5336
5337    <listitem>
5338    <para>
5339    Prevent <link linkend="sql-copy"><command>COPY FROM</command></link>
5340    from treating <literal>\.</literal> as an end-of-file marker when
5341    reading <acronym>CSV</acronym> files (Daniel Vérité, Tom Lane)
5342    <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;770233748">&sect;</ulink>
5343    <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;da8a4c166">&sect;</ulink>
5344    </para>
5345
5346    <para>
5347    <xref linkend="app-psql"/> will still treat
5348    <literal>\.</literal> as an end-of-file marker when reading
5349    <acronym>CSV</acronym> files from <literal>STDIN</literal>.
5350    Older <application>psql</application> clients connecting to
5351    <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> 18 servers might experience <link
5352    linkend="app-psql-meta-commands-copy"><literal>\copy</literal></link>
5353    problems.  This release also enforces that <literal>\.</literal>
5354    must appear alone on a line.
5355    </para>
5356    </listitem>
5357
5358<!--
5359Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
53602024-10-03 [e2bab2d79] Remove support for unlogged on partitioned tables
5361-->
5362
5363    <listitem>
5364    <para>
5365    Disallow unlogged partitioned tables (Michael Paquier)
5366    <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e2bab2d79">&sect;</ulink>
5367    </para>
5368
5369    <para>
5370    Previously <link linkend="sql-altertable"><command>ALTER TABLE SET
5371    [UN]LOGGED</command></link> did nothing, and the creation of an
5372    unlogged partitioned table did not cause its children to be unlogged.
5373    </para>
5374    </listitem>
5375
5376<!--
5377Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
53782025-01-23 [01463e1cc] Ensure that AFTER triggers run as the instigating user.
5379-->
5380
5381    <listitem>
5382    <para>
5383    Execute <literal>AFTER</literal> <link
5384    linkend="triggers">triggers</link> as the role that was active when
5385    trigger events were queued (Laurenz Albe)
5386    <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;01463e1cc">&sect;</ulink>
5387    </para>
5388
5389    <para>
5390    Previously such triggers were run as the role that was active at
5391    trigger execution time (e.g., at <xref linkend="sql-commit"/>).
5392    This is significant for cases where the role is changed between queue
5393    time and transaction commit.
5394    </para>
5395    </listitem>
5396
5397<!--
5398Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
53992024-09-12 [fefa76f70] Remove old RULE privilege completely.
5400-->
5401
5402    <listitem>
5403    <para>
5404    Remove non-functional support for rule privileges in <xref
5405    linkend="sql-grant"/>/<xref linkend="sql-revoke"/> (Fujii Masao)
5406    <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;fefa76f70">&sect;</ulink>
5407    </para>
5408
5409    <para>
5410    These have been non-functional since
5411    <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> 8.2.
5412    </para>
5413    </listitem>
5414
5415<!--
5416Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
54172024-08-12 [f0d112759] Remove "parent" column from pg_backend_memory_contexts
5418-->
5419
5420    <listitem>
5421    <para>
5422    Remove column <link
5423    linkend="view-pg-backend-memory-contexts"><structname>pg_backend_memory_contexts</structname></link>.<structfield>parent</structfield>
5424    (Melih Mutlu)
5425    <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f0d112759">&sect;</ulink>
5426    </para>
5427
5428    <para>
5429    This is no longer needed since
5430    <structname>pg_backend_memory_contexts</structname>.<structfield>path</structfield>
5431    was added.
5432    </para>
5433    </listitem>
5434
5435<!--
5436Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
54372024-07-25 [32d3ed816] Add path column to pg_backend_memory_contexts view
5438Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
54392025-04-18 [d9e03864b] Make levels 1-based in
5440    pg_log_backend_memory_contexts()
5441Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
54422025-04-21 [706cbed35] doc: Fix memory context level in pg_log_backend_memory_c
5443-->
5444
5445    <listitem>
5446    <para>
5447    Change
5448    <structname>pg_backend_memory_contexts</structname>.<structfield>level</structfield>
5449    and <link
5450    linkend="functions-admin-signal-table"><function>pg_log_backend_memory_contexts()</function></link>
5451    to be one-based (Melih Mutlu, Atsushi Torikoshi, David Rowley,
5452    Fujii Masao)
5453    <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;32d3ed816">&sect;</ulink>
5454    <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d9e03864b">&sect;</ulink>
5455    <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;706cbed35">&sect;</ulink>
5456    </para>
5457
5458    <para>
5459    These were previously zero-based.
5460    </para>
5461    </listitem>
5462
5463<!--
5464Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
54652024-12-17 [fb1a18810f0] Remove ts_locale.c's lowerstr()
5466-->
5467
5468    <listitem>
5469    <para>
5470    Change <link linkend="textsearch">full text search</link> to use the
5471    default collation provider of the cluster to read configuration files
5472    and dictionaries, rather than always using libc (Peter Eisentraut)
5473    <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;fb1a18810f0">&sect;</ulink>
5474    </para>
5475
5476    <para>
5477    Clusters that default to non-libc collation providers (e.g., ICU,
5478    builtin) that behave differently than libc for characters processed
5479    by LC_CTYPE could observe changes in behavior of some full-text
5480    search functions, as well as the <xref linkend="pgtrgm"/> extension.
5481    When upgrading such clusters using <xref linkend="pgupgrade"/>, it
5482    is recommended to reindex all indexes related to full-text search
5483    and <application>pg_trgm</application> after the upgrade.
5484    </para>
5485    </listitem>
5486
5487   </itemizedlist>
5488
5489  </sect2>
5490
5491  <sect2 id="release-18-changes">
5492   <title>Changes</title>
5493
5494    <para>
5495     Below you will find a detailed account of the changes between
5496    <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> 18 and the previous major
5497    release.
5498    </para>
5499
5500   <sect3 id="release-18-server">
5501    <title>Server</title>
5502
5503    <sect4 id="release-18-optimizer">
5504     <title>Optimizer</title>
5505
5506     <itemizedlist>
5507
5508<!--
5509Author: Alexander Korotkov <akorotkov@postgresql.org>
55102025-02-17 [fc069a3a6] Implement Self-Join Elimination
5511-->
5512
5513      <listitem>
5514      <para>
5515      Automatically remove some unnecessary table self-joins (Andrey
5516      Lepikhov, Alexander Kuzmenkov, Alexander Korotkov, Alena Rybakina)
5517      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;fc069a3a6">&sect;</ulink>
5518      </para>
5519
5520      <para>
5521      This optimization can be disabled using server variable <xref
5522      linkend="guc-enable-self-join-elimination"/>.
5523      </para>
5524      </listitem>
5525
5526<!--
5527Author: Alexander Korotkov <akorotkov@postgresql.org>
55282025-04-04 [c0962a113] Convert 'x IN (VALUES ...)' to 'x = ANY ...' then approp
5529-->
5530
5531      <listitem>
5532      <para>
5533      Convert some <link
5534      linkend="functions-comparisons-in-scalar"><literal>IN (VALUES
5535      ...)</literal></link> to <literal>x = ANY ...</literal> for better
5536      optimizer statistics (Alena Rybakina, Andrei Lepikhov)
5537      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c0962a113">&sect;</ulink>
5538      </para>
5539      </listitem>
5540
5541<!--
5542Author: Alexander Korotkov <akorotkov@postgresql.org>
55432024-11-24 [ae4569161] Teach bitmap path generation about transforming OR-claus
5544-->
5545
5546      <listitem>
5547      <para>
5548      Allow transforming <link
5549      linkend="functions-logical"><literal>OR</literal></link>-clauses
5550      to arrays for faster index processing (Alexander Korotkov, Andrey
5551      Lepikhov)
5552      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ae4569161">&sect;</ulink>
5553      </para>
5554      </listitem>
5555
5556<!--
5557Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
55582024-09-10 [52c707483] Use a hash table to de-duplicate column names in ruleuti
5559Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
55602024-12-19 [276279295] Convert SetOp to read its inputs as outerPlan and innerP
5561Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
55622024-12-19 [8d96f57d5] Improve planner's handling of SetOp plans.
5563Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
55642024-09-05 [908a96861] Optimize WindowAgg's use of tuplestores
5565-->
5566
5567      <listitem>
5568      <para>
5569      Speed up the processing of <link
5570      linkend="sql-intersect"><literal>INTERSECT</literal></link>,
5571      <link linkend="sql-except"><literal>EXCEPT</literal></link>, <link
5572      linkend="tutorial-window">window aggregates</link>, and <link
5573      linkend="sql-createview">view column aliases</link> (Tom Lane,
5574      David Rowley)
5575      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;52c707483">&sect;</ulink>
5576      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;276279295">&sect;</ulink>
5577      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8d96f57d5">&sect;</ulink>
5578      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;908a96861">&sect;</ulink>
5579      </para>
5580      </listitem>
5581
5582<!--
5583Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
55842024-11-26 [a8ccf4e93] Reordering DISTINCT keys to match input path's pathkeys
5585-->
5586
5587      <listitem>
5588      <para>
5589      Allow the keys of <link linkend="sql-distinct"><command>SELECT
5590      DISTINCT</command></link> to be internally reordered to avoid sorting
5591      (Richard Guo)
5592      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a8ccf4e93">&sect;</ulink>
5593      </para>
5594
5595      <para>
5596      This optimization can be disabled using <xref
5597      linkend="guc-enable-distinct-reordering"/>.
5598      </para>
5599      </listitem>
5600
5601<!--
5602Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
56032024-12-12 [bd10ec529] Detect redundant GROUP BY columns using UNIQUE indexes
5604-->
5605
5606      <listitem>
5607      <para>
5608      Ignore <link linkend="sql-groupby"><literal>GROUP BY</literal></link>
5609      columns that are functionally dependent on other columns (Zhang
5610      Mingli, Jian He, David Rowley)
5611      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bd10ec529">&sect;</ulink>
5612      </para>
5613
5614      <para>
5615      If a <literal>GROUP BY</literal> clause includes all columns of
5616      a unique index, as well as other columns of the same table, those
5617      other columns are redundant and can be dropped from the grouping.
5618      This was already true for non-deferred primary keys.
5619      </para>
5620      </listitem>
5621
5622<!--
5623Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
56242024-10-09 [67a54b9e8] Allow pushdown of HAVING clauses with grouping sets
5625Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
56262024-09-10 [247dea89f] Introduce an RTE for the grouping step
5627Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
56282024-09-10 [f5050f795] Mark expressions nullable by grouping sets
5629Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
56302025-03-13 [cc5d98525] Fix incorrect handling of subquery pullup
5631-->
5632
5633      <listitem>
5634      <para>
5635      Allow some <link
5636      linkend="sql-having"><literal>HAVING</literal></link> clauses
5637      on <link linkend="queries-grouping-sets"><literal>GROUPING
5638      SETS</literal></link> to be pushed to <link
5639      linkend="sql-where"><literal>WHERE</literal></link> clauses
5640      (Richard Guo)
5641      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;67a54b9e8">&sect;</ulink>
5642      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;247dea89f">&sect;</ulink>
5643      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f5050f795">&sect;</ulink>
5644      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cc5d98525">&sect;</ulink>
5645      </para>
5646
5647      <para>
5648      This allows earlier row filtering.  This release also fixes some
5649      <literal>GROUPING SETS</literal> queries that used to return
5650      incorrect results.
5651      </para>
5652      </listitem>
5653
5654<!--
5655Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
56562024-07-09 [036bdcec9] Teach planner how to estimate rows for timestamp generat
5657Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
56582024-12-02 [97173536e] Add a planner support function for numeric generate_seri
5659-->
5660
5661      <listitem>
5662      <para>
5663      Improve row estimates for <link
5664      linkend="functions-srf-series"><function>generate_series()</function></link>
5665      using <link linkend="datatype-numeric"><type>numeric</type></link>
5666      and <link linkend="datatype-datetime"><type>timestamp</type></link>
5667      values (David Rowley, Song Jinzhou)
5668      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;036bdcec9">&sect;</ulink>
5669      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;97173536e">&sect;</ulink>
5670      </para>
5671      </listitem>
5672
5673<!--
5674Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
56752024-07-05 [aa86129e1] Support "Right Semi Join" plan shapes
5676-->
5677
5678      <listitem>
5679      <para>
5680      Allow the optimizer to use <literal>Right Semi Join</literal> plans
5681      (Richard Guo)
5682      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;aa86129e1">&sect;</ulink>
5683      </para>
5684
5685      <para>
5686      Semi-joins are used when needing to find if there is at least
5687      one match.
5688      </para>
5689      </listitem>
5690
5691<!--
5692Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
56932024-10-09 [828e94c9d] Consider explicit incremental sort for mergejoins
5694-->
5695
5696      <listitem>
5697      <para>
5698      Allow merge joins to use <link
5699      linkend="guc-enable-incremental-sort">incremental sorts</link>
5700      (Richard Guo)
5701      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;828e94c9d">&sect;</ulink>
5702      </para>
5703      </listitem>
5704
5705<!--
5706Author: Amit Langote <amitlan@postgresql.org>
57072025-04-04 [88f55bc97] Make derived clause lookup in EquivalenceClass more effi
5708Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
57092025-04-08 [d69d45a5a] Speedup child EquivalenceMember lookup in planner
5710-->
5711
5712      <listitem>
5713      <para>
5714      Improve the efficiency of planning queries accessing many partitions
5715      (Ashutosh Bapat, Yuya Watari, David Rowley)
5716      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;88f55bc97">&sect;</ulink>
5717      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d69d45a5a">&sect;</ulink>
5718      </para>
5719      </listitem>
5720
5721<!--
5722Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
57232024-07-30 [9b282a935] Fix partitionwise join with partially-redundant join cla
5724Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
57252024-07-29 [513f4472a] Reduce memory used by partitionwise joins
5726-->
5727
5728      <listitem>
5729      <para>
5730      Allow <link linkend="guc-enable-partitionwise-join">partitionwise
5731      joins</link> in more cases, and reduce its memory usage (Richard Guo,
5732      Tom Lane, Ashutosh Bapat)
5733      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9b282a935">&sect;</ulink>
5734      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;513f4472a">&sect;</ulink>
5735      </para>
5736      </listitem>
5737
5738<!--
5739Author: Alexander Korotkov <akorotkov@postgresql.org>
57402025-03-10 [fae535da0] Teach Append to consider tuple_fraction when accumulatin
5741-->
5742
5743      <listitem>
5744      <para>
5745      Improve cost estimates of partition queries (Nikita Malakhov,
5746      Andrei Lepikhov)
5747      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;fae535da0">&sect;</ulink>
5748      </para>
5749      </listitem>
5750
5751<!--
5752Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
57532025-04-02 [0dca5d68d] Change SQL-language functions to use the plan cache.
5754Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
57552025-04-17 [09b07c295] Minor performance improvement for SQL-language functions
5756-->
5757
5758      <listitem>
5759      <para>
5760      Improve <link linkend="xfunc-sql"><acronym>SQL</acronym>-language
5761      function</link> plan caching (Alexander Pyhalov, Tom Lane)
5762      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0dca5d68d">&sect;</ulink>
5763      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;09b07c295">&sect;</ulink>
5764      </para>
5765      </listitem>
5766
5767<!--
5768Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
57692024-08-21 [e22253467] Treat number of disabled nodes in a path as a separate c
5770-->
5771
5772      <listitem>
5773      <para>
5774      Improve handling of disabled optimizer features (Robert Haas)
5775      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e22253467">&sect;</ulink>
5776      </para>
5777      </listitem>
5778
5779     </itemizedlist>
5780
5781    </sect4>
5782
5783    <sect4 id="release-18-indexes">
5784     <title>Indexes</title>
5785
5786     <itemizedlist>
5787
5788<!--
5789Author: Peter Geoghegan <pg@bowt.ie>
57902025-04-04 [92fe23d93] Add nbtree skip scan optimization.
5791Author: Peter Geoghegan <pg@bowt.ie>
57922025-04-04 [8a510275d] Further optimize nbtree search scan key comparisons.
5793-->
5794
5795      <listitem>
5796      <para>
5797      Allow skip scans of <link linkend="xfunc-sql">btree</link> indexes
5798      (Peter Geoghegan)
5799      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;92fe23d93">&sect;</ulink>
5800      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8a510275d">&sect;</ulink>
5801      </para>
5802
5803      <para>
5804      This allows multi-column btree indexes to be used in more cases such
5805      as when there are no restrictions on the first or early indexed
5806      columns (or there are non-equality ones), and there are useful
5807      restrictions on later indexed columns.
5808      </para>
5809      </listitem>
5810
5811<!--
5812Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
58132025-03-18 [f278e1fe3] Allow non-btree unique indexes for partition keys
5814Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
58152025-03-18 [9d6db8bec] Allow non-btree unique indexes for matviews
5816-->
5817
5818      <listitem>
5819      <para>
5820      Allow non-btree unique indexes to be used as partition keys and in
5821      materialized views (Mark Dilger)
5822      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f278e1fe3">&sect;</ulink>
5823      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9d6db8bec">&sect;</ulink>
5824      </para>
5825
5826      <para>
5827      The index type must still support equality.
5828      </para>
5829      </listitem>
5830
5831<!--
5832Author: Tomas Vondra <tomas.vondra@postgresql.org>
58332025-03-03 [8492feb98] Allow parallel CREATE INDEX for GIN indexes
5834-->
5835
5836      <listitem>
5837      <para>
5838      Allow <link linkend="gin"><literal>GIN</literal></link> indexes to
5839      be created in parallel (Tomas Vondra, Matthias van de Meent)
5840      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8492feb98">&sect;</ulink>
5841      </para>
5842      </listitem>
5843
5844<!--
5845Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
58462025-04-02 [e9e7b6604] Add GiST and btree sortsupport routines for range types
5847-->
5848
5849      <listitem>
5850      <para>
5851      Allow values to be sorted to speed range-type <link
5852      linkend="gist">GiST</link> and <link linkend="btree">btree</link>
5853      index builds (Bernd Helmle)
5854      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e9e7b6604">&sect;</ulink>
5855      </para>
5856      </listitem>
5857
5858     </itemizedlist>
5859
5860    </sect4>
5861
5862    <sect4 id="release-18-performance">
5863     <title>General Performance</title>
5864
5865     <itemizedlist>
5866
5867<!--
5868Author: Andres Freund <andres@anarazel.de>
58692025-03-17 [02844012b] aio: Basic subsystem initialization
5870Author: Andres Freund <andres@anarazel.de>
58712025-03-17 [da7226993] aio: Add core asynchronous I/O infrastructure
5872Author: Andres Freund <andres@anarazel.de>
58732025-03-18 [55b454d0e] aio: Infrastructure for io_method=worker
5874Author: Andres Freund <andres@anarazel.de>
58752025-03-18 [247ce06b8] aio: Add io_method=worker
5876Author: Thomas Munro <tmunro@postgresql.org>
58772025-03-19 [10f664684] Introduce io_max_combine_limit.
5878Author: Thomas Munro <tmunro@postgresql.org>
58792025-03-19 [06fb5612c] Increase io_combine_limit range to 1MB.
5880Author: Andres Freund <andres@anarazel.de>
58812025-03-26 [c325a7633] aio: Add io_method=io_uring
5882Author: Andres Freund <andres@anarazel.de>
58832025-03-29 [50cb7505b] aio: Implement support for reads in smgr/md/fd
5884Author: Andres Freund <andres@anarazel.de>
58852025-03-30 [047cba7fa] bufmgr: Implement AIO read support
5886Author: Andres Freund <andres@anarazel.de>
58872025-03-30 [12ce89fd0] bufmgr: Use AIO in StartReadBuffers()
5888Author: Andres Freund <andres@anarazel.de>
58892025-03-30 [2a5e709e7] Enable IO concurrency on all systems
5890-->
5891
5892      <listitem>
5893      <para>
5894      Add an asynchronous I/O subsystem (Andres Freund, Thomas Munro,
5895      Nazir Bilal Yavuz, Melanie Plageman)
5896      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;02844012b">&sect;</ulink>
5897      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;da7226993">&sect;</ulink>
5898      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;55b454d0e">&sect;</ulink>
5899      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;247ce06b8">&sect;</ulink>
5900      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;10f664684">&sect;</ulink>
5901      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;06fb5612c">&sect;</ulink>
5902      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c325a7633">&sect;</ulink>
5903      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;50cb7505b">&sect;</ulink>
5904      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;047cba7fa">&sect;</ulink>
5905      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;12ce89fd0">&sect;</ulink>
5906      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;2a5e709e7">&sect;</ulink>
5907      </para>
5908
5909      <para>
5910      This feature allows backends to queue multiple read requests,
5911      which allows for more efficient sequential scans, bitmap
5912      heap scans, vacuums, etc.  This is enabled by server
5913      variable <xref linkend="guc-io-method"/>, with server
5914      variables <xref linkend="guc-io-combine-limit"/> and <xref
5915      linkend="guc-io-max-combine-limit"/> added to control it.
5916      This also enables <xref linkend="guc-effective-io-concurrency"/>
5917      and <xref linkend="guc-maintenance-io-concurrency"/>
5918      values greater than zero for systems without
5919      <function>fadvise()</function> support.  The new system view <link
5920      linkend="view-pg-aios"><structname>pg_aios</structname></link>
5921      shows the file handles being used for asynchronous I/O.
5922      </para>
5923      </listitem>
5924
5925<!--
5926Author: Tomas Vondra <tomas.vondra@postgresql.org>
59272024-09-21 [c4d5cb71d] Increase the number of fast-path lock slots
5928-->
5929
5930      <listitem>
5931      <para>
5932      Improve the locking performance of queries that access many relations
5933      (Tomas Vondra)
5934      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c4d5cb71d">&sect;</ulink>
5935      </para>
5936      </listitem>
5937
5938<!--
5939Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
59402024-08-20 [adf97c156] Speed up Hash Join by making ExprStates support hashing
5941Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
59422024-12-11 [0f5738202] Use ExprStates for hashing in GROUP BY and SubPlans
5943Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
59442025-03-24 [4d143509c] Create accessor functions for TupleHashEntry.
5945Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
59462025-03-24 [a0942f441] Add ExecCopySlotMinimalTupleExtra().
5947Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
59482025-03-24 [626df47ad] Remove 'additional' pointer from TupleHashEntryData.
5949-->
5950
5951      <listitem>
5952      <para>
5953      Improve the performance and reduce memory usage of hash joins and
5954      <link linkend="sql-groupby"><literal>GROUP BY</literal></link>
5955      (David Rowley, Jeff Davis)
5956      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;adf97c156">&sect;</ulink>
5957      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0f5738202">&sect;</ulink>
5958      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4d143509c">&sect;</ulink>
5959      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a0942f441">&sect;</ulink>
5960      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;626df47ad">&sect;</ulink>
5961      </para>
5962
5963      <para>
5964      This also improves hash set operations used by <link
5965      linkend="sql-except"><literal>EXCEPT</literal></link>, and hash
5966      lookups of subplan values.
5967      </para>
5968      </listitem>
5969
5970<!--
5971Author: Melanie Plageman <melanieplageman@gmail.com>
59722025-02-11 [052026c9b] Eagerly scan all-visible pages to amortize aggressive va
5973Author: Melanie Plageman <melanieplageman@gmail.com>
59742025-03-03 [06eae9e62] Trigger more frequent autovacuums with relallfrozen
5975-->
5976
5977      <listitem>
5978      <para>
5979      Allow normal vacuums to freeze some pages, even though they are
5980      all-visible (Melanie Plageman)
5981      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;052026c9b">&sect;</ulink>
5982      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;06eae9e62">&sect;</ulink>
5983      </para>
5984
5985      <para>
5986      This reduces the overhead of later full-relation
5987      freezing.  The aggressiveness of this can be
5988      controlled by server variable and per-table setting <xref
5989      linkend="guc-vacuum-max-eager-freeze-failure-rate"/>.  Previously
5990      vacuum never processed all-visible pages until freezing was required.
5991      </para>
5992      </listitem>
5993
5994<!--
5995Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
59962025-03-20 [0164a0f9e] Add vacuum_truncate configuration parameter.
5997-->
5998
5999      <listitem>
6000      <para>
6001      Add server variable <xref linkend="guc-vacuum-truncate"/> to control
6002      file truncation during <xref linkend="sql-vacuum"/> (Nathan Bossart,
6003      Gurjeet Singh)
6004      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0164a0f9e">&sect;</ulink>
6005      </para>
6006
6007      <para>
6008      A storage-level parameter with the same name and behavior already
6009      existed.
6010      </para>
6011      </listitem>
6012
6013<!--
6014Author: Melanie Plageman <melanieplageman@gmail.com>
60152025-03-12 [ff79b5b2a] Increase default effective_io_concurrency to 16
6016Author: Melanie Plageman <melanieplageman@gmail.com>
60172025-03-18 [cc6be07eb] Increase default maintenance_io_concurrency to 16
6018-->
6019
6020      <listitem>
6021      <para>
6022      Increase server variables <xref
6023      linkend="guc-effective-io-concurrency"/>'s and <xref
6024      linkend="guc-maintenance-io-concurrency"/>'s default values to 16
