6025      (Melanie Plageman)
6026      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ff79b5b2a">&sect;</ulink>
6027      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cc6be07eb">&sect;</ulink>
6028      </para>
6029
6030      <para>
6031      This more accurately reflects modern hardware.
6032      </para>
6033      </listitem>
6034
6035     </itemizedlist>
6036
6037    </sect4>
6038
6039    <sect4 id="release-18-monitoring">
6040     <title>Monitoring</title>
6041
6042     <itemizedlist>
6043
6044<!--
6045Author: Melanie Plageman <melanieplageman@gmail.com>
60462025-03-12 [9219093ca] Modularize log_connections output
6047-->
6048
6049      <listitem>
6050      <para>
6051      Increase the logging granularity of server variable <xref
6052      linkend="guc-log-connections"/> (Melanie Plageman)
6053      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9219093ca">&sect;</ulink>
6054      </para>
6055
6056      <para>
6057      This server variable was previously only boolean, which is still
6058      supported.
6059      </para>
6060      </listitem>
6061
6062<!--
6063Author: Melanie Plageman <melanieplageman@gmail.com>
60642025-03-12 [18cd15e70] Add connection establishment duration logging
6065-->
6066
6067      <listitem>
6068      <para>
6069      Add <varname>log_connections</varname> option to report the duration
6070      of connection stages (Melanie Plageman)
6071      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;18cd15e70">&sect;</ulink>
6072      </para>
6073      </listitem>
6074
6075<!--
6076Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
60772025-04-07 [3516ea768] Add local-address escape "%L" to log_line_prefix.
6078-->
6079
6080      <listitem>
6081      <para>
6082      Add <xref linkend="guc-log-line-prefix"/> escape
6083      <literal>%L</literal> to output the client <acronym>IP</acronym>
6084      address (Greg Sabino Mullane)
6085      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3516ea768">&sect;</ulink>
6086      </para>
6087      </listitem>
6088
6089<!--
6090Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
60912025-03-14 [6d376c3b0] Add GUC option to log lock acquisition failures.
6092Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
60932025-06-03 [73bdcfab3] Rename log_lock_failure GUC to log_lock_failures for con
6094-->
6095
6096      <listitem>
6097      <para>
6098      Add server variable <xref linkend="guc-log-lock-failures"/> to log
6099      lock acquisition failures (Yuki Seino, Fujii Masao)
6100      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6d376c3b0">&sect;</ulink>
6101      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;73bdcfab3">&sect;</ulink>
6102      </para>
6103
6104      <para>
6105      Specifically it reports <link
6106      linkend="sql-for-update-share"><command>SELECT
6107      ... NOWAIT</command></link> lock failures.
6108      </para>
6109      </listitem>
6110
6111<!--
6112Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
61132025-01-28 [30a6ed0ce] Track per-relation cumulative time spent in [auto]vacuum
6114-->
6115
6116      <listitem>
6117      <para>
6118      Modify <link
6119      linkend="monitoring-pg-stat-all-tables-view"><structname>pg_stat_all_tables</structname></link>
6120      and its variants to report the time spent in <xref
6121      linkend="sql-vacuum"/>, <xref linkend="sql-analyze"/>, and their
6122      <link linkend="autovacuum">automatic</link> variants (Sami Imseih)
6123      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;30a6ed0ce">&sect;</ulink>
6124      </para>
6125
6126      <para>
6127      The new columns are <structfield>total_vacuum_time</structfield>,
6128      <structfield>total_autovacuum_time</structfield>,
6129      <structfield>total_analyze_time</structfield>, and
6130      <structfield>total_autoanalyze_time</structfield>.
6131      </para>
6132      </listitem>
6133
6134<!--
6135Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
61362025-02-11 [bb8dff999] Add cost-based vacuum delay time to progress views.
6137Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
61382025-02-14 [7720082ae] Add delay time to VACUUM/ANALYZE (VERBOSE) and autovacuu
6139-->
6140
6141      <listitem>
6142      <para>
6143      Add delay time reporting to <xref linkend="sql-vacuum"/> and <xref
6144      linkend="sql-analyze"/> (Bertrand Drouvot, Nathan Bossart)
6145      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bb8dff999">&sect;</ulink>
6146      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7720082ae">&sect;</ulink>
6147      </para>
6148
6149      <para>
6150      This information appears in the server log, the system views <link
6151      linkend="vacuum-progress-reporting"><structname>pg_stat_progress_vacuum</structname></link>
6152      and <link
6153      linkend="pg-stat-progress-analyze-view"><structname>pg_stat_progress_analyze</structname></link>,
6154      and the output of <xref linkend="sql-vacuum"/> and <xref
6155      linkend="sql-analyze"/> when in <literal>VERBOSE</literal>
6156      mode; tracking must be enabled with the server variable <xref
6157      linkend="guc-track-cost-delay-timing"/>.
6158      </para>
6159      </listitem>
6160
6161<!--
6162Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
61632024-08-13 [4c1b4cdb8] Add resource statistics reporting to ANALYZE VERBOSE.
6164Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
61652024-09-09 [bb7775234] Add WAL usage reporting to ANALYZE VERBOSE output.
6166-->
6167
6168      <listitem>
6169      <para>
6170      Add <acronym>WAL</acronym>, <acronym>CPU</acronym>, and average
6171      read statistics output to <command>ANALYZE VERBOSE</command>
6172      (Anthonin Bonnefoy)
6173      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4c1b4cdb8">&sect;</ulink>
6174      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bb7775234">&sect;</ulink>
6175      </para>
6176      </listitem>
6177
6178<!--
6179Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
61802025-02-17 [6a8a7ce47] Add information about WAL buffers full to VACUUM/ANALYZE
6181-->
6182
6183      <listitem>
6184      <para>
6185      Add full <acronym>WAL</acronym> buffer count to
6186      <command>VACUUM</command>/<command>ANALYZE (VERBOSE)</command>
6187      and autovacuum log output (Bertrand Drouvot)
6188      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6a8a7ce47">&sect;</ulink>
6189      </para>
6190      </listitem>
6191
6192<!--
6193Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
61942024-12-19 [9aea73fc6] Add backend-level statistics to pgstats
6195Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
61962025-03-03 [3f1db99bf] Handle auxiliary processes in SQL functions of backend s
6197-->
6198
6199      <listitem>
6200      <para>
6201      Add per-backend I/O statistics reporting (Bertrand Drouvot)
6202      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9aea73fc6">&sect;</ulink>
6203      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3f1db99bf">&sect;</ulink>
6204      </para>
6205
6206      <para>
6207      The statistics are accessed via <link
6208      linkend="pg-stat-get-backend-io"><function>pg_stat_get_backend_io()</function></link>.
6209      Per-backend I/O statistics can be cleared via <link
6210      linkend="monitoring-stats-funcs-table"><function>pg_stat_reset_backend_stats()</function></link>.
6211      </para>
6212      </listitem>
6213
6214<!--
6215Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
62162025-01-14 [f92c854cf] Make pg_stat_io count IOs as bytes instead of blocks for
6217-->
6218
6219      <listitem>
6220      <para>
6221      Add <link
6222      linkend="monitoring-pg-stat-io-view"><structname>pg_stat_io</structname></link>
6223      columns to report I/O activity in bytes (Nazir Bilal Yavuz)
6224      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f92c854cf">&sect;</ulink>
6225      </para>
6226
6227      <para>
6228      The new columns are <structfield>read_bytes</structfield>,
6229      <structfield>write_bytes</structfield>, and
6230      <structfield>extend_bytes</structfield>.  The
6231      <structfield>op_bytes</structfield> column, which always equaled
6232      <link linkend="guc-block-size"><varname>BLCKSZ</varname></link>,
6233      has been removed.
6234      </para>
6235      </listitem>
6236
6237<!--
6238Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
62392025-02-04 [a051e71e2] Add data for WAL in pg_stat_io and backend statistics
6240Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
62412025-02-20 [4538bd3f1] doc: Add details about object "wal" in pg_stat_io
6242Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
62432025-03-06 [7f7f324eb] Add more monitoring data for WAL writes in the WAL recei
6244-->
6245
6246      <listitem>
6247      <para>
6248      Add <acronym>WAL</acronym> I/O activity rows to
6249      <structname>pg_stat_io</structname> (Nazir Bilal Yavuz, Bertrand
6250      Drouvot, Michael Paquier)
6251      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a051e71e2">&sect;</ulink>
6252      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4538bd3f1">&sect;</ulink>
6253      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7f7f324eb">&sect;</ulink>
6254      </para>
6255
6256      <para>
6257      This includes <acronym>WAL</acronym> receiver activity and a wait
6258      event for such writes.
6259      </para>
6260
6261      </listitem>
6262
6263<!--
6264Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
62652025-02-26 [6c349d83b] Re-add GUC track_wal_io_timing
6266-->
6267
6268      <listitem>
6269      <para>
6270      Change server variable <xref linkend="guc-track-wal-io-timing"/>
6271      to control tracking <acronym>WAL</acronym> timing
6272      in <structname>pg_stat_io</structname> instead of <link
6273      linkend="pg-stat-wal-view"><structname>pg_stat_wal</structname></link>
6274      (Bertrand Drouvot)
6275      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6c349d83b">&sect;</ulink>
6276      </para>
6277      </listitem>
6278
6279<!--
6280Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
62812025-02-24 [2421e9a51] Remove read/sync fields from pg_stat_wal and GUC track_w
6282Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
62832025-02-26 [6c349d83b] Re-add GUC track_wal_io_timing
6284-->
6285
6286      <listitem>
6287      <para>
6288      Remove read/sync columns from <structname>pg_stat_wal</structname>
6289      (Bertrand Drouvot)
6290      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;2421e9a51">&sect;</ulink>
6291      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6c349d83b">&sect;</ulink>
6292      </para>
6293
6294      <para>
6295      This removes columns <structfield>wal_write</structfield>,
6296      <structfield>wal_sync</structfield>,
6297      <structfield>wal_write_time</structfield>, and
6298      <structfield>wal_sync_time</structfield>.
6299      </para>
6300      </listitem>
6301
6302<!--
6303Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
63042025-03-11 [76def4cdd] Add WAL data to backend statistics
6305-->
6306
6307      <listitem>
6308      <para>
6309      Add function <link
6310      linkend="pg-stat-get-backend-wal"><function>pg_stat_get_backend_wal()</function></link>
6311      to return per-backend <acronym>WAL</acronym> statistics (Bertrand
6312      Drouvot)
6313      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;76def4cdd">&sect;</ulink>
6314      </para>
6315
6316      <para>
6317      Per-backend <acronym>WAL</acronym>
6318      statistics can be cleared via <link
6319      linkend="monitoring-stats-funcs-table"><function>pg_stat_reset_backend_stats()</function></link>.
6320      </para>
6321      </listitem>
6322
6323<!--
6324Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
63252024-10-11 [4e1fad378] Add pg_ls_summariesdir().
6326-->
6327
6328      <listitem>
6329      <para>
6330      Add function <link
6331      linkend="functions-admin-genfile-table"><function>pg_ls_summariesdir()</function></link>
6332      to specifically list the contents of <link
6333      linkend="storage-file-layout"><envar>PGDATA</envar></link>/<link
6334      linkend="guc-wal-summary-keep-time"><filename>pg_wal/summaries</filename></link>
6335      (Yushi Ogiwara)
6336      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4e1fad378">&sect;</ulink>
6337      </para>
6338      </listitem>
6339
6340<!--
6341Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
63422024-09-30 [559efce1d] Add num_done counter to the pg_stat_checkpointer view.
6343-->
6344
6345      <listitem>
6346      <para>
6347      Add column <link
6348      linkend="monitoring-pg-stat-checkpointer-view"><structname>pg_stat_checkpointer</structname></link>.<structfield>num_done</structfield>
6349      to report the number of completed checkpoints (Anton A. Melnikov)
6350      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;559efce1d">&sect;</ulink>
6351      </para>
6352
6353      <para>
6354      Columns <structfield>num_timed</structfield> and
6355      <structfield>num_requested</structfield> count both completed and
6356      skipped checkpoints.
6357      </para>
6358      </listitem>
6359
6360<!--
6361Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
63622024-10-02 [17cc5f666] Fix inconsistent reporting of checkpointer stats.
6363-->
6364
6365      <listitem>
6366      <para>
6367      Add column
6368      <structname>pg_stat_checkpointer</structname>.<structfield>slru_written</structfield>
6369      to report <acronym>SLRU</acronym> buffers written (Nitin Jadhav)
6370      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;17cc5f666">&sect;</ulink>
6371      </para>
6372
6373      <para>
6374      Also, modify the checkpoint server log message to report separate
6375      shared buffer and <acronym>SLRU</acronym> buffer values.
6376      </para>
6377      </listitem>
6378
6379<!--
6380Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
63812024-11-11 [e7a9496de] Add two attributes to pg_stat_database for parallel work
6382-->
6383
6384      <listitem>
6385      <para>
6386      Add columns to <link
6387      linkend="monitoring-pg-stat-database-view"><structname>pg_stat_database</structname></link>
6388      to report parallel worker activity (Benoit Lobréau)
6389      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e7a9496de">&sect;</ulink>
6390      </para>
6391
6392      <para>
6393      The new columns are
6394      <structfield>parallel_workers_to_launch</structfield> and
6395      <structfield>parallel_workers_launched</structfield>.
6396      </para>
6397      </listitem>
6398
6399<!--
6400Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@alvh.no-ip.org>
64012025-03-18 [62d712ecf] Introduce squashing of constant lists in query jumbling
6402Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@alvh.no-ip.org>
64032025-03-27 [9fbd53dea] Remove the query_id_squash_values GUC
6404Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@kurilemu.de>
6405Branch: master Release: REL_18_BR [c2da1a5d6] 2025-06-24 19:36:32 +0200
6406-->
6407
6408      <listitem>
6409      <para>
6410      Have <link linkend="guc-compute-query-id">query id</link> computation
6411      of constant lists consider only the first and last constants (Dmitry
6412      Dolgov, Sami Imseih)
6413      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;62d712ecf">&sect;</ulink>
6414      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9fbd53dea">&sect;</ulink>
6415      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c2da1a5d6">&sect;</ulink>
6416      </para>
6417
6418      <para>
6419      Jumbling is used by <xref linkend="pgstatstatements"/>.
6420      </para>
6421      </listitem>
6422
6423<!--
6424Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
64252025-03-26 [787514b30] Use relation name instead of OID in query jumbling for R
6426-->
6427
6428      <listitem>
6429      <para>
6430      Adjust query id computations to group together queries using the
6431      same relation name (Michael Paquier, Sami Imseih)
6432      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;787514b30">&sect;</ulink>
6433      </para>
6434
6435      <para>
6436      This is true even if the tables in different schemas have different
6437      column names.
6438      </para>
6439      </listitem>
6440
6441<!--
6442Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
64432024-07-01 [12227a1d5] Add context type field to pg_backend_memory_contexts
6444-->
6445
6446      <listitem>
6447      <para>
6448      Add column <link
6449      linkend="view-pg-backend-memory-contexts"><structname>pg_backend_memory_contexts</structname></link>.<structfield>type</structfield>
6450      to report the type of memory context (David Rowley)
6451      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;12227a1d5">&sect;</ulink>
6452      </para>
6453      </listitem>
6454
6455<!--
6456Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
64572024-07-25 [32d3ed816] Add path column to pg_backend_memory_contexts view
6458-->
6459
6460      <listitem>
6461      <para>
6462      Add column
6463      <structname>pg_backend_memory_contexts</structname>.<structfield>path</structfield>
6464      to show memory context parents (Melih Mutlu)
6465      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;32d3ed816">&sect;</ulink>
6466      </para>
6467      </listitem>
6468
6469     </itemizedlist>
6470
6471    </sect4>
6472
6473    <sect4 id="release-18-privileges">
6474     <title>Privileges</title>
6475
6476     <itemizedlist>
6477
6478<!--
6479Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
64802024-07-04 [4564f1ceb] Add pg_get_acl() to get the ACL for a database object
6481Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
64822024-07-10 [d898665bf] Extend pg_get_acl() to handle sub-object IDs
6483-->
6484
6485      <listitem>
6486      <para>
6487      Add function <link
6488      linkend="functions-info-object-table"><function>pg_get_acl()</function></link>
6489      to retrieve database access control details (Joel Jacobson)
6490      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4564f1ceb">&sect;</ulink>
6491      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d898665bf">&sect;</ulink>
6492      </para>
6493      </listitem>
6494
6495<!--
6496Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
64972024-09-12 [4eada203a] Add has_largeobject_privilege function.
6498-->
6499
6500      <listitem>
6501      <para>
6502      Add function <link
6503      linkend="functions-info-access-table"><function>has_largeobject_privilege()</function></link>
6504      to check large object privileges (Yugo Nagata)
6505      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4eada203a">&sect;</ulink>
6506      </para>
6507      </listitem>
6508
6509<!--
6510Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
65112025-04-04 [0d6c47766] Extend ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES to define default privil
6512-->
6513
6514      <listitem>
6515      <para>
6516      Allow <xref linkend="sql-alterdefaultprivileges"/> to define
6517      large object default privileges (Takatsuka Haruka, Yugo Nagata,
6518      Laurenz Albe)
6519      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0d6c47766">&sect;</ulink>
6520      </para>
6521      </listitem>
6522
6523<!--
6524Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
65252024-07-09 [ccd38024b] Introduce pg_signal_autovacuum_worker.
6526-->
6527
6528      <listitem>
6529      <para>
6530      Add predefined role <link
6531      linkend="predefined-roles"><literal>pg_signal_autovacuum_worker</literal></link>
6532      (Kirill Reshke)
6533      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ccd38024b">&sect;</ulink>
6534      </para>
6535
6536      <para>
6537      This allows sending signals to autovacuum workers.
6538      </para>
6539      </listitem>
6540
6541     </itemizedlist>
6542
6543    </sect4>
6544
6545    <sect4 id="release-18-server-config">
6546     <title>Server Configuration</title>
6547
6548     <itemizedlist>
6549
6550<!--
6551Author: Daniel Gustafsson <dgustafsson@postgresql.org>
65522025-02-20 [b3f0be788] Add support for OAUTHBEARER SASL mechanism
6553-->
6554
6555      <listitem>
6556      <para>
6557      Add support for the <link linkend="auth-oauth">OAuth authentication
6558      method</link> (Jacob Champion, Daniel Gustafsson, Thomas Munro)
6559      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b3f0be788">&sect;</ulink>
6560      </para>
6561
6562      <para>
6563      This adds an <literal>oauth</literal> authentication method to <link
6564      linkend="auth-pg-hba-conf"><filename>pg_hba.conf</filename></link>,
6565      libpq OAuth options, a server variable <xref
6566      linkend="guc-oauth-validator-libraries"/> to load
6567      token validation libraries, and a configure flag <link
6568      linkend="configure-option-with-libcurl"><option>--with-libcurl</option></link>
6569      to add the required compile-time libraries.
6570      </para>
6571      </listitem>
6572
6573<!--
6574Author: Daniel Gustafsson <dgustafsson@postgresql.org>
65752024-10-24 [45188c2ea] Support configuring TLSv1.3 cipher suites
6576-->
6577
6578      <listitem>
6579      <para>
6580      Add server variable <xref linkend="guc-ssl-tls13-ciphers"/> to allow
6581      specification of multiple colon-separated TLSv1.3 cipher suites
6582      (Erica Zhang, Daniel Gustafsson)
6583      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;45188c2ea">&sect;</ulink>
6584      </para>
6585      </listitem>
6586
6587<!--
6588Author: Daniel Gustafsson <dgustafsson@postgresql.org>
65892025-03-18 [daa02c6bd] Add X25519 to the default set of curves
6590-->
6591
6592      <listitem>
6593      <para>
6594      Change server variable <xref linkend="guc-ssl-groups"/>'s default
6595      to include elliptic curve X25519 (Daniel Gustafsson, Jacob Champion)
6596      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;daa02c6bd">&sect;</ulink>
6597      </para>
6598      </listitem>
6599
6600<!--
6601Author: Daniel Gustafsson <dgustafsson@postgresql.org>
66022024-10-24 [3d1ef3a15] Support configuring multiple ECDH curves
6603-->
6604
6605      <listitem>
6606      <para>
6607      Rename server variable <varname>ssl_ecdh_curve</varname> to <xref
6608      linkend="guc-ssl-groups"/> and allow multiple colon-separated
6609      <acronym>ECDH</acronym> curves to be specified (Erica Zhang,
6610      Daniel Gustafsson)
6611      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3d1ef3a15">&sect;</ulink>
6612      </para>
6613
6614      <para>
6615      The previous name still works.
6616      </para>
6617      </listitem>
6618
6619<!--
6620Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
66212025-04-02 [a460251f0] Make cancel request keys longer
6622Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
66232024-07-29 [9d9b9d46f] Move cancel key generation to after forking the backend
6624-->
6625
6626      <listitem>
6627      <para>
6628      Make <link linkend="functions-admin-signal-table">cancel request
6629      keys</link> 256 bits (Heikki Linnakangas, Jelte Fennema-Nio)
6630      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a460251f0">&sect;</ulink>
6631      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9d9b9d46f">&sect;</ulink>
6632      </para>
6633
6634      <para>
6635      This is only possible when the server and client support wire
6636      protocol version 3.2, introduced in this release.
6637      </para>
6638      </listitem>
6639
6640<!--
6641Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
66422025-01-06 [c758119e5] Allow changing autovacuum_max_workers without restarting
6643-->
6644
6645      <listitem>
6646      <para>
6647      Add server variable <xref linkend="guc-autovacuum-worker-slots"/>
6648      to specify the maximum number of background workers (Nathan Bossart)
6649      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c758119e5">&sect;</ulink>
6650      </para>
6651
6652      <para>
6653      With this variable set, <xref linkend="guc-autovacuum-max-workers"/>
6654      can be adjusted at runtime up to this maximum without a server
6655      restart.
6656      </para>
6657      </listitem>
6658
6659<!--
6660Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
66612025-02-05 [306dc520b] Introduce autovacuum_vacuum_max_threshold.
6662-->
6663
6664      <listitem>
6665      <para>
6666      Allow specification of the fixed number of dead tuples that will
6667      trigger an <link linkend="autovacuum">autovacuum</link> (Nathan
6668      Bossart, Frédéric Yhuel)
6669      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;306dc520b">&sect;</ulink>
6670      </para>
6671
6672      <para>
6673      The server variable is <xref
6674      linkend="guc-autovacuum-vacuum-max-threshold"/>.  Percentages are
6675      still used for triggering.
6676      </para>
6677      </listitem>
6678
6679<!--
6680Author: Andres Freund <andres@anarazel.de>
66812025-03-24 [adb5f85fa] Redefine max_files_per_process to control additionally o
6682-->
6683
6684      <listitem>
6685      <para>
6686      Change server variable <xref linkend="guc-max-files-per-process"/>
6687      to limit only files opened by a backend (Andres Freund)
6688      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;adb5f85fa">&sect;</ulink>
6689      </para>
6690
6691      <para>
6692      Previously files opened by the postmaster were also counted toward
6693      this limit.
6694      </para>
6695      </listitem>
6696
6697<!--
6698Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
66992024-07-26 [0dcaea569] Introduce num_os_semaphores GUC.
6700-->
6701
6702      <listitem>
6703      <para>
6704      Add server variable <xref linkend="guc-num-os-semaphores"/> to
6705      report the required number of semaphores (Nathan Bossart)
6706      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0dcaea569">&sect;</ulink>
6707      </para>
6708
6709      <para>
6710      This is useful for operating system configuration.
6711      </para>
6712      </listitem>
6713
6714<!--
6715Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
67162025-03-19 [4f7f7b037] extension_control_path
6717Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
67182025-05-02 [81eaaa2c4] Make "directory" setting work with extension_control_pat
6719-->
6720
6721      <listitem>
6722      <para>
6723      Add server variable <xref linkend="guc-extension-control-path"/> to
6724      specify the location of extension control files (Peter Eisentraut,
6725      Matheus Alcantara)
6726      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4f7f7b037">&sect;</ulink>
6727      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;81eaaa2c4">&sect;</ulink>
6728      </para>
6729      </listitem>
6730
6731     </itemizedlist>
6732
6733    </sect4>
6734
6735    <sect4 id="release-18-replication">
6736     <title>Streaming Replication and Recovery</title>
6737
6738     <itemizedlist>
6739
6740<!--
6741Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
67422025-02-19 [ac0e33136] Invalidate inactive replication slots.
6743-->
6744
6745      <listitem>
6746      <para>
6747      Allow inactive replication slots to be automatically invalidated using
6748      server variable <xref linkend="guc-idle-replication-slot-timeout"/>
6749      (Nisha Moond, Bharath Rupireddy)
6750      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ac0e33136">&sect;</ulink>
6751      </para>
6752      </listitem>
6753
6754<!--
6755Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
67562025-03-21 [04ff636cb] Add GUC option to control maximum active replication ori
6757-->
6758
6759      <listitem>
6760      <para>
6761      Add server variable <xref
6762      linkend="guc-max-active-replication-origins"/> to control the
6763      maximum active replication origins (Euler Taveira)
6764      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;04ff636cb">&sect;</ulink>
6765      </para>
6766
6767      <para>
6768      This was previously controlled by <xref
6769      linkend="guc-max-replication-slots"/>, but this new setting allows
6770      a higher origin count in cases where fewer slots are required.
6771      </para>
6772      </listitem>
6773
6774     </itemizedlist>
6775
6776    </sect4>
6777
6778    <sect4 id="release-18-logical">
6779     <title><link linkend="logical-replication">Logical Replication</link></title>
6780
6781     <itemizedlist>
6782
6783<!--
6784Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
67852024-10-30 [745217a05] Replicate generated columns when specified in the column
6786Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
67872024-11-07 [7054186c4] Replicate generated columns when 'publish_generated_colu
6788Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
67892024-12-04 [87ce27de6] Ensure stored generated columns must be published when r
6790Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
67912025-01-30 [6252b1eaf] Doc: Generated column replication.
6792-->
6793
6794      <listitem>
6795      <para>
6796      Allow the values of <link
6797      linkend="sql-createtable-parms-generated-stored">generated
6798      columns</link> to be logically replicated (Shubham Khanna, Vignesh C,
6799      Zhijie Hou, Shlok Kyal, Peter Smith)
6800      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;745217a05">&sect;</ulink>
6801      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7054186c4">&sect;</ulink>
6802      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;87ce27de6">&sect;</ulink>
6803      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6252b1eaf">&sect;</ulink>
6804      </para>
6805
6806      <para>
6807      If the publication specifies a column list, all specified
6808      columns, generated and non-generated, are published.
6809      Without a specified column list, publication option
6810      <literal>publish_generated_columns</literal> controls whether
6811      generated columns are published. Previously generated columns
6812      were not replicated and the subscriber had to compute
6813      the values if possible;  this is particularly useful for
6814      non-<productname>PostgreSQL</productname> subscribers which lack
6815      such a capability.
6816      </para>
6817      </listitem>
6818
6819<!--
6820Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
68212024-10-28 [1bf1140be] Change the default value of the streaming option to 'par
6822-->
6823
6824      <listitem>
6825      <para>
6826      Change the default <xref linkend="sql-createsubscription"/> streaming
6827      option from <literal>off</literal> to <literal>parallel</literal>
6828      (Vignesh C)
6829      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1bf1140be">&sect;</ulink>
6830      </para>
6831      </listitem>
6832
6833<!--
6834Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
68352024-07-24 [1462aad2e] Allow altering of two_phase option of a SUBSCRIPTION.
6836Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
68372025-04-03 [4868c96bc] Fix slot synchronization for two_phase enabled slots.
6838-->
6839
6840      <listitem>
6841      <para>
6842      Allow <xref linkend="sql-altersubscription"/> to change the
6843      replication slot's two-phase commit behavior (Hayato Kuroda, Ajin
6844      Cherian, Amit Kapila, Zhijie Hou)
6845      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1462aad2e">&sect;</ulink>
6846      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4868c96bc">&sect;</ulink>
6847      </para>
6848      </listitem>
6849
6850<!--
6851Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
68522024-08-20 [9758174e2] Log the conflicts while applying changes in logical repl
6853Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
68542024-08-22 [edcb71258] Doc: explain the log format of logical replication confl
6855Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
68562024-08-29 [640178c92] Rename the conflict types for the origin differ cases.
6857Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
68582024-09-04 [6c2b5edec] Collect statistics about conflicts in logical replicatio
6859Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
68602025-03-24 [73eba5004] Detect and Log multiple_unique_conflicts type conflict.
6861-->
6862
6863      <listitem>
6864      <para>
6865      Log <link linkend="hot-standby-conflict">conflicts</link> while
6866      applying logical replication changes (Zhijie Hou, Nisha Moond)
6867      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9758174e2">&sect;</ulink>
6868      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;edcb71258">&sect;</ulink>
6869      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;640178c92">&sect;</ulink>
6870      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6c2b5edec">&sect;</ulink>
6871      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;73eba5004">&sect;</ulink>
6872      </para>
6873
6874      <para>
6875      Also report in new columns of <link
6876      linkend="monitoring-pg-stat-subscription-stats"><structname>pg_stat_subscription_stats</structname></link>.
6877      </para>
6878      </listitem>
6879
6880     </itemizedlist>
6881
6882    </sect4>
6883
6884   </sect3>
6885
6886   <sect3 id="release-18-utility">
6887    <title>Utility Commands</title>
6888
6889    <itemizedlist>
6890
6891<!--
6892Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
68932025-02-07 [83ea6c540] Virtual generated columns
6894Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
68952025-03-28 [cdc168ad4] Add support for not-null constraints on virtual generate
6896Author: Richard Guo <rguo@postgresql.org>
68972025-02-25 [1e4351af3] Expand virtual generated columns in the planner
6898-->
6899
6900     <listitem>
6901     <para>
6902     Allow <link linkend="sql-createtable-parms-generated-stored">generated
6903     columns</link> to be virtual, and make them the default (Peter
6904     Eisentraut, Jian He, Richard Guo, Dean Rasheed)
6905     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;83ea6c540">&sect;</ulink>
6906     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cdc168ad4">&sect;</ulink>
6907     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1e4351af3">&sect;</ulink>
6908     </para>
6909
6910     <para>
6911     Virtual generated columns generate their values when the columns
6912     are read, not written.  The write behavior can still be specified
6913     via the <literal>STORED</literal> option.
6914     </para>
6915     </listitem>
6916
6917<!--
6918Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
69192025-01-16 [80feb727c] Add OLD/NEW support to RETURNING in DML queries.
6920-->
6921
6922     <listitem>
6923     <para>
6924     Add <literal>OLD</literal>/<literal>NEW</literal> support to <link
6925     linkend="dml-returning"><literal>RETURNING</literal></link> in
6926     <acronym>DML</acronym> queries (Dean Rasheed)
6927     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;80feb727c">&sect;</ulink>
6928     </para>
6929
6930     <para>
6931     Previously <literal>RETURNING</literal> only returned new values for
6932     <xref linkend="sql-insert"/> and <xref linkend="sql-update"/>, and old
6933     values for <xref linkend="sql-delete"/>; <xref linkend="sql-merge"/>
6934     would return the appropriate value for the internal query executed.
6935     This new syntax allows the <literal>RETURNING</literal> list of
6936     <command>INSERT</command>/<command>UPDATE</command>/<command>DELETE</command>/<command>MERGE</command>
6937     to explicitly return old and new values by using the special aliases
6938     <literal>old</literal> and <literal>new</literal>.  These aliases
6939     can be renamed to avoid identifier conflicts.
6940     </para>
6941     </listitem>
6942
6943<!--
6944Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
69452025-02-19 [302cf1575] Add support for LIKE in CREATE FOREIGN TABLE
6946-->
6947
6948     <listitem>
6949     <para>
6950     Allow foreign tables to be created like existing local tables
6951     (Zhang Mingli)
6952     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;302cf1575">&sect;</ulink>
6953     </para>
6954
6955     <para>
6956     The syntax is <link linkend="sql-createforeigntable"><command>CREATE
6957     FOREIGN TABLE ... LIKE</command></link>.
6958     </para>
6959     </listitem>
6960
6961<!--
6962Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
69632024-11-27 [85b7efa1c] Support LIKE with nondeterministic collations
6964-->
6965
6966     <listitem>
6967     <para>
6968     Allow <link linkend="functions-like"><literal>LIKE</literal></link>
6969     with <link linkend="collation-nondeterministic">nondeterministic
6970     collations</link> (Peter Eisentraut)
6971     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;85b7efa1c">&sect;</ulink>
6972     </para>
6973     </listitem>
6974
6975<!--
6976Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
69772025-02-21 [329304c90] Support text position search functions with nondetermini
6978-->
6979
6980     <listitem>
6981     <para>
6982     Allow text position search functions with nondeterministic collations
6983     (Peter Eisentraut)
6984     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;329304c90">&sect;</ulink>
6985     </para>
6986
6987     <para>
6988     These used to generate an error.
6989     </para>
6990     </listitem>
6991
6992<!--
6993Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
69942025-01-17 [d3d098316] Support PG_UNICODE_FAST locale in the builtin collation
6995-->
6996
6997     <listitem>
6998     <para>
6999     Add builtin collation provider <link
7000     linkend="locale-providers"><literal>PG_UNICODE_FAST</literal></link>
7001     (Jeff Davis)
7002     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d3d098316">&sect;</ulink>
7003     </para>
7004
7005     <para>
7006     This locale supports case mapping, but sorts in code point order,
7007     not natural language order.
7008     </para>
7009     </listitem>
7010
7011<!--
7012Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
70132024-09-24 [62ddf7ee9] Add ONLY support for VACUUM and ANALYZE
7014-->
7015
7016     <listitem>
7017     <para>
7018     Allow <xref linkend="sql-vacuum"/> and <xref linkend="sql-analyze"/>
7019     to process partitioned tables without processing their children
7020     (Michael Harris)
7021     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;62ddf7ee9">&sect;</ulink>
7022     </para>
7023
7024     <para>
7025     This is enabled with the new <literal>ONLY</literal> option.  This is
7026     useful since autovacuum does not process partitioned tables, just
7027     its children.
7028     </para>
7029     </listitem>
7030
7031<!--
7032Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
70332024-10-11 [e839c8ecc] Create functions pg_set_relation_stats, pg_clear_relatio
7034Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
70352024-10-24 [d32d14639] Add functions pg_restore_relation_stats(), pg_restore_at
7036Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
70372025-03-25 [650ab8aaf] Stats: use schemaname/relname instead of regclass.
7038-->
7039
7040     <listitem>
7041     <para>
7042     Add functions to modify per-relation and per-column optimizer
7043     statistics (Corey Huinker)
7044     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e839c8ecc">&sect;</ulink>
7045     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d32d14639">&sect;</ulink>
7046     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;650ab8aaf">&sect;</ulink>
7047     </para>
7048
7049     <para>
7050     The functions are <link
7051     linkend="functions-admin-statsmod"><function>pg_restore_relation_stats()</function></link>,
7052     <function>pg_restore_attribute_stats()</function>,
7053     <function>pg_clear_relation_stats()</function>, and
7054     <function>pg_clear_attribute_stats()</function>.
7055     </para>
7056     </listitem>
7057
7058
7059<!--
7060Author: Thomas Munro <tmunro@postgresql.org>
70612025-04-08 [f78ca6f3e] Introduce file_copy_method setting.
7062-->
7063
7064     <listitem>
7065     <para>
7066     Add server variable <xref linkend="guc-file-copy-method"/> to control
7067     the file copying method (Nazir Bilal Yavuz)
7068     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f78ca6f3e">&sect;</ulink>
7069     </para>
7070
7071     <para>
7072     This controls whether <link
7073     linkend="sql-createdatabase"><command>CREATE DATABASE
7074     ... STRATEGY=FILE_COPY</command></link> and <link
7075     linkend="sql-alterdatabase"><command>ALTER DATABASE ... SET
7076     TABLESPACE</command></link> uses file copy or clone.
7077     </para>
7078     </listitem>
7079
7080    </itemizedlist>
7081
7082    <sect4 id="release-18-constraints">
7083     <title><link linkend="ddl-constraints">Constraints</link></title>
7084
7085     <itemizedlist>
7086
7087<!--
7088Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
70892024-09-17 [fc0438b4e] Add temporal PRIMARY KEY and UNIQUE constraints
7090Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
70912024-09-17 [89f908a6d] Add temporal FOREIGN KEY contraints
7092-->
7093
7094      <listitem>
7095      <para>
7096      Allow the specification of non-overlapping <link
7097      linkend="sql-createtable-parms-primary-key"><literal>PRIMARY
7098      KEY</literal></link>, <link linkend="sql-createtable-parms-unique">
7099      <literal>UNIQUE</literal></link>, and
7100      <link linkend="sql-createtable-parms-references">foreign key</link>
7101      constraints (Paul A. Jungwirth)
7102      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;fc0438b4e">&sect;</ulink>
7103      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;89f908a6d">&sect;</ulink>
7104      </para>
7105
7106      <para>
7107      This is specified by <literal>WITHOUT OVERLAPS</literal> for
7108      <literal>PRIMARY KEY</literal> and <literal>UNIQUE</literal>, and by
7109      <literal>PERIOD</literal> for foreign keys, all applied to the last
7110      specified column.
7111      </para>
7112      </listitem>
7113
7114<!--
7115Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
71162025-01-11 [ca87c415e] Add support for NOT ENFORCED in CHECK constraints
7117Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
71182025-04-02 [eec0040c4] Add support for NOT ENFORCED in foreign key constraints
7119-->
7120
7121      <listitem>
7122      <para>
7123      Allow <link
7124      linkend="sql-createtable-parms-check"><literal>CHECK</literal></link>
7125      and <link linkend="sql-createtable-parms-references">foreign
7126      key</link> constraints to be specified as <literal>NOT
7127      ENFORCED</literal> (Amul Sul)
7128      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ca87c415e">&sect;</ulink>
7129      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;eec0040c4">&sect;</ulink>
7130      </para>
7131
7132      <para>
7133      This also adds column <link
7134      linkend="catalog-pg-constraint"><structname>pg_constraint</structname></link>.<structfield>conenforced</structfield>.
7135      </para>
7136      </listitem>
7137
7138<!--
7139Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
71402024-11-15 [9321d2fdf] Fix collation handling for foreign keys
7141-->
7142
7143      <listitem>
7144      <para>
7145      Require <link
7146      linkend="sql-createtable-parms-references">primary/foreign key</link>
7147      relationships to use either deterministic collations or the the
7148      same nondeterministic collations (Peter Eisentraut)
7149      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9321d2fdf">&sect;</ulink>
7150      </para>
7151
7152      <para>
7153      The restore of a <xref linkend="app-pgdump"/>, also used by <xref
7154      linkend="pgupgrade"/>, will fail if these requirements are not met;
7155      schema changes must be made for these upgrade methods to succeed.
7156      </para>
7157      </listitem>
7158
7159<!--
7160Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@alvh.no-ip.org>
71612024-11-08 [14e87ffa5] Add pg_constraint rows for not-null constraints
7162Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
7163Branch: master Release: REL_18_BR [81ce602d4] 2025-06-26 20:25:34 +0900
7164-->
7165
7166      <listitem>
7167      <para>
7168      Store column <link
7169      linkend="sql-createtable-parms-not-null"><literal>NOT
7170      NULL</literal></link> specifications in <link
7171      linkend="catalog-pg-constraint"><structname>pg_constraint</structname></link>
7172      (Álvaro Herrera, Bernd Helmle)
7173      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;14e87ffa5">&sect;</ulink>
7174      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;81ce602d4">&sect;</ulink>
7175      </para>
7176
7177      <para>
7178      This allows names to be specified for <literal>NOT NULL</literal>
7179      constraint.  This also adds <literal>NOT NULL</literal> constraints
7180      to foreign tables and <literal>NOT NULL</literal> inheritance
7181      control to local tables.
7182      </para>
7183      </listitem>
7184
7185<!--
7186Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@alvh.no-ip.org>
71872025-04-07 [a379061a2] Allow NOT NULL constraints to be added as NOT VALID
7188-->
7189
7190      <listitem>
7191      <para>
7192      Allow <xref linkend="sql-altertable"/> to set the <literal>NOT
7193      VALID</literal> attribute of <literal>NOT NULL</literal> constraints
7194      (Rushabh Lathia, Jian He)
7195      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a379061a2">&sect;</ulink>
7196      </para>
7197      </listitem>
7198
7199<!--
7200Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@alvh.no-ip.org>
72012025-03-05 [f4e53e10b] Add ALTER TABLE ... ALTER CONSTRAINT ... SET [NO] INHERI
7202Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@alvh.no-ip.org>
72032025-03-27 [4a02af8b1] Simplify syntax for ALTER TABLE ALTER CONSTRAINT NO INHE
7204-->
7205
7206      <listitem>
7207      <para>
7208      Allow modification of the inheritability of <literal>NOT
7209      NULL</literal> constraints (Suraj Kharage, Álvaro Herrera)
7210      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f4e53e10b">&sect;</ulink>
7211      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4a02af8b1">&sect;</ulink>
7212      </para>
7213
7214      <para>
7215      The syntax is <link linkend="sql-altertable"><command>ALTER TABLE
7216      ... ALTER CONSTRAINT ... [NO] INHERIT</command></link>.
7217      </para>
7218      </listitem>
7219
7220<!--
7221Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@alvh.no-ip.org>
72222025-01-23 [b663b9436] Allow NOT VALID foreign key constraints on partitioned t
7223-->
7224
7225      <listitem>
7226      <para>
7227      Allow <literal>NOT VALID</literal> foreign key constraints on
7228      partitioned tables (Amul Sul)
7229      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b663b9436">&sect;</ulink>
7230      </para>
7231      </listitem>
7232
7233<!--
7234Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@alvh.no-ip.org>
72352024-09-30 [4dea33ce7] Don't disallow DROP of constraints ONLY on partitioned t
7236-->
7237
7238      <listitem>
7239      <para>
7240      Allow <link
7241      linkend="sql-altertable-desc-drop-constraint">dropping</link>
7242      of constraints <literal>ONLY</literal> on partitioned tables
7243      (Álvaro Herrera)
7244      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4dea33ce7">&sect;</ulink>
7245      </para>
7246
7247      <para>
7248      This was previously erroneously prohibited.
7249      </para>
7250      </listitem>
7251
7252     </itemizedlist>
7253    </sect4>
7254
7255    <sect4 id="release-18-copy">
7256     <title><xref linkend="sql-copy"/></title>
7257
7258     <itemizedlist>
7259
7260<!--
7261Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
72622024-10-08 [4ac2a9bec] Add REJECT_LIMIT option to the COPY command.
7263-->
7264
7265      <listitem>
7266      <para>
7267      Add <literal>REJECT_LIMIT</literal> to control the number of invalid
7268      rows <command>COPY FROM</command> can ignore (Atsushi Torikoshi)
7269      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4ac2a9bec">&sect;</ulink>
7270      </para>
7271
7272      <para>
7273      This is available when <literal>ON_ERROR = 'ignore'</literal>.
7274      </para>
7275      </listitem>
7276
7277<!--
7278Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
72792025-04-04 [534874fac] Allow "COPY table TO" command to copy rows from material
7280-->
7281
7282      <listitem>
7283      <para>
7284      Allow <command>COPY TO</command> to copy rows from populated
7285      materialized views (Jian He)
7286      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;534874fac">&sect;</ulink>
7287      </para>
7288      </listitem>
7289
7290<!--
7291Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
72922024-10-03 [e7834a1a2] Add LOG_VERBOSITY = 'silent' support to COPY command.
7293-->
7294
7295      <listitem>
7296      <para>
7297      Add <command>COPY</command> <literal>LOG_VERBOSITY</literal> level
7298      <literal>silent</literal> to suppress log output of ignored rows
7299      (Atsushi Torikoshi)
7300      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e7834a1a2">&sect;</ulink>
7301      </para>
7302
7303      <para>
7304      This new level suppresses output for discarded input rows when
7305      <literal>on_error = 'ignore'</literal>.
7306      </para>
7307      </listitem>
7308
7309<!--
7310Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
73112025-02-06 [401a6956f] Disallow COPY FREEZE on foreign tables.
7312-->
7313
7314      <listitem>
7315      <para>
7316      Disallow <command>COPY FREEZE</command> on foreign tables (Nathan
7317      Bossart)
7318      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;401a6956f">&sect;</ulink>
7319      </para>
7320
7321      <para>
7322      Previously, the <command>COPY</command> worked but the
7323      <literal>FREEZE</literal> was ignored, so disallow this command.
7324      </para>
7325      </listitem>
7326
7327     </itemizedlist>
7328    </sect4>
7329
7330    <sect4 id="release-18-explain">
7331     <title><xref linkend="sql-explain"/></title>
7332
7333     <itemizedlist>
7334
7335<!--
7336Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
73372024-12-11 [c2a4078eb] Enable BUFFERS with EXPLAIN ANALYZE by default
7338-->
7339
7340      <listitem>
7341      <para>
7342      Automatically include <literal>BUFFERS</literal> output in
7343      <command>EXPLAIN ANALYZE</command> (Guillaume Lelarge, David Rowley)
7344      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c2a4078eb">&sect;</ulink>
7345      </para>
7346      </listitem>
7347
7348<!--
7349Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
73502025-02-17 [320545bfc] Add information about WAL buffers being full to EXPLAIN
7351-->
7352
7353      <listitem>
7354      <para>
7355      Add full <acronym>WAL</acronym> buffer count to <command>EXPLAIN
7356      (WAL)</command> output (Bertrand Drouvot)
7357      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;320545bfc">&sect;</ulink>
7358      </para>
7359      </listitem>
7360
7361<!--
7362Author: Peter Geoghegan <pg@bowt.ie>
73632025-03-11 [0fbceae84] Show index search count in EXPLAIN ANALYZE, take 2.
7364-->
7365
7366      <listitem>
7367      <para>
7368      In <command>EXPLAIN ANALYZE</command>, report the number of index
7369      lookups used per index scan node (Peter Geoghegan)
7370      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0fbceae84">&sect;</ulink>
7371      </para>
7372      </listitem>
7373
7374<!--
7375Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
73762025-02-21 [ddb17e387] Allow EXPLAIN to indicate fractional rows.
7377Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
73782025-02-27 [95dbd827f] EXPLAIN: Always use two fractional digits for row counts
7379-->
7380
7381      <listitem>
7382      <para>
7383      Modify <command>EXPLAIN</command> to output fractional row counts
7384      (Ibrar Ahmed, Ilia Evdokimov, Robert Haas)
7385      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ddb17e387">&sect;</ulink>
7386      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;95dbd827f">&sect;</ulink>
7387      </para>
7388      </listitem>
7389
7390<!--
7391Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
73922024-07-05 [1eff8279d] Add memory/disk usage for Material nodes in EXPLAIN
7393Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
73942024-07-05 [53abb1e0e] Fix newly introduced issue in EXPLAIN for Materialize no
7395Author: Tatsuo Ishii <ishii@postgresql.org>
73962024-09-17 [95d6e9af0] Add memory/disk usage for Window aggregate nodes in EXPL
7397Author: Tatsuo Ishii <ishii@postgresql.org>
73982024-09-23 [40708acd6] Add memory/disk usage for more executor nodes.
7399-->
7400
7401      <listitem>
7402      <para>
7403      Add memory and disk usage details to <literal>Material</literal>,
7404      <literal>Window Aggregate</literal>, and common table expression
7405      nodes to <command>EXPLAIN</command> output (David Rowley, Tatsuo
7406      Ishii)
7407      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1eff8279d">&sect;</ulink>
7408      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;53abb1e0e">&sect;</ulink>
7409      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;95d6e9af0">&sect;</ulink>
7410      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;40708acd6">&sect;</ulink>
7411      </para>
7412      </listitem>
7413
7414
7415<!--
7416Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
74172025-03-11 [8b1b34254] Improve EXPLAIN's display of window functions.
7418-->
7419
7420      <listitem>
7421      <para>
7422      Add details about window function arguments to
7423      <command>EXPLAIN</command> output (Tom Lane)
7424      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8b1b34254">&sect;</ulink>
7425      </para>
7426      </listitem>
7427
7428<!--
7429Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
74302024-07-09 [5a1e6df3b] Show Parallel Bitmap Heap Scan worker stats in EXPLAIN A
7431-->
7432
7433      <listitem>
7434      <para>
7435      Add <literal>Parallel Bitmap Heap Scan</literal> worker cache
7436      statistics to <command>EXPLAIN ANALYZE</command> (David Geier,
7437      Heikki Linnakangas, Donghang Lin, Alena Rybakina, David Rowley)
7438      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;5a1e6df3b">&sect;</ulink>
7439      </para>
7440      </listitem>
7441
7442<!--
7443Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
74442024-08-21 [c01743aa4] Show number of disabled nodes in EXPLAIN ANALYZE output.
7445Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
74462024-10-11 [161320b4b] Adjust EXPLAIN's output for disabled nodes
7447Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
74482024-10-29 [84b8fccbe] Doc: add detail about EXPLAIN's "Disabled" property
7449-->
7450
7451      <listitem>
7452      <para>
7453      Indicate disabled nodes in <command>EXPLAIN ANALYZE</command> output
7454      (Robert Haas, David Rowley, Laurenz Albe)
7455      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c01743aa4">&sect;</ulink>
7456      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;161320b4b">&sect;</ulink>
7457      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;84b8fccbe">&sect;</ulink>
7458      </para>
7459      </listitem>
7460
7461     </itemizedlist>
7462
7463    </sect4>
7464
7465   </sect3>
7466
7467   <sect3 id="release-18-datatypes">
7468    <title>Data Types</title>
7469
7470    <itemizedlist>
7471
7472<!--
7473Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
74742025-01-23 [4e7f62bc3] Add support for Unicode case folding.
7475Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
74762025-01-17 [286a365b9] Support Unicode full case mapping and conversion.
7477-->
7478
7479     <listitem>
7480     <para>
7481     Improve <link linkend="collation-managing-standard">Unicode</link>
7482     full case mapping and conversion (Jeff Davis)
7483     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4e7f62bc3">&sect;</ulink>
7484     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;286a365b9">&sect;</ulink>
7485     </para>
7486
7487     <para>
7488     This adds the ability to do conditional and title case mapping,
7489     and case map single characters to multiple characters.
7490     </para>
7491     </listitem>
7492
7493<!--
7494Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
74952025-01-24 [a5579a90a] Make jsonb casts to scalar types translate JSON null to
7496-->
7497
7498     <listitem>
7499     <para>
7500     Allow <link linkend="datatype-json"><type>jsonb</type></link>
7501     <literal>null</literal> values to be cast to scalar types as
7502     <literal>NULL</literal> (Tom Lane)
7503     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a5579a90a">&sect;</ulink>
7504     </para>
7505
7506     <para>
7507     Previously such casts generated an error.
7508     </para>
7509     </listitem>
7510
7511<!--
7512Author: Andrew Dunstan <andrew@dunslane.net>
75132025-03-05 [4603903d2] Allow json{b}_strip_nulls() to remove null array elements
7514-->
7515
7516     <listitem>
7517     <para>
7518     Add optional parameter to <link
7519     linkend="functions-json-processing-table"><function>json{b}_strip_nulls</function></link>
7520     to allow removal of null array elements (Florents Tselai)
7521     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4603903d2">&sect;</ulink>
7522     </para>
7523     </listitem>
7524
7525<!--
7526Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
75272025-04-01 [6c12ae09f] Introduce a SQL-callable function array_sort(anyarray).
7528-->
7529
7530     <listitem>
7531     <para>
7532     Add function <link
7533     linkend="array-functions-table"><function>array_sort()</function></link>
7534     which sorts an array's first dimension (Junwang Zhao, Jian He)
7535     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6c12ae09f">&sect;</ulink>
7536     </para>
7537     </listitem>
7538
7539<!--
7540Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
75412024-11-01 [49d6c7d8d] Add SQL function array_reverse()
7542-->
7543
7544     <listitem>
7545     <para>
7546     Add function <link
7547     linkend="array-functions-table"><function>array_reverse()</function></link>
7548     which reverses an array's first dimension (Aleksander Alekseev)
7549     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;49d6c7d8d">&sect;</ulink>
7550     </para>
7551     </listitem>
7552
7553<!--
7554Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
75552025-03-13 [0697b2390] Add reverse(bytea).
7556-->
7557
7558     <listitem>
7559     <para>
7560     Add function <link
7561     linkend="functions-string-other"><function>reverse()</function></link>
7562     to reverse bytea bytes (Aleksander Alekseev)
7563     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0697b2390">&sect;</ulink>
7564     </para>
7565     </listitem>
7566
7567<!--
7568Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
75692025-03-07 [6da469bad] Allow casting between bytea and integer types.
7570-->
7571
7572     <listitem>
7573     <para>
7574     Allow casting between integer types and <link
7575     linkend="datatype-binary"><type>bytea</type></link> (Aleksander
7576     Alekseev)
7577     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6da469bad">&sect;</ulink>
7578     </para>
7579
7580     <para>
7581     The integer values are stored as <type>bytea</type> two's complement
7582     values.
7583     </para>
7584     </listitem>
7585
7586<!--
7587Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
75882025-04-03 [82a46cca9] Update Unicode data to Unicode 16.0.0
7589-->
7590
7591     <listitem>
7592     <para>
7593     Update Unicode data to <link
7594     linkend="collation-managing-standard">Unicode</link> 16.0.0 (Peter
7595     Eisentraut)
7596     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;82a46cca9">&sect;</ulink>
7597     </para>
7598     </listitem>
7599
7600<!--
7601Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
76022025-02-18 [b464e51ab] Update to latest Snowball sources.
7603-->
7604
7605     <listitem>
7606     <para>
7607     Add full text search <link
7608     linkend="textsearch-snowball-dictionary">stemming</link> for Estonian
7609     (Tom Lane)
7610     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b464e51ab">&sect;</ulink>
7611     </para>
7612     </listitem>
7613
7614<!--
7615Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
76162024-09-24 [cd838e200] Neaten up our choices of SQLSTATEs for XML-related error
7617-->
7618
7619     <listitem>
7620     <para>
7621     Improve the <link linkend="datatype-xml"><type>XML</type></link>
7622     error codes to more closely match the <acronym>SQL</acronym> standard
7623     (Tom Lane)
7624     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cd838e200">&sect;</ulink>
7625     </para>
7626
7627     <para>
7628     These errors are reported via <link
7629     linkend="errcodes-appendix"><literal>SQLSTATE</literal></link>.
7630     </para>
7631     </listitem>
7632
7633    </itemizedlist>
7634
7635   </sect3>
7636
7637   <sect3 id="release-18-functions">
7638    <title>Functions</title>
7639
7640    <itemizedlist>
7641
7642<!--
7643Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
76442025-01-24 [bfc599206] Add SQL function CASEFOLD().
7645-->
7646
7647     <listitem>
7648     <para>
7649     Add function <link
7650     linkend="functions-string-other"><function>casefold()</function></link>
7651     to allow for more sophisticated case-insensitive matching (Jeff Davis)
7652     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bfc599206">&sect;</ulink>
7653     </para>
7654
7655     <para>
7656     This allows more accurate comparisons, i.e., a character can have
7657     multiple upper or lower case equivalents, or upper or lower case
7658     conversion changes the number of characters.
7659     </para>
7660     </listitem>
7661
7662<!--
7663Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
76642024-07-11 [a0f1fce80] Add min and max aggregates for composite types (records)
7665Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
76662024-10-08 [2d24fd942] Add min and max aggregates for bytea type.
7667-->
7668
7669     <listitem>
7670     <para>
7671     Allow <link
7672     linkend="functions-aggregate-table"><function>MIN()</function></link>/<link
7673     linkend="functions-aggregate-table"><function>MAX()</function></link>
7674     aggregates on arrays and composite types (Aleksander Alekseev,
7675     Marat Buharov)
7676     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a0f1fce80">&sect;</ulink>
7677     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;2d24fd942">&sect;</ulink>
7678     </para>
7679     </listitem>
7680
7681<!--
7682Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
76832024-08-16 [6be39d77a] Fix extraction of week and quarter fields from intervals
7684-->
7685
7686     <listitem>
7687     <para>
7688     Add a <literal>WEEK</literal> option to <link
7689     linkend="functions-datetime-extract"><function>EXTRACT()</function></link>
7690     (Tom Lane)
7691     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6be39d77a">&sect;</ulink>
7692     </para>
7693     </listitem>
7694
7695<!--
7696Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
76972024-08-16 [6be39d77a] Fix extraction of week and quarter fields from intervals
7698-->
7699
7700     <listitem>
7701     <para>
7702     Improve the output <literal>EXTRACT(QUARTER ...)</literal> for
7703     negative values (Tom Lane)
7704     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6be39d77a">&sect;</ulink>
7705     </para>
7706     </listitem>
7707
7708<!--
7709Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
77102025-01-22 [172e6b3ad] Support RN (roman-numeral format) in to_number().
7711-->
7712
7713     <listitem>
7714     <para>
7715     Add roman numeral support to <link
7716     linkend="functions-formatting-table"><function>to_number()</function></link>
7717     (Hunaid Sohail)
7718     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;172e6b3ad">&sect;</ulink>
7719     </para>
7720
7721     <para>
7722     This is accessed via the <literal>RN</literal> pattern.
7723     </para>
7724     </listitem>
7725
7726<!--
7727Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
77282024-12-11 [78c5e141e] Add UUID version 7 generation function.
7729-->
7730
7731     <listitem>
7732     <para>
7733     Add <link linkend="datatype-uuid"><type>UUID</type></link>
7734     version 7 generation function <link
7735     linkend="func_uuid_gen_table"><function>uuidv7()</function></link>
7736     (Andrey Borodin)
7737     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;78c5e141e">&sect;</ulink>
7738     </para>
7739
7740     <para>
7741     This <type>UUID</type> value is
7742     temporally sortable.  Function alias <link
7743     linkend="func_uuid_gen_table"><function>uuidv4()</function></link>
7744     has been added to explicitly generate version 4 UUIDs.
7745     </para>
7746     </listitem>
7747
7748<!--
7749Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
77502024-08-12 [760162fed] Add user-callable CRC functions.
7751-->
7752
7753     <listitem>
7754     <para>
7755     Add functions <link
7756     linkend="functions-binarystring-other"><function>crc32()</function></link>
7757     and <link
7758     linkend="functions-binarystring-other"><function>crc32c()</function></link>
7759     to compute <acronym>CRC</acronym> values (Aleksander Alekseev)
7760     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;760162fed">&sect;</ulink>
7761     </para>
7762     </listitem>
7763
7764<!--
7765Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
77662025-03-26 [a3b6dfd41] Add support for gamma() and lgamma() functions.
7767-->
7768
7769     <listitem>
7770     <para>
7771     Add math functions <link
7772     linkend="functions-math-func-table"><function>gamma()</function></link>
7773     and <link
7774     linkend="functions-math-func-table"><function>lgamma()</function></link>
7775     (Dean Rasheed)
7776     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a3b6dfd41">&sect;</ulink>
7777     </para>
7778     </listitem>
7779
7780<!--
7781Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
77822025-03-03 [246dedc5d] Allow =&gt; syntax for named cursor arguments in plpgsql.
7783-->
7784
7785     <listitem>
7786     <para>
7787     Allow <literal>=&gt;</literal> syntax for named cursor arguments in
7788     <link linkend="plpgsql">PL/pgSQL</link> (Pavel Stehule)
7789     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;246dedc5d">&sect;</ulink>
7790     </para>
7791
7792     <para>
7793     We previously only accepted <literal>:=</literal>.
7794     </para>
7795     </listitem>
7796
7797<!--
7798Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
77992024-07-25 [580f8727c] Add argument names to the regexp_XXX functions.
7800-->
7801
7802     <listitem>
7803     <para>
7804     Allow <link
7805     linkend="functions-posix-regexp"><function>regexp_match[es]()</function></link>/<link
7806     linkend="functions-posix-regexp"><function>regexp_like()</function></link>/<link
7807     linkend="functions-posix-regexp"><function>regexp_replace()</function></link>/<link
7808     linkend="functions-posix-regexp"><function>regexp_count()</function></link>/<link
7809     linkend="functions-posix-regexp"><function>regexp_instr()</function></link>/<link
7810     linkend="functions-posix-regexp"><function>regexp_substr()</function></link>/<link
7811     linkend="functions-posix-regexp"><function>regexp_split_to_table()</function></link>/<link
7812     linkend="functions-posix-regexp"><function>regexp_split_to_array()</function></link>
7813     to use named arguments (Jian He)
7814     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;580f8727c">&sect;</ulink>
7815     </para>
7816     </listitem>
7817
7818    </itemizedlist>
7819
7820   </sect3>
7821
7822   <sect3 id="release-18-libpq">
7823    <title><link linkend="libpq">Libpq</link></title>
7824
7825    <itemizedlist>
7826
7827<!--
7828Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
78292024-09-09 [cdb6b0fdb] Add PQfullProtocolVersion() to surface the precise proto
7830-->
7831
7832     <listitem>
7833     <para>
7834     Add function <link
7835     linkend="libpq-PQfullProtocolVersion"><function>PQfullProtocolVersion()</function></link>
7836     to report the full, including minor, protocol version number (Jacob
7837     Champion, Jelte Fennema-Nio)
7838     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cdb6b0fdb">&sect;</ulink>
7839     </para>
7840     </listitem>
7841
7842<!--
7843Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
78442025-04-02 [285613c60] libpq: Add min/max_protocol_version connection options
7845Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
78462025-04-02 [507034910] libpq: Handle NegotiateProtocolVersion message different
7847-->
7848
7849     <listitem>
7850     <para>
7851     Add libpq connection <link
7852     linkend="libpq-connect-ssl-max-protocol-version">parameters</link>
7853     and <link linkend="libpq-envars">environment variables</link> to
7854     specify the minimum and maximum acceptable protocol version for
7855     connections (Jelte Fennema-Nio)
7856     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;285613c60">&sect;</ulink>
7857     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;507034910">&sect;</ulink>
7858     </para>
7859     </listitem>
7860
7861<!--
7862Author: Tomas Vondra <tomas.vondra@postgresql.org>
78632024-08-19 [28a1121fd] Mark search_path as GUC_REPORT
7864Author: Tomas Vondra <tomas.vondra@postgresql.org>
78652024-08-19 [0d06a7eac] Document that search_path is reported by the server
7866-->
7867
7868     <listitem>
7869     <para>
7870     Report <xref linkend="guc-search-path"/> changes to the client
7871     (Alexander Kukushkin, Jelte Fennema-Nio, Tomas Vondra)
7872     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;28a1121fd">&sect;</ulink>
7873     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0d06a7eac">&sect;</ulink>
7874     </para>
7875     </listitem>
7876
7877<!--
7878Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@alvh.no-ip.org>
78792024-08-12 [ea92f3a0a] libpq: Trace frontend authentication challenges
7880Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@alvh.no-ip.org>
78812024-08-14 [a5c6b8f22] libpq: Trace responses to SSLRequest and GSSENCRequest
7882Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@alvh.no-ip.org>
78832024-08-16 [b8b3f861f] libpq: Trace all messages received from the server
7884Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
78852025-02-24 [e87c14b19] libpq: Trace all NegotiateProtocolVersion fields
7886Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@alvh.no-ip.org>
78872024-08-09 [7adec2d5f] libpq: Trace StartupMessage/SSLRequest/GSSENCRequest cor
7888-->
7889
7890     <listitem>
7891     <para>
7892     Add <link
7893     linkend="libpq-PQtrace"><function>PQtrace()</function></link> output
7894     for all message types, including authentication (Jelte Fennema-Nio)
7895     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ea92f3a0a">&sect;</ulink>
7896     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a5c6b8f22">&sect;</ulink>
7897     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b8b3f861f">&sect;</ulink>
7898     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e87c14b19">&sect;</ulink>
7899     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7adec2d5f">&sect;</ulink>
7900     </para>
7901     </listitem>
7902
7903<!--
7904Author: Daniel Gustafsson <dgustafsson@postgresql.org>
79052025-04-03 [2da74d8d6] libpq: Add support for dumping SSL key material to file
7906-->
7907
7908     <listitem>
7909     <para>
7910     Add libpq connection parameter <link
7911     linkend="libpq-connect-sslkeylogfile"><varname>sslkeylogfile</varname></link>
7912     which dumps out <acronym>SSL</acronym> key material (Abhishek Chanda,
7913     Daniel Gustafsson)
7914     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;2da74d8d6">&sect;</ulink>
7915     </para>
7916
7917     <para>
7918     This is useful for debugging.
7919     </para>
7920     </listitem>
7921
7922<!--
7923Author: Thomas Munro <tmunro@postgresql.org>
79242025-03-25 [3c86223c9] libpq: Deprecate pg_int64.
7925-->
7926
7927     <listitem>
7928     <para>
7929     Modify some libpq function signatures to use
7930     <literal>int64_t</literal> (Thomas Munro)
7931     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3c86223c9">&sect;</ulink>
7932     </para>
7933
7934     <para>
7935     These previously used <literal>pg_int64</literal>, which is now
7936     deprecated.
7937     </para>
7938     </listitem>
7939
7940    </itemizedlist>
7941
7942   </sect3>
7943
7944   <sect3 id="release-18-psql">
7945    <title><xref linkend="app-psql"/></title>
7946
7947    <itemizedlist>
7948
7949<!--
7950Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
79512024-08-22 [d55322b0d] psql: Add more meta-commands able to use the extended pr
7952Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
7953Branch: master Release: REL_18_BR [fc39b286a] 2025-06-24 13:12:46 +0900
7954-->
7955
7956     <listitem>
7957     <para>
7958     Allow <application>psql</application> to parse, bind, and close
7959     named prepared statements (Anthonin Bonnefoy, Michael Paquier)
7960     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d55322b0d">&sect;</ulink>
7961     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;fc39b286a">&sect;</ulink>
7962     </para>
7963
7964     <para>
7965     This is accomplished with new commands <link
7966     linkend="app-psql-meta-command-parse"><literal>\parse</literal></link>,
7967     <link
7968     linkend="app-psql-meta-command-bind-named"><literal>\bind_named</literal></link>,
7969     and <link
7970     linkend="app-psql-meta-command-close-prepared"><literal>\close_prepared</literal></link>.
7971     </para>
7972     </listitem>
7973
7974<!--
7975Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
79762025-02-21 [41625ab8e] psql: Add support for pipelines
7977Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
79782025-03-18 [17caf6644] psql: Add \sendpipeline to send query buffers while in a
7979Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
79802025-03-19 [2cce0fe44] psql: Allow queries terminated by semicolons while in pi
7981-->
7982
7983     <listitem>
7984     <para>
7985     Add <application>psql</application> backslash commands to allowing
7986     issuance of pipeline queries (Anthonin Bonnefoy)
7987     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;41625ab8e">&sect;</ulink>
7988     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;17caf6644">&sect;</ulink>
7989     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;2cce0fe44">&sect;</ulink>
7990     </para>
7991
7992     <para>
7993     The new commands are <link
7994     linkend="app-psql-meta-command-pipeline"><literal>\startpipeline</literal></link>,
7995     <literal>\syncpipeline</literal>, <literal>\sendpipeline</literal>,
7996     <literal>\endpipeline</literal>, <literal>\flushrequest</literal>,
7997     <literal>\flush</literal>, and <literal>\getresults</literal>.
7998     </para>
7999     </listitem>
8000
8001<!--
8002Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
80032025-02-25 [3ce357584] psql: Add pipeline status to prompt and some state varia
8004-->
8005
8006     <listitem>
8007     <para>
8008     Allow adding pipeline status to the <application>psql</application>
8009     prompt and add related state variables (Anthonin Bonnefoy)
8010     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3ce357584">&sect;</ulink>
8011     </para>
8012
8013     <para>
8014     The new prompt character is <literal>%P</literal> and
8015     the new <application>psql</application> variables are <link
8016     linkend="app-psql-variables-pipeline-sync-count"><literal>PIPELINE_SYNC_COUNT</literal></link>,
8017     <link
8018     linkend="app-psql-variables-pipeline-command-count"><literal>PIPELINE_COMMAND_COUNT</literal></link>,
8019     and <link
8020     linkend="app-psql-variables-pipeline-result-count"><literal>PIPELINE_RESULT_COUNT</literal></link>.
8021     </para>
8022     </listitem>
8023
8024<!--
8025Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
80262024-12-18 [477728b5d] psql: Add more information about service name
8027-->
8028
8029     <listitem>
8030     <para>
8031     Allow adding the connection service name to the
8032     <application>psql</application> prompt or access it via
8033     <application>psql</application> variable (Michael Banck)
8034     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;477728b5d">&sect;</ulink>
8035     </para>
8036     </listitem>
8037
8038<!--
8039Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
80402025-01-14 [00f4c2959] psql: Add option to use expanded mode to all list comman
8041-->
8042
8043     <listitem>
8044     <para>
8045     Add <application>psql</application> option to use expanded mode on
8046     all list commands (Dean Rasheed)
8047     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;00f4c2959">&sect;</ulink>
8048     </para>
8049
8050     <para>
8051     Adding backslash suffix <literal>x</literal> enables this.
8052     </para>
8053     </listitem>
8054
8055<!--
8056Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@alvh.no-ip.org>
80572025-02-22 [bba2fbc62] Change \conninfo to use tabular format
8058-->
8059
8060     <listitem>
8061     <para>
8062     Change <application>psql</application>'s <xref
8063     linkend="app-psql-meta-command-conninfo"/> to use tabular format
8064     and include more information (Álvaro Herrera, Maiquel Grassi,
8065     Hunaid Sohail)
8066     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bba2fbc62">&sect;</ulink>
8067     </para>
8068     </listitem>
8069
8070<!--
8071Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
80722025-01-14 [2355e5111] psql: Add leakproof indicator to \df+, \do+, \dAo+, and
8073-->
8074
8075     <listitem>
8076     <para>
8077     Add function's leakproof indicator
8078     to <application>psql</application>'s <link
8079     linkend="app-psql-meta-command-df-lc"><literal>\df+</literal></link>,
8080     <literal>\do+</literal>, <literal>\dAo+</literal>, and
8081     <literal>\dC+</literal> outputs (Yugo Nagata)
8082     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;2355e5111">&sect;</ulink>
8083     </para>
8084     </listitem>
8085
8086<!--
8087Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
80882024-07-02 [978f38c77] Add information about access method for partitioned rela
8089-->
8090
8091     <listitem>
8092     <para>
8093     Add access method details for partitioned relations in <link
8094     linkend="app-psql-meta-command-dp-uc"><literal>\dP+</literal></link>
8095     (Justin Pryzby)
8096     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;978f38c77">&sect;</ulink>
8097     </para>
8098     </listitem>
8099
8100<!--
8101Author: Magnus Hagander <magnus@hagander.net>
81022025-03-24 [d696406a9] psql: show default extension version in \dx output
8103-->
8104
8105     <listitem>
8106     <para>
8107     Add <literal>default_version</literal>
8108     to the <application>psql</application> <link
8109     linkend="app-psql-meta-command-dx-lc"><literal>\dx</literal></link>
8110     extension output (Magnus Hagander)
8111     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d696406a9">&sect;</ulink>
8112     </para>
8113     </listitem>
8114
8115<!--
8116Author: Daniel Gustafsson <dgustafsson@postgresql.org>
81172025-03-25 [1a759c832] psql: Make default \watch interval configurable
8118-->
8119
8120     <listitem>
8121     <para>
8122     Add <application>psql</application> variable <xref
8123     linkend="app-psql-variables-watch-interval"/> to set the default <link
8124     linkend="app-psql-meta-command-watch"><literal>\watch</literal></link>
8125     wait time (Daniel Gustafsson)
8126     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1a759c832">&sect;</ulink>
8127     </para>
8128     </listitem>
8129
8130    </itemizedlist>
8131
8132   </sect3>
8133
8134   <sect3 id="release-18-server-apps">
8135    <title>Server Applications</title>
8136
8137    <itemizedlist>
8138
8139<!--
8140Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
81412024-10-01 [983a588e0] initdb: Add new option "- -no-data-checksums"
8142Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
81432024-10-16 [04bec894a] initdb: Change default to using data checksums.
8144-->
8145
8146     <listitem>
8147     <para>
8148     Change <xref linkend="app-initdb"/> to default to enabling checksums
8149     (Greg Sabino Mullane)
8150     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;983a588e0">&sect;</ulink>
8151     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;04bec894a">&sect;</ulink>
8152     </para>
8153
8154     <para>
8155     The new <application>initdb</application> option
8156     <option>--no-data-checksums</option> disables checksums.
8157     </para>
8158     </listitem>
8159
8160<!--
8161Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
81622025-03-25 [cf131fa94] initdb: Add - -no-sync-data-files.
8163-->
8164
8165     <listitem>
8166     <para>
8167     Add <application>initdb</application> option
8168     <option>--no-sync-data-files</option> to avoid syncing heap/index
8169     files (Nathan Bossart)
8170     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cf131fa94">&sect;</ulink>
8171     </para>
8172
8173     <para>
8174     <application>initdb</application> option <option>--no-sync</option>
8175     is still available to avoid syncing any files.
8176     </para>
8177     </listitem>
8178
8179<!--
8180Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
81812025-03-18 [edba754f0] vacuumdb: Add option for analyzing only relations missin
8182Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
81832025-04-30 [987910502] vacuumdb: Don't skip empty relations in - -missing-stats-
8184-->
8185
8186     <listitem>
8187     <para>
8188     Add <xref linkend="app-vacuumdb"/> option
8189     <option>--missing-stats-only</option> to compute only missing
8190     optimizer statistics (Corey Huinker, Nathan Bossart)
8191     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;edba754f0">&sect;</ulink>
8192     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;987910502">&sect;</ulink>
8193     </para>
8194
8195     <para>
8196     This option can only be run by superusers and can only
8197     be used with options <option>--analyze-only</option> and
8198     <option>--analyze-in-stages</option>.
8199     </para>
8200     </listitem>
8201
8202<!--
8203Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
82042025-03-17 [99aeb8470] pg_combinebackup: Add -k, - -link option.
8205-->
8206
8207     <listitem>
8208     <para>
8209     Add <xref linkend="app-pgcombinebackup"/> option
8210     <option>-k</option>/<option>--link</option> to enable hard linking
8211     (Israel Barth Rubio, Robert Haas)
8212     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;99aeb8470">&sect;</ulink>
8213     </para>
8214
8215     <para>
8216     Only some files can be hard linked.  This should not be used if the
8217     backups will be used independently.
8218     </para>
8219     </listitem>
8220
8221<!--
8222Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
82232024-09-27 [8dfd31290] pg_verifybackup: Verify tar-format backups.
8224-->
8225
8226     <listitem>
8227     <para>
8228     Allow <xref linkend="app-pgverifybackup"/> to verify tar-format
8229     backups (Amul Sul)
8230     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8dfd31290">&sect;</ulink>
8231     </para>
8232     </listitem>
8233
8234<!--
8235Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
82362025-03-12 [4ecdd4110] pg_rewind: Add dbname to primary_conninfo when using - -w
8237-->
8238
8239     <listitem>
8240     <para>
8241     If <xref linkend="app-pgrewind"/>'s <option>--source-server</option>
8242     specifies a database name, use it in
8243     <option>--write-recovery-conf</option> output (Masahiko Sawada)
8244     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4ecdd4110">&sect;</ulink>
8245     </para>
8246     </listitem>
8247
8248<!--
8249Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
82502025-02-21 [30666d185] pg_resetwal: Add - -char-signedness option to change the
8251-->
8252
8253     <listitem>
8254     <para>
8255     Add <xref linkend="app-pgresetwal"/> option
8256     <option>--char-signedness</option> to change the default
8257     <type>char</type> signedness (Masahiko Sawada)
8258     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;30666d185">&sect;</ulink>
8259     </para>
8260     </listitem>
8261
8262    </itemizedlist>
8263
8264
