8265    <sect4 id="release-18-pgdump">
8266     <title><link
8267     linkend="app-pgdump"><application>pg_dump</application></link>/<link
8268     linkend="app-pg-dumpall"><application>pg_dumpall</application></link>/<link
8269     linkend="app-pgrestore"><application>pg_restore</application></link></title>
8270
8271     <itemizedlist>
8272
8273<!--
8274Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
82752025-03-25 [bde2fb797] Add pg_dump - -with-{schema|data|statistics} options.
8276Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
82772025-08-02 [a3e8dc143] Simplify options in pg_dump and pg_restore.
8278-->
8279
8280      <listitem>
8281      <para>
8282      Add <xref linkend="app-pgdump"/> option <option>--statistics</option>
8283      (Jeff Davis)
8284      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bde2fb797">&sect;</ulink>
8285      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a3e8dc143">&sect;</ulink>
8286      </para>
8287      </listitem>
8288
8289<!--
8290Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
82912025-03-25 [9c49f0e8c] pg_dump: Add - -sequence-data.
8292Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
82932025-05-07 [acea3fc49] pg_dumpall: Add - -sequence-data.
8294-->
8295
8296      <listitem>
8297      <para>
8298      Add <application>pg_dump</application> and <xref
8299      linkend="app-pg-dumpall"/> option <option>--sequence-data</option> to
8300      dump sequence data that would normally be excluded (Nathan Bossart)
8301      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9c49f0e8c">&sect;</ulink>
8302      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;acea3fc49">&sect;</ulink>
8303      </para>
8304      </listitem>
8305
8306<!--
8307Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
83082025-02-20 [1fd1bd871] Transfer statistics during pg_upgrade.
8309-->
8310
8311      <listitem>
8312      <para>
8313      Add <xref linkend="app-pgdump"/>, <xref linkend="app-pg-dumpall"/>,
8314      and <xref linkend="app-pgrestore"/> options
8315      <option>--statistics-only</option>, <option>--no-statistics</option>,
8316      <option>--no-data</option>, and <option>--no-schema</option>
8317      (Corey Huinker, Jeff Davis)
8318      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1fd1bd871">&sect;</ulink>
8319      </para>
8320      </listitem>
8321
8322<!--
8323Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
83242025-03-16 [cd3c45125] pg_dump, pg_dumpall, pg_restore: Add - -no-policies optio
8325-->
8326
8327      <listitem>
8328      <para>
8329      Add option <option>--no-policies</option> to disable row level
8330      security policy processing in <xref linkend="app-pgdump"/>,
8331      <xref linkend="app-pg-dumpall"/>, <xref linkend="app-pgrestore"/>
8332      (Nikolay Samokhvalov)
8333      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cd3c45125">&sect;</ulink>
8334      </para>
8335
8336      <para>
8337      This is useful for migrating to systems with different policies.
8338      </para>
8339      </listitem>
8340
8341     </itemizedlist>
8342
8343    </sect4>
8344
8345    <sect4 id="release-18-pgupgrade">
8346     <title><xref linkend="pgupgrade"/></title>
8347
8348     <itemizedlist>
8349
8350<!--
8351Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
83522025-02-20 [1fd1bd871] Transfer statistics during pg_upgrade.
8353Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
83542025-03-18 [c9d502eb6] Update guidance for running vacuumdb after pg_upgrade.
8355Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
83562025-04-30 [d5f1b6a75] Further adjust guidance for running vacuumdb after pg_up
8357Author: Jeff Davis <jdavis@postgresql.org>
83582025-02-20 [1fd1bd871] Transfer statistics during pg_upgrade.
8359-->
8360
8361      <listitem>
8362      <para>
8363      Allow <application>pg_upgrade</application> to preserve optimizer
8364      statistics (Corey Huinker, Jeff Davis, Nathan Bossart)
8365      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1fd1bd871">&sect;</ulink>
8366      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c9d502eb6">&sect;</ulink>
8367      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;d5f1b6a75">&sect;</ulink>
8368      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1fd1bd871">&sect;</ulink>
8369      </para>
8370
8371      <para>
8372      Extended statistics are not preserved.  Also add
8373      <application>pg_upgrade</application> option
8374      <option>--no-statistics</option> to disable statistics preservation.
8375      </para>
8376      </listitem>
8377
8378<!--
8379Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
83802024-09-16 [40e2e5e92] Introduce framework for parallelizing various pg_upgrade
8381Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
83822024-09-16 [6d3d2e8e5] pg_upgrade: Parallelize retrieving relation information.
8383Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
83842024-09-16 [7baa36de5] pg_upgrade: Parallelize subscription check.
8385Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
83862024-09-16 [46cad8b31] pg_upgrade: Parallelize retrieving loadable libraries.
8387Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
83882024-09-16 [6ab8f27bc] pg_upgrade: Parallelize retrieving extension updates.
8389Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
83902024-09-16 [bbf83cab9] pg_upgrade: Parallelize data type checks.
8391Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
83922024-09-16 [9db3018cf] pg_upgrade: Parallelize contrib/isn check.
8393Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
83942024-09-16 [c34eabfbb] pg_upgrade: Parallelize postfix operator check.
8395Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
83962024-09-16 [cf2f82a37] pg_upgrade: Parallelize incompatible polymorphics check.
8397Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
83982024-09-16 [f93f5f7b9] pg_upgrade: Parallelize WITH OIDS check.
8399Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
84002024-09-16 [c880cf258] pg_upgrade: Parallelize encoding conversion check.
8401-->
8402
8403      <listitem>
8404      <para>
8405      Allow <application>pg_upgrade</application> to process database
8406      checks in parallel (Nathan Bossart)
8407      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;40e2e5e92">&sect;</ulink>
8408      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6d3d2e8e5">&sect;</ulink>
8409      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7baa36de5">&sect;</ulink>
8410      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;46cad8b31">&sect;</ulink>
8411      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6ab8f27bc">&sect;</ulink>
8412      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;bbf83cab9">&sect;</ulink>
8413      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9db3018cf">&sect;</ulink>
8414      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c34eabfbb">&sect;</ulink>
8415      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cf2f82a37">&sect;</ulink>
8416      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f93f5f7b9">&sect;</ulink>
8417      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c880cf258">&sect;</ulink>
8418      </para>
8419
8420      <para>
8421      This is controlled by the existing <option>--jobs</option> option.
8422      </para>
8423      </listitem>
8424
8425<!--
8426Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
84272025-03-25 [626d7236b] pg_upgrade: Add - -swap for faster file transfer.
8428-->
8429
8430      <listitem>
8431      <para>
8432      Add <application>pg_upgrade</application> option
8433      <option>--swap</option> to swap directories rather than copy, clone,
8434      or link files (Nathan Bossart)
8435      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;626d7236b">&sect;</ulink>
8436      </para>
8437
8438      <para>
8439      This mode is potentially the fastest.
8440      </para>
8441      </listitem>
8442
8443<!--
8444Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
84452025-02-21 [a8238f87f] pg_upgrade: Preserve default char signedness value from
8446Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
84472025-02-21 [1aab68059] pg_upgrade: Add - -set-char-signedness to set the default
8448-->
8449
8450      <listitem>
8451      <para>
8452      Add <application>pg_upgrade</application> option
8453      <option>--set-char-signedness</option> to set the default
8454      <type>char</type> signedness of new cluster (Masahiko Sawada)
8455      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a8238f87f">&sect;</ulink>
8456      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;1aab68059">&sect;</ulink>
8457      </para>
8458
8459      <para>
8460      This is to handle cases where a
8461      pre-<productname>PostgreSQL</productname> 18 cluster's default
8462      <acronym>CPU</acronym> signedness does not match the new cluster.
8463      </para>
8464      </listitem>
8465
8466     </itemizedlist>
8467
8468    </sect4>
8469
8470    <sect4 id="release-18-logicalrep-app">
8471     <title>Logical Replication Applications</title>
8472
8473     <itemizedlist>
8474
8475<!--
8476Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
84772025-03-28 [fb2ea12f4] pg_createsubscriber: Add '- -all' option.
8478-->
8479
8480      <listitem>
8481      <para>
8482      Add <xref linkend="app-pgcreatesubscriber"/> option
8483      <option>--all</option> to create logical replicas for all databases
8484      (Shubham Khanna)
8485      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;fb2ea12f4">&sect;</ulink>
8486      </para>
8487      </listitem>
8488
8489<!--
8490Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
84912025-03-20 [e5aeed4b8] pg_createsubscriber: Add -R publications option.
8492Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
8493Branch: master Release: REL_18_BR [60dda7bbc] 2025-06-25 10:50:43 +0200
8494-->
8495
8496      <listitem>
8497      <para>
8498      Add <application>pg_createsubscriber</application> option
8499      <option>--clean</option> to remove publications (Shubham Khanna)
8500      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e5aeed4b8">&sect;</ulink>
8501      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;60dda7bbc">&sect;</ulink>
8502      </para>
8503      </listitem>
8504
8505<!--
8506Author: Amit Kapila <akapila@postgresql.org>
85072025-02-26 [e117cfb2f] Add two-phase option in pg_createsubscriber.
8508-->
8509
8510      <listitem>
8511      <para>
8512      Add <application>pg_createsubscriber</application> option
8513      <option>--enable-two-phase</option> to enable prepared transactions
8514      (Shubham Khanna)
8515      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e117cfb2f">&sect;</ulink>
8516      </para>
8517      </listitem>
8518
8519<!--
8520Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
85212025-04-04 [cf2655a90] pg_recvlogical: Add - -failover option.
8522-->
8523
8524      <listitem>
8525      <para>
8526      Add <xref linkend="app-pgrecvlogical"/> option
8527      <option>--enable-failover</option> to specify failover slots (Hayato Kuroda)
8528      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cf2655a90">&sect;</ulink>
8529      </para>
8530
8531      <para>
8532      Also add option <option>--enable-two-phase</option> as a synonym
8533      for <option>--two-phase</option>, and deprecate the latter.
8534      </para>
8535      </listitem>
8536
8537<!--
8538Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
85392025-03-25 [c68100aa4] Allow pg_recvlogical - -drop-slot to work without - -dbnam
8540-->
8541
8542      <listitem>
8543      <para>
8544      Allow <application>pg_recvlogical</application>
8545      <option>--drop-slot</option> to work without
8546      <option>--dbname</option> (Hayato Kuroda)
8547      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c68100aa4">&sect;</ulink>
8548      </para>
8549      </listitem>
8550
8551     </itemizedlist>
8552
8553    </sect4>
8554
8555   </sect3>
8556
8557   <sect3 id="release-18-source-code">
8558    <title>Source Code</title>
8559
8560    <itemizedlist>
8561
8562<!--
8563Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
85642024-07-05 [4b211003e] Support loading of injection points
8565Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
85662024-07-18 [a0a5869a8] Add INJECTION_POINT_CACHED() to run injection points dir
8567-->
8568
8569     <listitem>
8570     <para>
8571     Separate the loading and running of <link
8572     linkend="xfunc-addin-injection-points">injection points</link>
8573     (Michael Paquier, Heikki Linnakangas)
8574     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4b211003e">&sect;</ulink>
8575     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a0a5869a8">&sect;</ulink>
8576     </para>
8577
8578     <para>
8579     Injection points can now be created, but not run, via <link
8580     linkend="xfunc-addin-injection-points"><function>INJECTION_POINT_LOAD()</function></link>,
8581     and such injection points can be run via <link
8582     linkend="xfunc-addin-injection-points"><function>INJECTION_POINT_CACHED()</function></link>.
8583     </para>
8584     </listitem>
8585
8586<!--
8587Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
85882025-05-10 [371f2db8b] Add support for runtime arguments in injection points
8589-->
8590
8591     <listitem>
8592     <para>
8593     Support runtime arguments in injection points (Michael Paquier)
8594     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;371f2db8b">&sect;</ulink>
8595     </para>
8596     </listitem>
8597
8598<!--
8599Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
86002024-07-26 [20e0e7da9] Add test for early backend startup errors
8601-->
8602
8603     <listitem>
8604     <para>
8605     Allow inline injection point test code with <link
8606     linkend="xfunc-addin-injection-points"><function>IS_INJECTION_POINT_ATTACHED()</function></link>
8607     (Heikki Linnakangas)
8608     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;20e0e7da9">&sect;</ulink>
8609     </para>
8610     </listitem>
8611
8612<!--
8613Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
86142024-08-05 [ca6fde922] Optimize JSON escaping using SIMD
8615-->
8616
8617     <listitem>
8618     <para>
8619     Improve the performance of processing long <link
8620     linkend="datatype-json"><type>JSON</type></link> strings using
8621     <acronym>SIMD</acronym> (Single Instruction Multiple Data) (David
8622     Rowley)
8623     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ca6fde922">&sect;</ulink>
8624     </para>
8625     </listitem>
8626
8627<!--
8628Author: John Naylor <john.naylor@postgresql.org>
86292025-04-06 [3c6e8c123] Compute CRC32C using AVX-512 instructions where availabl
8630-->
8631
8632     <listitem>
8633     <para>
8634     Speed up CRC32C calculations using x86 <acronym>AVX</acronym>-512
8635     instructions (Raghuveer Devulapalli, Paul Amonson)
8636     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3c6e8c123">&sect;</ulink>
8637     </para>
8638     </listitem>
8639
8640<!--
8641Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
86422025-03-28 [6be53c276] Optimize popcount functions with ARM Neon intrinsics.
8643Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
86442025-03-28 [519338ace] Optimize popcount functions with ARM SVE intrinsics.
8645-->
8646
8647     <listitem>
8648     <para>
8649     Add <acronym>ARM</acronym> Neon and <acronym>SVE CPU</acronym>
8650     intrinsics for popcount (integer bit counting) (Chiranmoy
8651     Bhattacharya, Devanga Susmitha, Rama Malladi)
8652     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6be53c276">&sect;</ulink>
8653     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;519338ace">&sect;</ulink>
8654     </para>
8655     </listitem>
8656
8657<!--
8658Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
86592024-07-09 [ca481d3c9] Optimise numeric multiplication for short inputs.
8660Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
86612024-08-15 [c4e44224c] Extend mul_var_short() to 5 and 6-digit inputs.
8662Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
86632024-08-15 [8dc28d7eb] Optimise numeric multiplication using base-NBASE^2 arith
8664Author: Dean Rasheed <dean.a.rasheed@gmail.com>
86652024-10-04 [9428c001f] Speed up numeric division by always using the "fast" alg
8666-->
8667
8668     <listitem>
8669     <para>
8670     Improve the speed of numeric multiplication and division (Joel
8671     Jacobson, Dean Rasheed)
8672     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ca481d3c9">&sect;</ulink>
8673     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c4e44224c">&sect;</ulink>
8674     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8dc28d7eb">&sect;</ulink>
8675     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9428c001f">&sect;</ulink>
8676     </para>
8677     </listitem>
8678
8679<!--
8680Author: Tomas Vondra <tomas.vondra@postgresql.org>
86812025-04-07 [65c298f61] Add support for basic NUMA awareness
8682Author: Tomas Vondra <tomas.vondra@postgresql.org>
86832025-04-07 [8cc139bec] Introduce pg_shmem_allocations_numa view
8684Author: Tomas Vondra <tomas.vondra@postgresql.org>
86852025-04-07 [ba2a3c230] Add pg_buffercache_numa view with NUMA node info
8686-->
8687
8688     <listitem>
8689     <para>
8690     Add configure option <link
8691     linkend="configure-option-with-libnuma"><option>--with-libnuma</option></link>
8692     to enable <acronym>NUMA</acronym> awareness (Jakub Wartak, Bertrand
8693     Drouvot)
8694     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;65c298f61">&sect;</ulink>
8695     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8cc139bec">&sect;</ulink>
8696     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ba2a3c230">&sect;</ulink>
8697     </para>
8698
8699     <para>
8700     The function <link
8701     linkend="functions-info-session-table"><function>pg_numa_available()</function></link>
8702     reports on <acronym>NUMA</acronym> awareness, and system views <link
8703     linkend="view-pg-shmem-allocations-numa"><structname>pg_shmem_allocations_numa</structname></link>
8704     and <link
8705     linkend="pgbuffercache-pg-buffercache-numa"><structname>pg_buffercache_numa</structname></link>
8706     which report on shared memory distribution across
8707     <acronym>NUMA</acronym> nodes.
8708     </para>
8709     </listitem>
8710
8711<!--
8712Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
87132024-09-18 [b52c4fc3c] Add TOAST table to pg_index.
8714-->
8715
8716     <listitem>
8717     <para>
8718     Add <link
8719     linkend="storage-toast"><acronym>TOAST</acronym></link> table to <link
8720     linkend="catalog-pg-index"><structname>pg_index</structname></link>
8721     to allow for very large expression indexes (Nathan Bossart)
8722     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;b52c4fc3c">&sect;</ulink>
8723     </para>
8724     </listitem>
8725
8726<!--
8727Author: David Rowley <drowley@postgresql.org>
87282024-12-20 [02a8d0c45] Remove pg_attribute.attcacheoff column
8729-->
8730
8731     <listitem>
8732     <para>
8733     Remove column <link
8734     linkend="catalog-pg-attribute"><structname>pg_attribute</structname></link>.<structfield>attcacheoff</structfield>
8735     (David Rowley)
8736     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;02a8d0c45">&sect;</ulink>
8737     </para>
8738     </listitem>
8739
8740<!--
8741Author: Melanie Plageman <melanieplageman@gmail.com>
87422025-03-03 [99f8f3fbb] Add relallfrozen to pg_class
8743-->
8744
8745     <listitem>
8746     <para>
8747     Add column <link
8748     linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>relallfrozen</structfield>
8749     (Melanie Plageman)
8750     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;99f8f3fbb">&sect;</ulink>
8751     </para>
8752     </listitem>
8753
8754<!--
8755Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
87562024-09-10 [56fead44d] Add amgettreeheight index AM API routine
8757Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
87582025-03-07 [af4002b38] Rename amcancrosscompare
8759-->
8760
8761     <listitem>
8762     <para>
8763     Add <link
8764     linkend="indexam"><structfield>amgettreeheight</structfield></link>,
8765     <literal>amconsistentequality</literal>, and
8766     <literal>amconsistentordering</literal> to the index access method
8767     <acronym>API</acronym> (Mark Dilger)
8768     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;56fead44d">&sect;</ulink>
8769     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;af4002b38">&sect;</ulink>
8770     </para>
8771     </listitem>
8772
8773<!--
8774Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
87752024-09-17 [7406ab623] Add stratnum GiST support function
8776-->
8777
8778     <listitem>
8779     <para>
8780     Add GiST support function <link
8781     linkend="gist-extensibility"><function>stratnum()</function></link>
8782     (Paul A. Jungwirth)
8783     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7406ab623">&sect;</ulink>
8784     </para>
8785     </listitem>
8786
8787<!--
8788Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
87892025-02-21 [44fe30fda] Add default_char_signedness field to ControlFileData.
8790-->
8791
8792     <listitem>
8793     <para>
8794     Record the default <acronym>CPU</acronym> signedness of
8795     <literal>char</literal> in <xref linkend="app-pgcontroldata"/>
8796     (Masahiko Sawada)
8797     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;44fe30fda">&sect;</ulink>
8798     </para>
8799     </listitem>
8800
8801<!--
8802Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
88032025-03-12 [72a3d0462] Prepare for Python "Limited API" in PL/Python
8804Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
88052025-03-14 [0793ab810] Activate Python "Limited API" in PL/Python
8806-->
8807
8808     <listitem>
8809     <para>
8810     Add support for Python "Limited <acronym>API</acronym>" in <link
8811     linkend="plpython">PL/Python</link> (Peter Eisentraut)
8812     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;72a3d0462">&sect;</ulink>
8813     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;0793ab810">&sect;</ulink>
8814     </para>
8815
8816     <para>
8817     This helps prevent problems caused by
8818     <application>Python</application> 3.x version mismatches.
8819     </para>
8820     </listitem>
8821
8822<!--
8823Author: Jacob Champion <jchampion@postgresql.org>
88242025-04-29 [45363fca6] Bump the minimum supported Python version to 3.6.8
8825-->
8826
8827     <listitem>
8828     <para>
8829     Change the minimum supported <application>Python</application>
8830     version to 3.6.8 (Jacob Champion)
8831     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;45363fca6">&sect;</ulink>
8832     </para>
8833     </listitem>
8834
8835<!--
8836Author: Daniel Gustafsson <dgustafsson@postgresql.org>
88372024-09-02 [a70e01d43] Remove support for OpenSSL older than 1.1.0
8838Author: Daniel Gustafsson <dgustafsson@postgresql.org>
88392024-10-24 [6c66b7443] Raise the minimum supported OpenSSL version to 1.1.1
8840-->
8841
8842     <listitem>
8843     <para>
8844     Remove support for <application>OpenSSL</application> versions older
8845     than 1.1.1 (Daniel Gustafsson)
8846     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a70e01d43">&sect;</ulink>
8847     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6c66b7443">&sect;</ulink>
8848     </para>
8849     </listitem>
8850
8851<!--
8852Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
88532024-10-01 [972c2cd28] jit: Require at least LLVM 14, if enabled.
8854-->
8855
8856     <listitem>
8857     <para>
8858     If <application>LLVM</application> is enabled, require version 14
8859     or later (Thomas Munro)
8860     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;972c2cd28">&sect;</ulink>
8861     </para>
8862     </listitem>
8863
8864<!--
8865Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
88662025-03-26 [9324c8c58] Introduce PG_MODULE_MAGIC_EXT macro.
8867-->
8868
8869     <listitem>
8870     <para>
8871     Add macro <link
8872     linkend="functions-info"><literal>PG_MODULE_MAGIC_EXT</literal></link>
8873     to allow extensions to report their name and version (Andrei Lepikhov)
8874     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9324c8c58">&sect;</ulink>
8875     </para>
8876
8877     <para>
8878     This information can be access via the new function <link
8879     linkend="functions-info-session-table"><function>pg_get_loaded_modules()</function></link>.
8880     </para>
8881     </listitem>
8882
8883<!--
8884Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
88852024-09-09 [218527d01] Don't bother checking the result of SPI_connect[_ext] an
8886-->
8887
8888     <listitem>
8889     <para>
8890     Document that <link
8891     linkend="spi-spi-connect"><function>SPI_connect()</function></link>/<link
8892     linkend="spi-spi-connect"><function>SPI_connect_ext()</function></link>
8893     always returns success (<literal>SPI_OK_CONNECT</literal>) (Stepan
8894     Neretin)
8895     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;218527d01">&sect;</ulink>
8896     </para>
8897
8898     <para>
8899     Errors are always reported via <function>ereport()</function>.
8900     </para>
8901     </listitem>
8902
8903<!--
8904Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
89052024-07-31 [e54a42ac9] Add API and ABI stability guidance to the C language doc
8906-->
8907
8908     <listitem>
8909     <para>
8910     Add <link linkend="xfunc-api-abi-stability-guidance">documentation
8911     section</link> about <acronym>API</acronym> and <acronym>ABI</acronym>
8912     compatibility (David Wheeler, Peter Eisentraut)
8913     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e54a42ac9">&sect;</ulink>
8914     </para>
8915     </listitem>
8916
8917<!--
8918Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
89192025-01-27 [5afaba629] doc: Meson is not experimental on Windows
8920-->
8921
8922     <listitem>
8923     <para>
8924     Remove the experimental designation of
8925     <application>Meson</application> builds on <systemitem
8926     class="osname">Windows</systemitem> (Aleksander Alekseev)
8927     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;5afaba629">&sect;</ulink>
8928     </para>
8929     </listitem>
8930
8931<!--
8932Author: Thomas Munro <tmunro@postgresql.org>
89332024-07-30 [e25626677] Remove - -disable-spinlocks.
8934Author: Thomas Munro <tmunro@postgresql.org>
89352024-07-30 [813852613] Remove - -disable-atomics, require 32 bit atomics.
8936-->
8937
8938     <listitem>
8939     <para>
8940     Remove configure options <option>--disable-spinlocks</option> and
8941     <option>--disable-atomics</option> (Thomas Munro)
8942     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e25626677">&sect;</ulink>
8943     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;813852613">&sect;</ulink>
8944     </para>
8945
8946     <para>
8947     Thirty-two-bit atomic operations are now required.
8948     </para>
8949     </listitem>
8950
8951<!--
8952Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
89532024-07-01 [edadeb071] Remove support for HPPA (a/k/a PA-RISC) architecture.
8954-->
8955
8956     <listitem>
8957     <para>
8958     Remove support for the
8959     <acronym>HPPA</acronym>/<acronym>PA-RISC</acronym> architecture
8960     (Tom Lane)
8961     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;edadeb071">&sect;</ulink>
8962     </para>
8963     </listitem>
8964
8965    </itemizedlist>
8966
8967   </sect3>
8968
8969   <sect3 id="release-18-modules">
8970    <title>Additional Modules</title>
8971
8972    <itemizedlist>
8973
8974<!--
8975Author: Masahiko Sawada <msawada@postgresql.org>
89762024-10-14 [7cdfeee32] Add contrib/pg_logicalinspect.
8977-->
8978
8979     <listitem>
8980     <para>
8981     Add extension <xref linkend="pglogicalinspect"/> to inspect logical
8982     snapshots (Bertrand Drouvot)
8983     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7cdfeee32">&sect;</ulink>
8984     </para>
8985     </listitem>
8986
8987<!--
8988Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
89892025-03-26 [8d5ceb113] pg_overexplain: Additional EXPLAIN options for debugging
8990-->
8991
8992     <listitem>
8993     <para>
8994     Add extension <xref linkend="pgoverexplain"/> which adds debug details
8995     to <link linkend="sql-explain"><command>EXPLAIN</command></link>
8996     output (Robert Haas)
8997     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;8d5ceb113">&sect;</ulink>
8998     </para>
8999     </listitem>
9000
9001<!--
9002Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
90032024-07-26 [c297a47c5] postgres_fdw: Add "used_in_xact" column to postgres_fdw_
9004Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
90052024-07-26 [857df3cef] postgres_fdw: Add connection status check to postgres_fd
9006Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
90072024-09-18 [4f08ab554] postgres_fdw: Extend postgres_fdw_get_connections to ret
9008Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
90092025-03-03 [fe186bda7] postgres_fdw: Extend postgres_fdw_get_connections to ret
9010-->
9011
9012     <listitem>
9013     <para>
9014     Add output columns to <link
9015     linkend="postgres-fdw-functions"><function>postgres_fdw_get_connections()</function></link>
9016     (Hayato Kuroda, Sagar Dilip Shedge)
9017     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c297a47c5">&sect;</ulink>
9018     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;857df3cef">&sect;</ulink>
9019     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4f08ab554">&sect;</ulink>
9020     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;fe186bda7">&sect;</ulink>
9021     </para>
9022
9023     <para>
9024     New output column <structfield>used_in_xact</structfield> indicates
9025     if the foreign data wrapper is being used by a current transaction,
9026     <structfield>closed</structfield> indicates if it is closed,
9027     <structfield>user_name</structfield> indicates the user name, and
9028     <structfield>remote_backend_pid</structfield> indicates the remote
9029     backend process identifier.
9030     </para>
9031     </listitem>
9032
9033<!--
9034Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
90352025-01-15 [761c79508] postgres_fdw: SCRAM authentication pass-through
9036-->
9037
9038     <listitem>
9039     <para>
9040     Allow <link linkend="auth-password"><acronym>SCRAM</acronym></link>
9041     authentication from the client to be passed to <xref
9042     linkend="postgres-fdw"/> servers (Matheus Alcantara, Peter Eisentraut)
9043     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;761c79508">&sect;</ulink>
9044     </para>
9045
9046     <para>
9047     This avoids storing <application>postgres_fdw</application>
9048     authentication information in the database, and is
9049     enabled with the <application>postgres_fdw</application> <link
9050     linkend="postgres-fdw-option-use-scram-passthrough"><literal>use_scram_passthrough</literal></link>
9051     connection option.  libpq uses new connection parameters
9052     <xref linkend="libpq-connect-scram-client-key"/> and <xref
9053     linkend="libpq-connect-scram-server-key"/>.
9054     </para>
9055     </listitem>
9056
9057<!--
9058Author: Peter Eisentraut <peter@eisentraut.org>
90592025-03-26 [3642df265] dblink: SCRAM authentication pass-through
9060-->
9061
9062     <listitem>
9063     <para>
9064     Allow <acronym>SCRAM</acronym> authentication from the client to be
9065     passed to <xref linkend="dblink"/> servers (Matheus Alcantara)
9066     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;3642df265">&sect;</ulink>
9067     </para>
9068     </listitem>
9069
9070<!--
9071Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
90722024-10-03 [a1c4c8a9e] file_fdw: Add on_error and log_verbosity options to file
9073-->
9074
9075     <listitem>
9076     <para>
9077     Add <literal>on_error</literal> and <literal>log_verbosity</literal>
9078     options to <xref linkend="file-fdw"/> (Atsushi Torikoshi)
9079     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;a1c4c8a9e">&sect;</ulink>
9080     </para>
9081
9082     <para>
9083     These control how <application>file_fdw</application> handles and
9084     reports invalid file rows.
9085     </para>
9086     </listitem>
9087
9088<!--
9089Author: Fujii Masao <fujii@postgresql.org>
90902024-11-20 [6c8f67032] file_fdw: Add REJECT_LIMIT option to file_fdw.
9091-->
9092
9093     <listitem>
9094     <para>
9095     Add <literal>reject_limit</literal> to control the number of
9096     invalid rows <application>file_fdw</application> can ignore (Atsushi
9097     Torikoshi)
9098     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6c8f67032">&sect;</ulink>
9099     </para>
9100
9101     <para>
9102     This is active when <literal>ON_ERROR = 'ignore'</literal>.
9103     </para>
9104     </listitem>
9105
9106<!--
9107Author: Nathan Bossart <nathan@postgresql.org>
91082025-01-07 [f7e1b3828] Add passwordcheck.min_password_length.
9109-->
9110
9111     <listitem>
9112     <para>
9113     Add configurable variable <varname>min_password_length</varname> to
9114     <xref linkend="passwordcheck"/> (Emanuele Musella, Maurizio Boriani)
9115     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;f7e1b3828">&sect;</ulink>
9116     </para>
9117
9118     <para>
9119     This controls the minimum password length.
9120     </para>
9121     </listitem>
9122
9123<!--
9124Author: Tatsuo Ishii <ishii@postgresql.org>
91252024-10-11 [cae0f3c40] pgbench: Improve result outputs related to failed transa
9126-->
9127
9128     <listitem>
9129     <para>
9130     Have <xref linkend="pgbench"/> report the number of failed, retried,
9131     or skipped transactions in per-script reports (Yugo Nagata)
9132     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cae0f3c40">&sect;</ulink>
9133     </para>
9134     </listitem>
9135
9136<!--
9137Author: Tom Lane <tgl@sss.pgh.pa.us>
91382025-03-16 [448904423] contrib/isn: Make weak mode a GUC setting, and fix relat
9139-->
9140
9141     <listitem>
9142     <para>
9143     Add <xref linkend="isn"/> server variable <literal>weak</literal>
9144     to control invalid check digit acceptance (Viktor Holmberg)
9145     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;448904423">&sect;</ulink>
9146     </para>
9147
9148     <para>
9149     This was previously only controlled by function <link
9150     linkend="isn-functions"><function>isn_weak()</function></link>.
9151     </para>
9152     </listitem>
9153
9154<!--
9155Author: Heikki Linnakangas <heikki.linnakangas@iki.fi>
91562025-04-03 [e4309f73f] Add support for sorted gist index builds to btree_gist
9157-->
9158
9159     <listitem>
9160     <para>
9161     Allow values to be sorted to speed <xref linkend="btree-gist"/>
9162     index builds (Bernd Helmle, Andrey Borodin)
9163     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;e4309f73f">&sect;</ulink>
9164     </para>
9165     </listitem>
9166
9167<!--
9168Author: Tomas Vondra <tomas.vondra@postgresql.org>
91692025-03-29 [14ffaece0] amcheck: Add gin_index_check() to verify GIN index
9170-->
9171
9172     <listitem>
9173     <para>
9174     Add <xref linkend="amcheck"/> check function <link
9175     linkend="amcheck-functions"><function>gin_index_check()</function></link>
9176     to verify <literal>GIN</literal> indexes (Grigory Kryachko, Heikki
9177     Linnakangas, Andrey Borodin)
9178     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;14ffaece0">&sect;</ulink>
9179     </para>
9180     </listitem>
9181
9182<!--
9183Author: Andres Freund <andres@anarazel.de>
91842025-04-08 [dcf7e1697] Add pg_buffercache_evict_{relation,all} functions
9185-->
9186
9187     <listitem>
9188     <para>
9189     Add functions <link
9190     linkend="pgbuffercache-pg-buffercache-evict-relation"><function>pg_buffercache_evict_relation()</function></link>
9191     and <link
9192     linkend="pgbuffercache-pg-buffercache-evict-all"><function>pg_buffercache_evict_all()</function></link>
9193     to evict unpinned shared buffers (Nazir Bilal Yavuz)
9194     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;dcf7e1697">&sect;</ulink>
9195     </para>
9196
9197     <para>
9198     The existing function <link
9199     linkend="pgbuffercache-pg-buffercache-evict"><function>pg_buffercache_evict()</function></link>
9200     now returns the buffer flush status.
9201     </para>
9202     </listitem>
9203
9204<!--
9205Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
92062025-03-18 [c65bc2e1d] Make it possible for loadable modules to add EXPLAIN opt
9207Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
92082025-03-18 [4fd02bf7c] Add some new hooks so extensions can add details to EXPL
9209Author: Robert Haas <rhaas@postgresql.org>
92102025-03-20 [50ba65e73] Add an additional hook for EXPLAIN option validation.
9211-->
9212
9213     <listitem>
9214     <para>
9215     Allow extensions to install custom <xref linkend="sql-explain"/>
9216     options (Robert Haas, Sami Imseih)
9217     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;c65bc2e1d">&sect;</ulink>
9218     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;4fd02bf7c">&sect;</ulink>
9219     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;50ba65e73">&sect;</ulink>
9220     </para>
9221     </listitem>
9222
9223<!--
9224Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
92252024-08-04 [7949d9594] Introduce pluggable APIs for Cumulative Statistics
9226Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
92272024-08-05 [2eff9e678] Add helper routines to retrieve data for custom fixed-nu
9228-->
9229
9230     <listitem>
9231     <para>
9232     Allow extensions to use the server's cumulative statistics
9233     <acronym>API</acronym> (Michael Paquier)
9234     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;7949d9594">&sect;</ulink>
9235     <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;2eff9e678">&sect;</ulink>
9236     </para>
9237     </listitem>
9238
9239    </itemizedlist>
9240
9241    <sect4 id="release-18-pgstatstatements">
9242     <title><xref linkend="pgstatstatements"/></title>
9243
9244     <itemizedlist>
9245
9246<!--
9247Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
92482024-10-28 [6b652e6ce] Set query ID for inner queries of CREATE TABLE AS and DE
9249-->
9250
9251      <listitem>
9252      <para>
9253      Allow the queries of <xref linkend="sql-createtableas"/>
9254      and <xref linkend="sql-declare"/> to be tracked by
9255      <application>pg_stat_statements</application> (Anthonin Bonnefoy)
9256      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;6b652e6ce">&sect;</ulink>
9257      </para>
9258
9259      <para>
9260      They are also now assigned query ids.
9261      </para>
9262      </listitem>
9263
9264<!--
9265Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
92662024-09-30 [dc6851596] Show values of SET statements as constants in pg_stat_st
9267-->
9268
9269      <listitem>
9270      <para>
9271      Allow the parameterization of <xref linkend="sql-set"/> values in
9272      <application>pg_stat_statements</application> (Greg Sabino Mullane,
9273      Michael Paquier)
9274      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;dc6851596">&sect;</ulink>
9275      </para>
9276
9277      <para>
9278      This reduces the bloat caused by <command>SET</command> statements
9279      with differing constants.
9280      </para>
9281      </listitem>
9282
9283<!--
9284Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
92852024-10-09 [cf54a2c00] pg_stat_statements: Add columns to track parallel worker
9286-->
9287
9288      <listitem>
9289      <para>
9290      Add <link
9291      linkend="pgstatstatements-pg-stat-statements"><structname>pg_stat_statements</structname></link>
9292      columns to report parallel activity (Guillaume Lelarge)
9293      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;cf54a2c00">&sect;</ulink>
9294      </para>
9295
9296      <para>
9297      The new columns are
9298      <structfield>parallel_workers_to_launch</structfield> and
9299      <structfield>parallel_workers_launched</structfield>.
9300      </para>
9301      </listitem>
9302
9303<!--
9304Author: Michael Paquier <michael@paquier.xyz>
93052025-02-17 [ce5bcc4a9] pg_stat_statements: Add wal_buffers_full
9306-->
9307
9308      <listitem>
9309      <para>
9310      Add
9311      <structname>pg_stat_statements</structname>.<structfield>wal_buffers_full</structfield>
9312      to report full <acronym>WAL</acronym> buffers (Bertrand Drouvot)
9313      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;ce5bcc4a9">&sect;</ulink>
9314      </para>
9315      </listitem>
9316
9317     </itemizedlist>
9318
9319    </sect4>
9320
9321    <sect4 id="release-18-pgcrypto">
9322     <title><xref linkend="pgcrypto"/></title>
9323
9324     <itemizedlist>
9325
9326<!--
9327Author: Álvaro Herrera <alvherre@alvh.no-ip.org>
93282025-04-05 [749a9e20c] Add modern SHA-2 based password hashes to pgcrypto.
9329-->
9330
9331      <listitem>
9332      <para>
9333      Add <application>pgcrypto</application> algorithms <link
9334      linkend="pgcrypto-crypt-algorithms"><literal>sha256crypt</literal></link>
9335      and <link
9336      linkend="pgcrypto-crypt-algorithms"><literal>sha512crypt</literal></link>
9337      (Bernd Helmle)
9338      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;749a9e20c">&sect;</ulink>
9339      </para>
9340      </listitem>
9341
9342<!--
9343Author: Daniel Gustafsson <dgustafsson@postgresql.org>
93442025-02-14 [9ad1b3d01] pgcrypto: Add support for CFB mode in AES encryption
9345-->
9346
9347      <listitem>
9348      <para>
9349      Add <link
9350      linkend="pgcrypto-raw-enc-funcs"><acronym>CFB</acronym></link> mode
9351      to <application>pgcrypto</application> encryption and decryption
9352      (Umar Hayat)
9353      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;9ad1b3d01">&sect;</ulink>
9354      </para>
9355      </listitem>
9356
9357<!--
9358Author: Daniel Gustafsson <dgustafsson@postgresql.org>
93592025-01-24 [924d89a35] pgcrypto: Add function to check FIPS mode
9360-->
9361
9362      <listitem>
9363      <para>
9364      Add function <link
9365      linkend="pgcrypto-openssl-support-funcs"><function>fips_mode()</function></link>
9366      to report the server's <acronym>FIPS</acronym> mode (Daniel
9367      Gustafsson)
9368      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;924d89a35">&sect;</ulink>
9369      </para>
9370      </listitem>
9371
9372<!--
9373Author: Daniel Gustafsson <dgustafsson@postgresql.org>
93742025-01-24 [035f99cbe] pgcrypto: Make it possible to disable built-in crypto
9375-->
9376
9377      <listitem>
9378      <para>
9379      Add <application>pgcrypto</application> server variable <link
9380      linkend="pgcrypto-configuration-parameters-builtin_crypto_enabled"><varname>builtin_crypto_enabled</varname></link>
9381      to allow disabling builtin non-<acronym>FIPS</acronym> mode
9382      cryptographic functions (Daniel Gustafsson, Joe Conway)
9383      <ulink url="&commit_baseurl;035f99cbe">&sect;</ulink>
9384      </para>
9385
9386      <para>
9387      This is useful for guaranteeing <acronym>FIPS</acronym> mode behavior.
9388      </para>
9389      </listitem>
9390
9391     </itemizedlist>
9392    </sect4>
9393
9394   </sect3>
9395
9396  </sect2>
9397
